下旬：萩祭り * 10月10日：秋季例祭 * 10月最終日曜：[[七五三]] == アクセス == * [[京都市営バス|京都市バス]]、[[京都バス]] 「府立医大病院前」停留所下車徒歩1分 == 関連図書 == * {{Cite book |和書|author=二六興信所 編纂 |author2=山田米吉 編 |title=勤王事蹟別格官幣社精史 |publisher=二六興信所 |year=1935 |pages=74-77 |chapter=梨木神社 |id={{NDLJP|1112175/48}}}} * {{Cite book |和書|author=安津素彦 |author2=梅田義彦 編集兼監修者 |title=神道辞典 |publisher=[[神社新報|神社新報社]] |year=1968 |page=43 |id={{NDLJP|2997629}}}} * {{Cite book |和書|author=白井永二 |author-link=白井永二 |author2=土岐昌訓 編 |title=神社辞典 |publisher=[[東京堂出版]] |year=1979 |page=256 |id={{NDLJP|12266197}}}} == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{Notelist2}} === 出典 === {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite book |和書 |editor=山階会 |title=山階宮三代 |volume=上 |publisher=山階会 |year=1982 |id={{NDLJP|12260162}} |ref=harv}} == 関連項目 == * [[梨木神社爆破事件]] == 外部リンク == {{commonscat|Nashinoki-jinja}} {{osm box|w|638453452}} * {{Official website}} * {{Wayback|date=20100402162411|url=http://www.kyoto-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/02/014/index.html|title=梨木神社}}（京都府神社庁・神社本庁からの脱退によりリンク切れ） * {{京都観光Navi|7|435}} * {{TripAdvisor|access-date=2021-3-24}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:なしのきしんしや}} [[Category:京都市の神社]] [[Category:別格官幣社]] [[Category:上京区の歴史]] [[Category:三条家|社なしのき]] [[Category:上京区の建築物]] [[Category:京都府の景観重要建造物]] [[Category:三条実美]] [[Category:単立神社]]

revision.comment: /* 歴史 */ 「書誌情報 ： 著者 京都府 [編] 出版者 京都府」に基づき、「author=京都府」→「editor=京都府」に訂正、「publisher=京都府」を追記。
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称=梨木神社 |画像=[[画像:Nashinoki Shrine Haiden 3.jpg|250px]]
拝殿 |所在地=[[京都府]][[京都市]][[上京区]][[寺町通]][[広小路通 (京都市)|広小路]]上ル染殿町680 |位置={{Coord|35|1|30|N|135|46|2.5|E|region:JP-26:type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{Ublist| [[三条実万|三條實萬]] | [[三条実美|三條實美]] }} |社格=旧[[別格官幣社]] |創建=[[1885年]]（[[明治]]18年） |別名=萩の宮 |例祭=[[4月18日]]・[[10月10日]] |地図={{Location map|Japan Kyoto city|width=200|caption=}}{{Infobox mapframe|zoom=14|marker=religious-shinto|frame-width=200|point=none|stroke-width=2}} }} '''梨木神社'''（なしのきじんじゃ）は、[[京都市]][[上京区]]染殿町にある[[神社]]。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[別格官幣社]]。[[明治維新]]に大きく貢献した[[三条実万|三條實萬]]・[[三条実美|三條實美]]父子を祭神とする。また境内には約500株の[[ハギ]]が植えられており、別名'''萩の宮'''とも呼ばれており、9月中旬から下旬には'''萩祭り'''がおこなわれる。 == 祭神 == * 主祭神 - [[三条実万|三條實萬]]、[[三条実美|三條實美]] == 歴史 == [[久邇宮朝彦親王]]の[[令旨]]により[[三条家]]の邸宅跡に[[三条実万|三條實萬]]を祀るため社殿が造営される。[[1885年]]（[[明治]]18年）10月10日、社号は地名から梨木神社とされ、さらに[[別格官幣社]]の列格を受けて創建される。その後[[1915年]]（[[大正]]4年）、[[大正天皇]]即位を記念して、子の[[三条実美|三條實美]]を合祀した。[[平安時代]]は[[藤原良房]]の娘[[藤原明子 (染殿后)|染殿后]]の里御所・染殿第であった。 [[1948年]]（[[昭和]]23年）に[[神社本庁]]の[[別表神社]]に加列されている。 1977年に反君主制主義者によって爆破された（[[梨木神社爆破事件]]）{{Cite news |和書 |title= |newspaper=[[京都新聞]] |date=1977-01-03 |edition=朝刊 |page=}}{{Cite news |和書 |title= |newspaper=京都新聞 |date=1977-01-13 |edition=朝刊 |page=}}。 社殿の修復等の資金集めに苦慮していた[[2013年]]（[[平成]]25年）、境内の参道を含む土地を[[マンション]]開発業者に60年の[[定期借地権]]で貸し、その賃貸料を社殿の修復費用に充てることとしたが、その計画が神社本庁の承認を得られなかったことから神社本庁から離脱して独立し、[[単立]]神社となった。これにより、別表神社の列から当社は外された。 神社本庁の別表神社が神社本庁から離脱したのは[[気多大社]]以来のこととなる。
皇室の参拝歴
{| class="wikitable" style="font-size:small;" !年月日!!参拝者!!備考 |- ||[[1885年]]（明治18年）10月6日{{sfn|山階会|1982|p=510}}||[[山階宮晃親王]]||神霊鎮座祭 |- ||1885年（明治18年）11月15日{{sfn|山階会|1982|p=512}}||山階宮晃親王||[[国分貞胤]]宅の茶事の帰路 |- ||[[1886年]]（明治19年）4月15日{{sfn|山階会|1982|p=520}}||山階宮晃親王|| |- ||1887年（明治20年）2月2日{{sfn|山階会|1982|p=534}}||山階宮晃親王||[[京都御所]]参内の途次 |- ||[[1887年]]（明治20年）1月23日{{Cite book |和書 |editor=高松宮家 |title=熾仁親王日記 |volume=巻4 |publisher=高松宮家 |year=1936 |page=461 |id={{NDLJP|1221430}}}}||[[有栖川宮熾仁親王]]|| |- ||1887年（明治20年）10月10日{{sfn|山階会|1982|p=545}}||山階宮晃親王|| |- ||[[1890年]]（明治23年）4月16日||山階宮晃親王{{sfn|山階会|1982|p=614}}、有栖川宮熾仁親王{{Cite book |和書 |editor=高松宮家 |title=熾仁親王日記 |volume=巻5 |publisher=高松宮家 |year=1936 |page=316 |id={{NDLJP|1221442}}}}||三条実万の三十年祭 |- ||[[1925年]]（大正14年）5月18日{{Cite book |和書 |editor=毎日新聞社 |editor-link=毎日新聞社 |title=毎日年鑑 |volume=1926 |publisher=毎日新聞社 |year=1925 |page=144 |id={{NDLJP|2994755}}}}||[[昭和天皇|皇太子裕仁親王（後の昭和天皇）]]|| |- ||[[1928年]]（昭和3年）10月26日{{Cite book |和書 |editor=京都府 |title=昭和大礼京都府記録 |volume=上巻 |publisher=京都府 |year=1929 |page=13 |id={{NDLJP|1190228}}}}||[[閑院宮載仁親王]]||大礼使総裁として上洛 |- |}
== 境内 == * 本殿 * 中門 * 拝殿 * 神門 * 歌碑 - [[上田秋成]]、[[湯川秀樹]]。 * 愛の木 - [[カツラ (植物)|桂]]の木の葉が[[ハート (シンボル)|ハート]]形になっている為に名付けられたとされる。 * 天壌無窮の石碑 - 三條實萬が祈念していた軸の言葉を石に刻んだもの。 * 茶室 - 旧春興殿、虚中庵。 * 社務所 Nashinoki-jinja-026.jpg|一の鳥居。[[扁額]]は[[閑院宮載仁親王]]の染筆{{Cite book |和書|author=鉄道省 |title=神まうで |year=1933 |page=92 |id={{NDLJP|1187400/84}}}}。 Nashinoki-jinja-027.jpg|二の鳥居。扁額は[[久邇宮朝彦親王]]の染筆。 === 染井の水 === 境内の[[井戸]]の水は「染井の水」と呼ばれ、'''京都三名水'''の一つで、唯一現存している{{Efn2|他の二つは[[京都御苑]]の「縣井」、堀川通五条西入るにある「左女牛井」。懸井は京都御苑の西北、[[宮内庁]]京都事務所の裏側に水が枯れ、現在は[[1996年]]（平成8年）に近くで掘り直し再生。}}{{Efn2|'''京都（御所）三名水'''と混同されることがある。こちらは醒ヶ井・県井・染殿井の三つで、染殿井は、京都御苑の東北、「母と子の森」の南の京都迎賓館の裏側にある。醒ヶ井は現在は石碑のみ。[[1991年]]（平成3年）に、四条堀川付近の四条醒ヶ井角にある京菓子屋「亀屋良長」が、社屋改築の際に湧いた豊富な地下水を「{{読み仮名|醒ヶ井|さめがい}}」と名付け復活した。染井と染殿井は同一水源と思われるが異なる井戸である。}}。飲料可。[[藤原良房]]の館にあり染物に使っていた{{Cite book |和書|author=小野芳朗 |title=水の環境史「京の名水」はなぜ失われたか |publisher=[[PHP研究所]] |series=[[PHP新書]] |date=2001年5月 |page=208 |ISBN=9784569616186}}。 == 祭礼 == * 毎月1日・15日：月次祭 * 1月最終日曜（または2月第一日曜）：[[元服]]式 * 4月18日：春季例祭 * 9月中旬 - 下旬：萩祭り * 10月10日：秋季例祭 * 10月最終日曜：[[七五三]] == アクセス == * [[京都市営バス|京都市バス]]、[[京都バス]] 「府立医大病院前」停留所下車徒歩1分 == 関連図書 == * {{Cite book |和書|author=二六興信所 編纂 |author2=山田米吉 編 |title=勤王事蹟別格官幣社精史 |publisher=二六興信所 |year=1935 |pages=74-77 |chapter=梨木神社 |id={{NDLJP|1112175/48}}}} * {{Cite book |和書|author=安津素彦 |author2=梅田義彦 編集兼監修者 |title=神道辞典 |publisher=[[神社新報|神社新報社]] |year=1968 |page=43 |id={{NDLJP|2997629}}}} * {{Cite book |和書|author=白井永二 |author-link=白井永二 |author2=土岐昌訓 編 |title=神社辞典 |publisher=[[東京堂出版]] |year=1979 |page=256 |id={{NDLJP|12266197}}}} == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{Notelist2}} === 出典 === {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite book |和書 |editor=山階会 |title=山階宮三代 |volume=上 |publisher=山階会 |year=1982 |id={{NDLJP|12260162}} |ref=harv}} == 関連項目 == * [[梨木神社爆破事件]] == 外部リンク == {{commonscat|Nashinoki-jinja}} {{osm box|w|638453452}} * {{Official website}} * {{Wayback|date=20100402162411|url=http://www.kyoto-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/02/014/index.html|title=梨木神社}}（京都府神社庁・神社本庁からの脱退によりリンク切れ） * {{京都観光Navi|7|435}} * {{TripAdvisor|access-date=2021-3-24}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:なしのきしんしや}} [[Category:京都市の神社]] [[Category:別格官幣社]] [[Category:上京区の歴史]] [[Category:三条家|社なしのき]] [[Category:上京区の建築物]] [[Category:京都府の景観重要建造物]] [[Category:三条実美]] [[Category:単立神社]]

{| class="infobox vcard" style="border-spacing:2px;" ! colspan="2" class="infobox-above" style="background-color:" |Nashinoki Shrine |- | colspan="2" class="infobox-image" |[[File:Nashinoki Shrine Haiden 3.jpg|frameless]] |} Nashinoki Shrine (梨木神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] in {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|en}}. Although it was never a [[Beppyo shrine]], it used to have the rank of Bekkaku kanpeisha under the modern system of ranked Shinto shrines.
Nashinoki Shrine is a relatively new Shinto shrine in Kyoto. It was built in 1885.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Fushimi Inari Taisha {{!}} Location {{!}} Shajiraku – Quality traditional food and cultural experiences at Kyoto shrines and temples.|url=https://www.shajiraku.jp/en/plan/location_detail.php?id=2|access-date=2023-04-07|website=社寺楽 Shajiraku|language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|date=2020-10-04|title=Nashinoki Shrine Kyoto, a Bush Clover Shrine {{!}} All About Kyoto|url=https://allaboutkyoto.com/en/nashinoki-shrine-kyoto/|access-date=2023-04-07|language=en-US|archive-date=2023-04-07|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230407205909/https://allaboutkyoto.com/en/nashinoki-shrine-kyoto/|url-status=dead}}</ref>
The Nashinoki Shrine hosts an annual {{ill|Bush clover|en}} festival.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Hagi Matsuri|url=https://www.discoverkyoto.com/event-calendar/september/hagi-matsuri-nashinoki-shrine/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Discover Kyoto|language=en}}</ref>
== Bombing == The '''Nashinoki Shrine Bombing Incident''' (なしのきじんじゃばくはじけん) was a [[1977]] ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 52) guerrilla bombing that took place on [[January 1]] in {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}'s {{ill|Kamigyo Ward|ja|上京区}}, at {{ill|Nashinoki Shrine|ja|梨木神社}}. It was perpetrated by {{ill|Saburō Katō (New Left)|ja|加藤三郎 (新左翼)}} of the {{ill|Japanese New Left|ja|日本の新左翼}}.
=== History of the Damaged Structure === Nashinoki Shrine was established in [[1885]] based on a decree from {{ill|Prince Kuni Asahiko|ja|久邇宮朝彦親王}}, on the site of the former Sanjo residence, enshrining {{ill|Sanetomi Sanjo|ja|三条実万}}, who contributed to the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}. Later, his son, {{ill|Sanjō Sanetomi|ja|三条実美}}, was also enshrined there. Katō criticized Sanjō Sanetomi as "the highest authority ({{ill|Daijō Daijin|ja|太政大臣}}) of the Meiji government, which became the bearer of {{ill|Japanese imperialism|ja|日本帝国主義}}," and proceeded to bomb Nashinoki Shrine, which enshrines him.
=== Overview of the Incident === On January 1, [[1977]] ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 52) at around 8:45 PM, an [[:en:Improvised explosive device|Improvised explosive device]] installed by Katō exploded in the main hall of Nashinoki Shrine, causing a fire. Thanks to the immediate firefighting efforts by the {{ill|guji|ja|宮司}} and {{ill|negi|ja|禰宜}}, the damage was limited to part of the main hall being scorched. Although it was New Year's Day, there were no worshippers present as it was nighttime, and no injuries were reported. Subsequent investigation found fragments of a fire extinguisher, confirming the incident as a bombing with a fire extinguisher bomb.
On January 5, Katō sent a {{ill|claim of responsibility|ja|犯行声明}} to the media under the names "Yami no Tsuchigumo" and "Uke Hime no Kodomotachi." The statement mentioned the "three crimes of Sanjō Sanetomi (establishing the exploitative and oppressive Meiji government, invading other peoples, and suppressing anti-establishment movements)," sentencing him to death by bombing. It is said that the inclusion of a non-terrorist sounding name like "Uke Hime no Kodomotachi" was intended as an antithesis to the "paternalistic {{ill|Emperor system|ja|天皇制}}."
== See Also == * {{ill|Theory of Japanese Indigenous People|ja|日本原住民論}} * {{ill|Anti-Japaneseism|ja|反日亡国論}} * {{ill|Anti-monarchism in Japan|ja|反天皇制}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nashinoki Shrine Bombing Incident}} [[Category:Explosions_in_Japan]] [[Category:Terrorist incidents in Japan]] [[Category:Religious terrorism]] [[Category:Incidents involving the Japanese New Left]] [[Category:Shinto history incidents]] [[Category:History of Kamigyo-ku]] [[Category:1977 crimes in Japan]] [[Category:January 1977 events in Asia]] [[Category:Shōwa period postwar Kyoto]]
== References == * {{ill|Kyoto Shimbun|ja|京都新聞}}, January 3, 1977 morning edition, January 13, 1977 morning edition * Saburō Katō, "Opinion Paper 'From the Pigs of the Earth to You'" {{ill|The Science of Thought Society|ja|思想の科学社}}, 1992
== References == {{Reflist}} [[Category:Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] [[Category:Nashinoki Shrine]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[simple:Nashinoki Shrine]][[Category:Nashinoki Shrine]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Nashinoki Shrine]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Nashinoki Shrine]][[Category:Nashinoki Shrine]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[en:Nashinoki Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[en:Nashinoki Shrine]][[en:Nashinoki Shrine]][[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Nashinoki Shrine]][[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Nashinoki Shrine]][[simple:Nashinoki Shrine]]

revision.text:
{{draft}} {{promising draft}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here [[every:Nashinoki Shrine|Nashinoki Shrine]] and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Nashinoki Shrine]][[en:draft:Nashinoki Shrine]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}}{| class="infobox vcard" style="border-spacing:2px;" ! colspan="2" class="infobox-above" style="background-color:" |Nashinoki Shrine |- | colspan="2" class="infobox-image" |[[File:Nashinoki Shrine Haiden 3.jpg|frameless]] |} Nashinoki Shrine (梨木神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] in {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|en}}. Although it was never a [[Beppyo shrine]], it used to have the rank of Bekkaku kanpeisha under the modern system of ranked Shinto shrines.
Nashinoki Shrine is a relatively new Shinto shrine in Kyoto. It was built in 1885.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Fushimi Inari Taisha {{!}} Location {{!}} Shajiraku – Quality traditional food and cultural experiences at Kyoto shrines and temples.|url=https://www.shajiraku.jp/en/plan/location_detail.php?id=2|access-date=2023-04-07|website=社寺楽 Shajiraku|language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|date=2020-10-04|title=Nashinoki Shrine Kyoto, a Bush Clover Shrine {{!}} All About Kyoto|url=https://allaboutkyoto.com/en/nashinoki-shrine-kyoto/|access-date=2023-04-07|language=en-US|archive-date=2023-04-07|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230407205909/https://allaboutkyoto.com/en/nashinoki-shrine-kyoto/|url-status=dead}}</ref>
The Nashinoki Shrine hosts an annual {{ill|Bush clover|en}} festival.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Hagi Matsuri|url=https://www.discoverkyoto.com/event-calendar/september/hagi-matsuri-nashinoki-shrine/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Discover Kyoto|language=en}}</ref>
== Bombing == The '''Nashinoki Shrine Bombing Incident''' (なしのきじんじゃばくはじけん) was a [[1977]] ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 52) guerrilla bombing that took place on [[January 1]] in {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}'s {{ill|Kamigyo Ward|ja|上京区}}, at {{ill|Nashinoki Shrine|ja|梨木神社}}. It was perpetrated by {{ill|Saburō Katō (New Left)|ja|加藤三郎 (新左翼)}} of the {{ill|Japanese New Left|ja|日本の新左翼}}.
=== History of the Damaged Structure === Nashinoki Shrine was established in [[1885]] based on a decree from {{ill|Prince Kuni Asahiko|ja|久邇宮朝彦親王}}, on the site of the former Sanjo residence, enshrining {{ill|Sanetomi Sanjo|ja|三条実万}}, who contributed to the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}. Later, his son, {{ill|Sanjō Sanetomi|ja|三条実美}}, was also enshrined there. Katō criticized Sanjō Sanetomi as "the highest authority ({{ill|Daijō Daijin|ja|太政大臣}}) of the Meiji government, which became the bearer of {{ill|Japanese imperialism|ja|日本帝国主義}}," and proceeded to bomb Nashinoki Shrine, which enshrines him.
=== Overview of the Incident === On January 1, [[1977]] ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 52) at around 8:45 PM, an [[:en:Improvised explosive device|Improvised explosive device]] installed by Katō exploded in the main hall of Nashinoki Shrine, causing a fire. Thanks to the immediate firefighting efforts by the {{ill|guji|ja|宮司}} and {{ill|negi|ja|禰宜}}, the damage was limited to part of the main hall being scorched. Although it was New Year's Day, there were no worshippers present as it was nighttime, and no injuries were reported. Subsequent investigation found fragments of a fire extinguisher, confirming the incident as a bombing with a fire extinguisher bomb.
On January 5, Katō sent a {{ill|claim of responsibility|ja|犯行声明}} to the media under the names "Yami no Tsuchigumo" and "Uke Hime no Kodomotachi." The statement mentioned the "three crimes of Sanjō Sanetomi (establishing the exploitative and oppressive Meiji government, invading other peoples, and suppressing anti-establishment movements)," sentencing him to death by bombing. It is said that the inclusion of a non-terrorist sounding name like "Uke Hime no Kodomotachi" was intended as an antithesis to the "paternalistic {{ill|Emperor system|ja|天皇制}}."
== See Also == * {{ill|Theory of Japanese Indigenous People|ja|日本原住民論}} * {{ill|Anti-Japaneseism|ja|反日亡国論}} * {{ill|Anti-monarchism in Japan|ja|反天皇制}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nashinoki Shrine Bombing Incident}} [[Category:Explosions_in_Japan]] [[Category:Terrorist incidents in Japan]] [[Category:Religious terrorism]] [[Category:Incidents involving the Japanese New Left]] [[Category:Shinto history incidents]] [[Category:History of Kamigyo-ku]] [[Category:1977 crimes in Japan]] [[Category:January 1977 events in Asia]] [[Category:Shōwa period postwar Kyoto]]
== References == * {{ill|Kyoto Shimbun|ja|京都新聞}}, January 3, 1977 morning edition, January 13, 1977 morning edition * Saburō Katō, "Opinion Paper 'From the Pigs of the Earth to You'" {{ill|The Science of Thought Society|ja|思想の科学社}}, 1992

Subsequent investigation found fragments of a fire extinguisher, confirming the incident as a bombing with a fire extinguisher bomb.
On January 5, Katō sent a {{ill|claim of responsibility|ja|犯行声明}} to the media under the names "Yami no Tsuchigumo" and "Uke Hime no Kodomotachi." The statement mentioned the "three crimes of Sanjō Sanetomi (establishing the exploitative and oppressive Meiji government, invading other peoples, and suppressing anti-establishment movements)," sentencing him to death by bombing. It is said that the inclusion of a non-terrorist sounding name like "Uke Hime no Kodomotachi" was intended as an antithesis to the "paternalistic {{ill|Emperor system|ja|天皇制}}."

[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}}{| class="infobox vcard" style="border-spacing:2px;" ! colspan="2" class="infobox-above" style="background-color:" |Nashinoki Shrine |- | colspan="2" class="infobox-image" |[[File:Nashinoki Shrine Haiden 3.jpg|frameless]] |} Nashinoki Shrine (梨木神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] in {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|en}}. Although it was never a [[Beppyo shrine]], it used to have the rank of Bekkaku kanpeisha under the modern system of ranked Shinto shrines.
Nashinoki Shrine is a relatively new Shinto shrine in Kyoto. It was built in 1885.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Fushimi Inari Taisha {{!}} Location {{!}} Shajiraku – Quality traditional food and cultural experiences at Kyoto shrines and temples.|url=https://www.shajiraku.jp/en/plan/location_detail.php?id=2|access-date=2023-04-07|website=社寺楽 Shajiraku|language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|date=2020-10-04|title=Nashinoki Shrine Kyoto, a Bush Clover Shrine {{!}} All About Kyoto|url=https://allaboutkyoto.com/en/nashinoki-shrine-kyoto/|access-date=2023-04-07|language=en-US|archive-date=2023-04-07|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230407205909/https://allaboutkyoto.com/en/nashinoki-shrine-kyoto/|url-status=dead}}</ref>
The Nashinoki Shrine hosts an annual {{ill|Bush clover|en}} festival.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Hagi Matsuri|url=https://www.discoverkyoto.com/event-calendar/september/hagi-matsuri-nashinoki-shrine/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Discover Kyoto|language=en}}</ref>
== Bombing == The '''Nashinoki Shrine Bombing Incident''' (なしのきじんじゃばくはじけん) was a [[1977]] ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 52) guerrilla bombing that took place on [[January 1]] in {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}'s {{ill|Kamigyo Ward|ja|上京区}}, at {{ill|Nashinoki Shrine|ja|梨木神社}}. It was perpetrated by {{ill|Saburō Katō (New Left)|ja|加藤三郎 (新左翼)}} of the {{ill|Japanese New Left|ja|日本の新左翼}}.
=== History of the Damaged Structure === Nashinoki Shrine was established in [[1885]] based on a decree from {{ill|Prince Kuni Asahiko|ja|久邇宮朝彦親王}}, on the site of the former Sanjo residence, enshrining {{ill|Sanetomi Sanjo|ja|三条実万}}, who contributed to the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}. Later, his son, {{ill|Sanjō Sanetomi|ja|三条実美}}, was also enshrined there. Katō criticized Sanjō Sanetomi as "the highest authority ({{ill|Daijō Daijin|ja|太政大臣}}) of the Meiji government, which became the bearer of {{ill|Japanese imperialism|ja|日本帝国主義}}," and proceeded to bomb Nashinoki Shrine, which enshrines him.
=== Overview of the Incident === On January 1, [[1977]] ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 52) at around 8:45 PM, an [[:en:Improvised explosive device|Improvised explosive device]] installed by Katō exploded in the main hall of Nashinoki Shrine, causing a fire. Thanks to the immediate firefighting efforts by the {{ill|guji|ja|宮司}} and {{ill|negi|ja|禰宜}}, the damage was limited to part of the main hall being scorched. Although it was New Year's Day, there were no worshippers present as it was nighttime, and no injuries were reported. Subsequent investigation found fragments of a fire extinguisher, confirming the incident as a bombing with a fire extinguisher bomb.
On January 5, Katō sent a {{ill|claim of responsibility|ja|犯行声明}} to the media under the names "Yami no Tsuchigumo" and "Uke Hime no Kodomotachi." The statement mentioned the "three crimes of Sanjō Sanetomi (establishing the exploitative and oppressive Meiji government, invading other peoples, and suppressing anti-establishment movements)," sentencing him to death by bombing. It is said that the inclusion of a non-terrorist sounding name like "Uke Hime no Kodomotachi" was intended as an antithesis to the "paternalistic {{ill|Emperor system|ja|天皇制}}."
== See Also == * {{ill|Theory of Japanese Indigenous People|ja|日本原住民論}} * {{ill|Anti-Japaneseism|ja|反日亡国論}} * {{ill|Anti-monarchism in Japan|ja|反天皇制}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nashinoki Shrine Bombing Incident}} [[Category:Explosions_in_Japan]] [[Category:Terrorist incidents in Japan]] [[Category:Religious terrorism]] [[Category:Incidents involving the Japanese New Left]] [[Category:Shinto history incidents]] [[Category:History of Kamigyo-ku]] [[Category:1977 crimes in Japan]] [[Category:January 1977 events in Asia]] [[Category:Shōwa period postwar Kyoto]]

[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:36, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}}{{draft}} {{promising draft}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here [[every:Nashinoki Shrine|Nashinoki Shrine]] and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Nashinoki Shrine]][[en:draft:Nashinoki Shrine]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}}{| class="infobox vcard" style="border-spacing:2px;" ! colspan="2" class="infobox-above" style="background-color:" |Nashinoki Shrine |- | colspan="2" class="infobox-image" |[[File:Nashinoki Shrine Haiden 3.jpg|frameless]] |} Nashinoki Shrine (梨木神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] in {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|en}}. Although it was never a [[Beppyo shrine]], it used to have the rank of Bekkaku kanpeisha under the modern system of ranked Shinto shrines.
Nashinoki Shrine is a relatively new Shinto shrine in Kyoto. It was built in 1885.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Fushimi Inari Taisha {{!}} Location {{!}} Shajiraku – Quality traditional food and cultural experiences at Kyoto shrines and temples.|url=https://www.shajiraku.jp/en/plan/location_detail.php?id=2|access-date=2023-04-07|website=社寺楽 Shajiraku|language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|date=2020-10-04|title=Nashinoki Shrine Kyoto, a Bush Clover Shrine {{!}} All About Kyoto|url=https://allaboutkyoto.com/en/nashinoki-shrine-kyoto/|access-date=2023-04-07|language=en-US|archive-date=2023-04-07|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230407205909/https://allaboutkyoto.com/en/nashinoki-shrine-kyoto/|url-status=dead}}</ref>
The Nashinoki Shrine hosts an annual {{ill|Bush clover|en}} festival.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Hagi Matsuri|url=https://www.discoverkyoto.com/event-calendar/september/hagi-matsuri-nashinoki-shrine/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Discover Kyoto|language=en}}</ref>
== Bombing == The '''Nashinoki Shrine Bombing Incident''' (なしのきじんじゃばくはじけん) was a [[1977]] ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 52) guerrilla bombing that took place on [[January 1]] in {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}'s {{ill|Kamigyo Ward|ja|上京区}}, at {{ill|Nashinoki Shrine|ja|梨木神社}}. It was perpetrated by {{ill|Saburō Katō (New Left)|ja|加藤三郎 (新左翼)}} of the {{ill|Japanese New Left|ja|日本の新左翼}}.
=== History of the Damaged Structure === Nashinoki Shrine was established in [[1885]] based on a decree from {{ill|Prince Kuni Asahiko|ja|久邇宮朝彦親王}}, on the site of the former Sanjo residence, enshrining {{ill|Sanetomi Sanjo|ja|三条実万}}, who contributed to the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}. Later, his son, {{ill|Sanjō Sanetomi|ja|三条実美}}, was also enshrined there. Katō criticized Sanjō Sanetomi as "the highest authority ({{ill|Daijō Daijin|ja|太政大臣}}) of the Meiji government, which became the bearer of {{ill|Japanese imperialism|ja|日本帝国主義}}," and proceeded to bomb Nashinoki Shrine, which enshrines him.
=== Overview of the Incident === On January 1, [[1977]] ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 52) at around 8:45 PM, an [[:en:Improvised explosive device|Improvised explosive device]] installed by Katō exploded in the main hall of Nashinoki Shrine, causing a fire. Thanks to the immediate firefighting efforts by the {{ill|guji|ja|宮司}} and {{ill|negi|ja|禰宜}}, the damage was limited to part of the main hall being scorched. Although it was New Year's Day, there were no worshippers present as it was nighttime, and no injuries were reported. Subsequent investigation found fragments of a fire extinguisher, confirming the incident as a bombing with a fire extinguisher bomb.
On January 5, Katō sent a {{ill|claim of responsibility|ja|犯行声明}} to the media under the names "Yami no Tsuchigumo" and "Uke Hime no Kodomotachi." The statement mentioned the "three crimes of Sanjō Sanetomi (establishing the exploitative and oppressive Meiji government, invading other peoples, and suppressing anti-establishment movements)," sentencing him to death by bombing. It is said that the inclusion of a non-terrorist sounding name like "Uke Hime no Kodomotachi" was intended as an antithesis to the "paternalistic {{ill|Emperor system|ja|天皇制}}."
== See Also == * {{ill|Theory of Japanese Indigenous People|ja|日本原住民論}} * {{ill|Anti-Japaneseism|ja|反日亡国論}} * {{ill|Anti-monarchism in Japan|ja|反天皇制}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nashinoki Shrine Bombing Incident}} [[Category:Explosions_in_Japan]] [[Category:Terrorist incidents in Japan]] [[Category:Religious terrorism]] [[Category:Incidents involving the Japanese New Left]] [[Category:Shinto history incidents]] [[Category:History of Kamigyo-ku]] [[Category:1977 crimes in Japan]] [[Category:January 1977 events in Asia]] [[Category:Shōwa period postwar Kyoto]]
== References == * {{ill|Kyoto Shimbun|ja|京都新聞}}, January 3, 1977 morning edition, January 13, 1977 morning edition * Saburō Katō, "Opinion Paper 'From the Pigs of the Earth to You'" {{ill|The Science of Thought Society|ja|思想の科学社}}, 1992

{{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here [[every:Nashinoki Shrine|Nashinoki Shrine]] and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Nashinoki Shrine]][[en:draft:Nashinoki Shrine]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:36, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here [[every:Nashinoki Shrine|Nashinoki Shrine]] and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Nashinoki Shrine]][[en:draft:Nashinoki Shrine]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}}{| class="infobox vcard" style="border-spacing:2px;" ! colspan="2" class="infobox-above" style="background-color:" |Nashinoki Shrine |- | colspan="2" class="infobox-image" |[[File:Nashinoki Shrine Haiden 3.jpg|frameless]] |} Nashinoki Shrine (梨木神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] in {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|en}}. Although it was never a [[Beppyo shrine]], it used to have the rank of Bekkaku kanpeisha under the modern system of ranked Shinto shrines.
Nashinoki Shrine is a relatively new Shinto shrine in Kyoto. It was built in 1885.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Fushimi Inari Taisha {{!}} Location {{!}} Shajiraku – Quality traditional food and cultural experiences at Kyoto shrines and temples.|url=https://www.shajiraku.jp/en/plan/location_detail.php?id=2|access-date=2023-04-07|website=社寺楽 Shajiraku|language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|date=2020-10-04|title=Nashinoki Shrine Kyoto, a Bush Clover Shrine {{!}} All About Kyoto|url=https://allaboutkyoto.com/en/nashinoki-shrine-kyoto/|access-date=2023-04-07|language=en-US|archive-date=2023-04-07|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230407205909/https://allaboutkyoto.com/en/nashinoki-shrine-kyoto/|url-status=dead}}</ref>
The Nashinoki Shrine hosts an annual {{ill|Bush clover|en}} festival.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Hagi Matsuri|url=https://www.discoverkyoto.com/event-calendar/september/hagi-matsuri-nashinoki-shrine/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Discover Kyoto|language=en}}</ref>
== Bombing == The '''Nashinoki Shrine Bombing Incident''' (なしのきじんじゃばくはじけん) was a [[1977]] ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 52) guerrilla bombing that took place on [[January 1]] in {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}'s {{ill|Kamigyo Ward|ja|上京区}}, at {{ill|Nashinoki Shrine|ja|梨木神社}}. It was perpetrated by {{ill|Saburō Katō (New Left)|ja|加藤三郎 (新左翼)}} of the {{ill|Japanese New Left|ja|日本の新左翼}}.
=== History of the Damaged Structure === Nashinoki Shrine was established in [[1885]] based on a decree from {{ill|Prince Kuni Asahiko|ja|久邇宮朝彦親王}}, on the site of the former Sanjo residence, enshrining {{ill|Sanetomi Sanjo|ja|三条実万}}, who contributed to the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}. Later, his son, {{ill|Sanjō Sanetomi|ja|三条実美}}, was also enshrined there. Katō criticized Sanjō Sanetomi as "the highest authority ({{ill|Daijō Daijin|ja|太政大臣}}) of the Meiji government, which became the bearer of {{ill|Japanese imperialism|ja|日本帝国主義}}," and proceeded to bomb Nashinoki Shrine, which enshrines him.
=== Overview of the Incident === On January 1, [[1977]] ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 52) at around 8:45 PM, an [[:en:Improvised explosive device|Improvised explosive device]] installed by Katō exploded in the main hall of Nashinoki Shrine, causing a fire. Thanks to the immediate firefighting efforts by the {{ill|guji|ja|宮司}} and {{ill|negi|ja|禰宜}}, the damage was limited to part of the main hall being scorched. Although it was New Year's Day, there were no worshippers present as it was nighttime, and no injuries were reported. Subsequent investigation found fragments of a fire extinguisher, confirming the incident as a bombing with a fire extinguisher bomb.
On January 5, Katō sent a {{ill|claim of responsibility|ja|犯行声明}} to the media under the names "Yami no Tsuchigumo" and "Uke Hime no Kodomotachi." The statement mentioned the "three crimes of Sanjō Sanetomi (establishing the exploitative and oppressive Meiji government, invading other peoples, and suppressing anti-establishment movements)," sentencing him to death by bombing. It is said that the inclusion of a non-terrorist sounding name like "Uke Hime no Kodomotachi" was intended as an antithesis to the "paternalistic {{ill|Emperor system|ja|天皇制}}."
== See Also == * {{ill|Theory of Japanese Indigenous People|ja|日本原住民論}} * {{ill|Anti-Japaneseism|ja|反日亡国論}} * {{ill|Anti-monarchism in Japan|ja|反天皇制}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nashinoki Shrine Bombing Incident}} [[Category:Explosions_in_Japan]] [[Category:Terrorist incidents in Japan]] [[Category:Religious terrorism]] [[Category:Incidents involving the Japanese New Left]] [[Category:Shinto history incidents]] [[Category:History of Kamigyo-ku]] [[Category:1977 crimes in Japan]] [[Category:January 1977 events in Asia]] [[Category:Shōwa period postwar Kyoto]]
== References == * {{ill|Kyoto Shimbun|ja|京都新聞}}, January 3, 1977 morning edition, January 13, 1977 morning edition * Saburō Katō, "Opinion Paper 'From the Pigs of the Earth to You'" {{ill|The Science of Thought Society|ja|思想の科学社}}, 1992

[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}}{| class="infobox vcard" style="border-spacing:2px;" ! colspan="2" class="infobox-above" style="background-color:" |Nashinoki Shrine |- | colspan="2" class="infobox-image" |[[File:Nashinoki Shrine Haiden 3.jpg|frameless]] |} Nashinoki Shrine (梨木神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] in {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|en}}. Although it was never a [[Beppyo shrine]], it used to have the rank of Bekkaku kanpeisha under the modern system of ranked Shinto shrines.
Nashinoki Shrine is a relatively new Shinto shrine in Kyoto. It was built in 1885.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Fushimi Inari Taisha {{!}} Location {{!}} Shajiraku – Quality traditional food and cultural experiences at Kyoto shrines and temples.|url=https://www.shajiraku.jp/en/plan/location_detail.php?id=2|access-date=2023-04-07|website=社寺楽 Shajiraku|language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|date=2020-10-04|title=Nashinoki Shrine Kyoto, a Bush Clover Shrine {{!}} All About Kyoto|url=https://allaboutkyoto.com/en/nashinoki-shrine-kyoto/|access-date=2023-04-07|language=en-US|archive-date=2023-04-07|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230407205909/https://allaboutkyoto.com/en/nashinoki-shrine-kyoto/|url-status=dead}}</ref>
The Nashinoki Shrine hosts an annual {{ill|Bush clover|en}} festival.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Hagi Matsuri|url=https://www.discoverkyoto.com/event-calendar/september/hagi-matsuri-nashinoki-shrine/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Discover Kyoto|language=en}}</ref>
== Bombing == The '''Nashinoki Shrine Bombing Incident''' (なしのきじんじゃばくはじけん) was a [[1977]] ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 52) guerrilla bombing that took place on [[January 1]] in {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}'s {{ill|Kamigyo Ward|ja|上京区}}, at {{ill|Nashinoki Shrine|ja|梨木神社}}. It was perpetrated by {{ill|Saburō Katō (New Left)|ja|加藤三郎 (新左翼)}} of the {{ill|Japanese New Left|ja|日本の新左翼}}.
=== History of the Damaged Structure === Nashinoki Shrine was established in [[1885]] based on a decree from {{ill|Prince Kuni Asahiko|ja|久邇宮朝彦親王}}, on the site of the former Sanjo residence, enshrining {{ill|Sanetomi Sanjo|ja|三条実万}}, who contributed to the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}. Later, his son, {{ill|Sanjō Sanetomi|ja|三条実美}}, was also enshrined there. Katō criticized Sanjō Sanetomi as "the highest authority ({{ill|Daijō Daijin|ja|太政大臣}}) of the Meiji government, which became the bearer of {{ill|Japanese imperialism|ja|日本帝国主義}}," and proceeded to bomb Nashinoki Shrine, which enshrines him.
=== Overview of the Incident === On January 1, [[1977]] ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 52) at around 8:45 PM, an [[:en:Improvised explosive device|Improvised explosive device]] installed by Katō exploded in the main hall of Nashinoki Shrine, causing a fire. Thanks to the immediate firefighting efforts by the {{ill|guji|ja|宮司}} and {{ill|negi|ja|禰宜}}, the damage was limited to part of the main hall being scorched. Although it was New Year's Day, there were no worshippers present as it was nighttime, and no injuries were reported. Subsequent investigation found fragments of a fire extinguisher, confirming the incident as a bombing with a fire extinguisher bomb.
On January 5, Katō sent a {{ill|claim of responsibility|ja|犯行声明}} to the media under the names "Yami no Tsuchigumo" and "Uke Hime no Kodomotachi." The statement mentioned the "three crimes of Sanjō Sanetomi (establishing the exploitative and oppressive Meiji government, invading other peoples, and suppressing anti-establishment movements)," sentencing him to death by bombing. It is said that the inclusion of a non-terrorist sounding name like "Uke Hime no Kodomotachi" was intended as an antithesis to the "paternalistic {{ill|Emperor system|ja|天皇制}}."
== See Also == * {{ill|Theory of Japanese Indigenous People|ja|日本原住民論}} * {{ill|Anti-Japaneseism|ja|反日亡国論}} * {{ill|Anti-monarchism in Japan|ja|反天皇制}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nashinoki Shrine Bombing Incident}} [[Category:Explosions_in_Japan]] [[Category:Terrorist incidents in Japan]] [[Category:Religious terrorism]] [[Category:Incidents involving the Japanese New Left]] [[Category:Shinto history incidents]] [[Category:History of Kamigyo-ku]] [[Category:1977 crimes in Japan]] [[Category:January 1977 events in Asia]] [[Category:Shōwa period postwar Kyoto]]

While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}}{| class="infobox vcard" style="border-spacing:2px;" ! colspan="2" class="infobox-above" style="background-color:" |Nashinoki Shrine |- | colspan="2" class="infobox-image" |[[File:Nashinoki Shrine Haiden 3.jpg|frameless]] |} Nashinoki Shrine (梨木神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] in {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|en}}. Although it was never a [[Beppyo shrine]], it used to have the rank of Bekkaku kanpeisha under the modern system of ranked Shinto shrines.
Nashinoki Shrine is a relatively new Shinto shrine in Kyoto. It was built in 1885.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Fushimi Inari Taisha {{!}} Location {{!}} Shajiraku – Quality traditional food and cultural experiences at Kyoto shrines and temples.|url=https://www.shajiraku.jp/en/plan/location_detail.php?id=2|access-date=2023-04-07|website=社寺楽 Shajiraku|language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|date=2020-10-04|title=Nashinoki Shrine Kyoto, a Bush Clover Shrine {{!}} All About Kyoto|url=https://allaboutkyoto.com/en/nashinoki-shrine-kyoto/|access-date=2023-04-07|language=en-US|archive-date=2023-04-07|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230407205909/https://allaboutkyoto.com/en/nashinoki-shrine-kyoto/|url-status=dead}}</ref>
The Nashinoki Shrine hosts an annual {{ill|Bush clover|en}} festival.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Hagi Matsuri|url=https://www.discoverkyoto.com/event-calendar/september/hagi-matsuri-nashinoki-shrine/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Discover Kyoto|language=en}}</ref>
== Bombing == The '''Nashinoki Shrine Bombing Incident''' (なしのきじんじゃばくはじけん) was a [[1977]] ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 52) guerrilla bombing that took place on [[January 1]] in {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}'s {{ill|Kamigyo Ward|ja|上京区}}, at {{ill|Nashinoki Shrine|ja|梨木神社}}. It was perpetrated by {{ill|Saburō Katō (New Left)|ja|加藤三郎 (新左翼)}} of the {{ill|Japanese New Left|ja|日本の新左翼}}.
=== History of the Damaged Structure === Nashinoki Shrine was established in [[1885]] based on a decree from {{ill|Prince Kuni Asahiko|ja|久邇宮朝彦親王}}, on the site of the former Sanjo residence, enshrining {{ill|Sanetomi Sanjo|ja|三条実万}}, who contributed to the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}. Later, his son, {{ill|Sanjō Sanetomi|ja|三条実美}}, was also enshrined there. Katō criticized Sanjō Sanetomi as "the highest authority ({{ill|Daijō Daijin|ja|太政大臣}}) of the Meiji government, which became the bearer of {{ill|Japanese imperialism|ja|日本帝国主義}}," and proceeded to bomb Nashinoki Shrine, which enshrines him.
=== Overview of the Incident === On January 1, [[1977]] ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 52) at around 8:45 PM, an [[:en:Improvised explosive device|Improvised explosive device]] installed by Katō exploded in the main hall of Nashinoki Shrine, causing a fire. Thanks to the immediate firefighting efforts by the {{ill|guji|ja|宮司}} and {{ill|negi|ja|禰宜}}, the damage was limited to part of the main hall being scorched. Although it was New Year's Day, there were no worshippers present as it was nighttime, and no injuries were reported. Subsequent investigation found fragments of a fire extinguisher, confirming the incident as a bombing with a fire extinguisher bomb.
On January 5, Katō sent a {{ill|claim of responsibility|ja|犯行声明}} to the media under the names "Yami no Tsuchigumo" and "Uke Hime no Kodomotachi." The statement mentioned the "three crimes of Sanjō Sanetomi (establishing the exploitative and oppressive Meiji government, invading other peoples, and suppressing anti-establishment movements)," sentencing him to death by bombing. It is said that the inclusion of a non-terrorist sounding name like "Uke Hime no Kodomotachi" was intended as an antithesis to the "paternalistic {{ill|Emperor system|ja|天皇制}}."

It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here [[every:Nashinoki Shrine|Nashinoki Shrine]] and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Nashinoki Shrine]][[en:draft:Nashinoki Shrine]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:36, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here [[every:Nashinoki Shrine|Nashinoki Shrine]] and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Nashinoki Shrine]][[en:draft:Nashinoki Shrine]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}}{| class="infobox vcard" style="border-spacing:2px;" ! colspan="2" class="infobox-above" style="background-color:" |Nashinoki Shrine |- | colspan="2" class="infobox-image" |[[File:Nashinoki Shrine Haiden 3.jpg|frameless]] |} Nashinoki Shrine (梨木神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] in {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|en}}. Although it was never a [[Beppyo shrine]], it used to have the rank of Bekkaku kanpeisha under the modern system of ranked Shinto shrines.
Nashinoki Shrine is a relatively new Shinto shrine in Kyoto. It was built in 1885.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Fushimi Inari Taisha {{!}} Location {{!}} Shajiraku – Quality traditional food and cultural experiences at Kyoto shrines and temples.|url=https://www.shajiraku.jp/en/plan/location_detail.php?id=2|access-date=2023-04-07|website=社寺楽 Shajiraku|language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|date=2020-10-04|title=Nashinoki Shrine Kyoto, a Bush Clover Shrine {{!}} All About Kyoto|url=https://allaboutkyoto.com/en/nashinoki-shrine-kyoto/|access-date=2023-04-07|language=en-US|archive-date=2023-04-07|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230407205909/https://allaboutkyoto.com/en/nashinoki-shrine-kyoto/|url-status=dead}}</ref>
The Nashinoki Shrine hosts an annual {{ill|Bush clover|en}} festival.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Hagi Matsuri|url=https://www.discoverkyoto.com/event-calendar/september/hagi-matsuri-nashinoki-shrine/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Discover Kyoto|language=en}}</ref>
== Bombing == The '''Nashinoki Shrine Bombing Incident''' (なしのきじんじゃばくはじけん) was a [[1977]] ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 52) guerrilla bombing that took place on [[January 1]] in {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}'s {{ill|Kamigyo Ward|ja|上京区}}, at {{ill|Nashinoki Shrine|ja|梨木神社}}. It was perpetrated by {{ill|Saburō Katō (New Left)|ja|加藤三郎 (新左翼)}} of the {{ill|Japanese New Left|ja|日本の新左翼}}.
=== History of the Damaged Structure === Nashinoki Shrine was established in [[1885]] based on a decree from {{ill|Prince Kuni Asahiko|ja|久邇宮朝彦親王}}, on the site of the former Sanjo residence, enshrining {{ill|Sanetomi Sanjo|ja|三条実万}}, who contributed to the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}. Later, his son, {{ill|Sanjō Sanetomi|ja|三条実美}}, was also enshrined there. Katō criticized Sanjō Sanetomi as "the highest authority ({{ill|Daijō Daijin|ja|太政大臣}}) of the Meiji government, which became the bearer of {{ill|Japanese imperialism|ja|日本帝国主義}}," and proceeded to bomb Nashinoki Shrine, which enshrines him.
=== Overview of the Incident === On January 1, [[1977]] ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 52) at around 8:45 PM, an [[:en:Improvised explosive device|Improvised explosive device]] installed by Katō exploded in the main hall of Nashinoki Shrine, causing a fire. Thanks to the immediate firefighting efforts by the {{ill|guji|ja|宮司}} and {{ill|negi|ja|禰宜}}, the damage was limited to part of the main hall being scorched. Although it was New Year's Day, there were no worshippers present as it was nighttime, and no injuries were reported. Subsequent investigation found fragments of a fire extinguisher, confirming the incident as a bombing with a fire extinguisher bomb.
On January 5, Katō sent a {{ill|claim of responsibility|ja|犯行声明}} to the media under the names "Yami no Tsuchigumo" and "Uke Hime no Kodomotachi." The statement mentioned the "three crimes of Sanjō Sanetomi (establishing the exploitative and oppressive Meiji government, invading other peoples, and suppressing anti-establishment movements)," sentencing him to death by bombing. It is said that the inclusion of a non-terrorist sounding name like "Uke Hime no Kodomotachi" was intended as an antithesis to the "paternalistic {{ill|Emperor system|ja|天皇制}}."
== See Also == * {{ill|Theory of Japanese Indigenous People|ja|日本原住民論}} * {{ill|Anti-Japaneseism|ja|反日亡国論}} * {{ill|Anti-monarchism in Japan|ja|反天皇制}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nashinoki Shrine Bombing Incident}} [[Category:Explosions_in_Japan]] [[Category:Terrorist incidents in Japan]] [[Category:Religious terrorism]] [[Category:Incidents involving the Japanese New Left]] [[Category:Shinto history incidents]] [[Category:History of Kamigyo-ku]] [[Category:1977 crimes in Japan]] [[Category:January 1977 events in Asia]] [[Category:Shōwa period postwar Kyoto]]

{{神社 |名称=梨木神社 |画像=[[画像:Nashinoki Shrine Haiden 3.jpg|250px]]
拝殿 |所在地=[[京都府]][[京都市]][[上京区]][[寺町通]][[広小路通 (京都市)|広小路]]上ル染殿町680 |位置={{Coord|35|1|30|N|135|46|2.5|E|region:JP-26:type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{Ublist| [[三条実万|三條實萬]] | [[三条実美|三條實美]] }} |社格=旧[[別格官幣社]] |創建=[[1885年]]（[[明治]]18年） |別名=萩の宮 |例祭=[[4月18日]]・[[10月10日]] |地図={{Location map|Japan Kyoto city|width=200|caption=}} }} '''梨木神社'''（なしのきじんじゃ）は、[[京都市]][[上京区]]染殿町にある[[神社]]。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[別格官幣社]]。[[明治維新]]に大きく貢献した[[三条実万|三條實萬]]・[[三条実美|三條實美]]父子を祭神とする。また境内には約500株の[[ハギ]]が植えられており、別名'''萩の宮'''とも呼ばれており、9月中旬から下旬には'''萩祭り'''がおこなわれる。 == 祭神 == * 主祭神 - [[三条実万|三條實萬]]、[[三条実美|三條實美]] == 歴史 == [[久邇宮朝彦親王]]の[[令旨]]により[[三条家]]の邸宅跡に[[三条実万|三條實萬]]を祀るため社殿が造営される。[[1885年]]（[[明治]]18年）10月10日、社号は地名から梨木神社とされ、さらに[[別格官幣社]]の列格を受けて創建される。その後[[1915年]]（[[大正]]4年）、[[大正天皇]]即位を記念して、子の[[三条実美|三條實美]]を合祀した。[[平安時代]]は[[藤原良房]]の娘[[藤原明子 (染殿后)|染殿后]]の里御所・染殿第であった。 [[1948年]]（[[昭和]]23年）に[[神社本庁]]の[[別表神社]]に加列されている。 1977年に反君主制主義者によって爆破された（[[梨木神社爆破事件]]）{{Cite news |和書 |title= |newspaper=[[京都新聞]] |date=1977-01-03 |edition=朝刊 |page=}}{{Cite news |和書 |title= |newspaper=京都新聞 |date=1977-01-13 |edition=朝刊 |page=}}。 社殿の修復等の資金集めに苦慮していた[[2013年]]（[[平成]]25年）、境内の参道を含む土地を[[マンション]]開発業者に60年の[[定期借地権]]で貸し、その賃貸料を社殿の修復費用に充てることとしたが、その計画が神社本庁の承認を得られなかったことから神社本庁から離脱して独立し、[[単立]]神社となった。これにより、別表神社の列から当社は外された。 神社本庁の別表神社が神社本庁から離脱したのは[[気多大社]]以来のこととなる。
皇室の参拝歴
{| class="wikitable" style="font-size:small;" !年月日!!参拝者!!備考 |- ||[[1885年]]（明治18年）10月6日{{sfn|山階会|1982|p=510}}||[[山階宮晃親王]]||神霊鎮座祭 |- ||1885年（明治18年）11月15日{{sfn|山階会|1982|p=512}}||山階宮晃親王||[[国分貞胤]]宅の茶事の帰路 |- ||[[1886年]]（明治19年）4月15日{{sfn|山階会|1982|p=520}}||山階宮晃親王|| |- ||1887年（明治20年）2月2日{{sfn|山階会|1982|p=534}}||山階宮晃親王||[[京都御所]]参内の途次 |- ||[[1887年]]（明治20年）1月23日{{Cite book |和書 |editor=高松宮家 |title=熾仁親王日記 |volume=巻4 |publisher=高松宮家 |year=1936 |page=461 |id={{NDLJP|1221430}}}}||[[有栖川宮熾仁親王]]|| |- ||1887年（明治20年）10月10日{{sfn|山階会|1982|p=545}}||山階宮晃親王|| |- ||[[1890年]]（明治23年）4月16日||山階宮晃親王{{sfn|山階会|1982|p=614}}、有栖川宮熾仁親王{{Cite book |和書 |editor=高松宮家 |title=熾仁親王日記 |volume=巻5 |publisher=高松宮家 |year=1936 |page=316 |id={{NDLJP|1221442}}}}||三条実万の三十年祭 |- ||[[1925年]]（大正14年）5月18日{{Cite book |和書 |editor=毎日新聞社 |editor-link=毎日新聞社 |title=毎日年鑑 |volume=1926 |publisher=毎日新聞社 |year=1925 |page=144 |id={{NDLJP|2994755}}}}||[[昭和天皇|皇太子裕仁親王（後の昭和天皇）]]|| |- ||[[1928年]]（昭和3年）10月26日{{Cite book |和書 |editor=京都府 |title=昭和大礼京都府記録 |volume=上巻 |publisher=京都府 |year=1929 |page=13 |id={{NDLJP|1190228}}}}||[[閑院宮載仁親王]]||大礼使総裁として上洛 |- |}
== 境内 == * 本殿 * 中門 * 拝殿 * 神門 * 歌碑 - [[上田秋成]]、[[湯川秀樹]]。 * 愛の木 - [[カツラ (植物)|桂]]の木の葉が[[ハート (シンボル)|ハート]]形になっている為に名付けられたとされる。 * 天壌無窮の石碑 - 三條實萬が祈念していた軸の言葉を石に刻んだもの。 * 茶室 - 旧春興殿、虚中庵。 * 社務所 Nashinoki-jinja-026.jpg|一の鳥居。[[扁額]]は[[閑院宮載仁親王]]の染筆{{Cite book |和書|author=鉄道省 |title=神まうで |year=1933 |page=92 |id={{NDLJP|1187400/84}}}}。 Nashinoki-jinja-027.jpg|二の鳥居。扁額は[[久邇宮朝彦親王]]の染筆。 === 染井の水 === 境内の[[井戸]]の水は「染井の水」と呼ばれ、'''京都三名水'''の一つで、唯一現存している{{Efn2|他の二つは[[京都御苑]]の「縣井」、堀川通五条西入るにある「左女牛井」。懸井は京都御苑の西北、[[宮内庁]]京都事務所の裏側に水が枯れ、現在は[[1996年]]（平成8年）に近くで掘り直し再生。}}{{Efn2|'''京都（御所）三名水'''と混同されることがある。こちらは醒ヶ井・県井・染殿井の三つで、染殿井は、京都御苑の東北、「母と子の森」の南の京都迎賓館の裏側にある。醒ヶ井は現在は石碑のみ。[[1991年]]（平成3年）に、四条堀川付近の四条醒ヶ井角にある京菓子屋「亀屋良長」が、社屋改築の際に湧いた豊富な地下水を「{{読み仮名|醒ヶ井|さめがい}}」と名付け復活した。染井と染殿井は同一水源と思われるが異なる井戸である。}}。飲料可。[[藤原良房]]の館にあり染物に使っていた{{Cite book |和書|author=小野芳朗 |title=水の環境史「京の名水」はなぜ失われたか |publisher=[[PHP研究所]] |series=[[PHP新書]] |date=2001年5月 |page=208 |ISBN=9784569616186}}。 == 祭礼 == * 毎月1日・15日：月次祭 * 1月最終日曜（または2月第一日曜）：[[元服]]式 * 4月18日：春季例祭 * 9月中旬 - 下旬：萩祭り * 10月10日：秋季例祭 * 10月最終日曜：[[七五三]] == アクセス == * [[京都市営バス|京都市バス]]、[[京都バス]] 「府立医大病院前」停留所下車徒歩1分 == 関連図書 == * {{Cite book |和書|author=二六興信所 編纂 |author2=山田米吉 編 |title=勤王事蹟別格官幣社精史 |publisher=二六興信所 |year=1935 |pages=74-77 |chapter=梨木神社 |id={{NDLJP|1112175/48}}}} * {{Cite book |和書|author=安津素彦 |author2=梅田義彦 編集兼監修者 |title=神道辞典 |publisher=[[神社新報|神社新報社]] |year=1968 |page=43 |id={{NDLJP|2997629}}}} * {{Cite book |和書|author=白井永二 |author-link=白井永二 |author2=土岐昌訓 編 |title=神社辞典 |publisher=[[東京堂出版]] |year=1979 |page=256 |id={{NDLJP|12266197}}}} == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{Notelist2}} === 出典 === {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite book |和書 |editor=山階会 |title=山階宮三代 |volume=上 |publisher=山階会 |year=1982 |id={{NDLJP|12260162}} |ref=harv}} == 関連項目 == * [[梨木神社爆破事件]] == 外部リンク == {{commonscat|Nashinoki-jinja}} {{osm box|w|638453452}} * {{Official website}} * {{Wayback|date=20100402162411|url=http://www.kyoto-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/02/014/index.html|title=梨木神社}}（京都府神社庁・神社本庁からの脱退によりリンク切れ） * {{京都観光Navi|7|435}} * {{TripAdvisor|access-date=2021-3-24}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:なしのきしんしや}} [[Category:京都市の神社]] [[Category:別格官幣社]] [[Category:上京区の歴史]] [[Category:三条家|社なしのき]] [[Category:上京区の建築物]] [[Category:京都府の景観重要建造物]] [[Category:三条実美]] [[Category:単立神社]]

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称=梨木神社 |画像=[[画像:Nashinoki Shrine Haiden 3.jpg|250px]]
拝殿 |所在地=[[京都府]][[京都市]][[上京区]][[寺町通]][[広小路通 (京都市)|広小路]]上ル染殿町680 |位置={{Coord|35|1|30|N|135|46|2.5|E|region:JP-26:type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{Ublist| [[三条実万|三條實萬]] | [[三条実美|三條實美]] }} |社格=旧[[別格官幣社]] |創建=[[1885年]]（[[明治]]18年） |別名=萩の宮 |例祭=[[4月18日]]・[[10月10日]] |地図={{Location map|Japan Kyoto city|label=梨木神社|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|width=200|caption=}} }} '''梨木神社'''（なしのきじんじゃ）は、[[京都市]][[上京区]]染殿町にある[[神社]]。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[別格官幣社]]。[[明治維新]]に大きく貢献した[[三条実万|三條實萬]]・[[三条実美|三條實美]]父子を祭神とする。また境内には約500株の[[ハギ]]が植えられており、別名'''萩の宮'''とも呼ばれており、9月中旬から下旬には'''萩祭り'''がおこなわれる。 == 祭神 == * 主祭神 - [[三条実万|三條實萬]]、[[三条実美|三條實美]] == 歴史 == [[久邇宮朝彦親王]]の[[令旨]]により[[三条家]]の邸宅跡に[[三条実万|三條實萬]]を祀るため社殿が造営される。[[1885年]]（[[明治]]18年）10月10日、社号は地名から梨木神社とされ、さらに[[別格官幣社]]の列格を受けて創建される。その後[[1915年]]（[[大正]]4年）、[[大正天皇]]即位を記念して、子の[[三条実美|三條實美]]を合祀した。[[平安時代]]は[[藤原良房]]の娘[[藤原明子 (染殿后)|染殿后]]の里御所・染殿第であった。 [[1948年]]（[[昭和]]23年）に[[神社本庁]]の[[別表神社]]に加列されている。 1977年に反君主制主義者によって爆破された（[[梨木神社爆破事件]]）{{Cite news |和書 |title= |newspaper=[[京都新聞]] |date=1977-01-03 |edition=朝刊 |page=}}{{Cite news |和書 |title= |newspaper=京都新聞 |date=1977-01-13 |edition=朝刊 |page=}}。 社殿の修復等の資金集めに苦慮していた[[2013年]]（[[平成]]25年）、境内の参道を含む土地を[[マンション]]開発業者に60年の[[定期借地権]]で貸し、その賃貸料を社殿の修復費用に充てることとしたが、その計画が神社本庁の承認を得られなかったことから神社本庁から離脱して独立し、[[単立]]神社となった。これにより、別表神社の列から当社は外された。 神社本庁の別表神社が神社本庁から離脱したのは[[気多大社]]以来のこととなる。
皇室の参拝歴
{| class="wikitable" style="font-size:small;" !年月日!!参拝者!!備考 |- ||[[1885年]]（明治18年）10月6日{{sfn|山階会|1982|p=510}}||[[山階宮晃親王]]||神霊鎮座祭 |- ||1885年（明治18年）11月15日{{sfn|山階会|1982|p=512}}||山階宮晃親王||[[国分貞胤]]宅の茶事の帰路 |- ||[[1886年]]（明治19年）4月15日{{sfn|山階会|1982|p=520}}||山階宮晃親王|| |- ||1887年（明治20年）2月2日{{sfn|山階会|1982|p=534}}||山階宮晃親王||[[京都御所]]参内の途次 |- ||[[1887年]]（明治20年）1月23日{{Cite book |和書 |editor=高松宮家 |title=熾仁親王日記 |volume=巻4 |publisher=高松宮家 |year=1936 |page=461 |id={{NDLJP|1221430}}}}||[[有栖川宮熾仁親王]]|| |- ||1887年（明治20年）10月10日{{sfn|山階会|1982|p=545}}||山階宮晃親王|| |- ||[[1890年]]（明治23年）4月16日||山階宮晃親王{{sfn|山階会|1982|p=614}}、有栖川宮熾仁親王{{Cite book |和書 |editor=高松宮家 |title=熾仁親王日記 |volume=巻5 |publisher=高松宮家 |year=1936 |page=316 |id={{NDLJP|1221442}}}}||三条実万の三十年祭 |- ||[[1925年]]（大正14年）5月18日{{Cite book |和書 |editor=毎日新聞社 |editor-link=毎日新聞社 |title=毎日年鑑 |volume=1926 |publisher=毎日新聞社 |year=1925 |page=144 |id={{NDLJP|2994755}}}}||[[昭和天皇|皇太子裕仁親王（後の昭和天皇）]]|| |- ||[[1928年]]（昭和3年）10月26日{{Cite book |和書 |editor=京都府 |title=昭和大礼京都府記録 |volume=上巻 |publisher=京都府 |year=1929 |page=13 |id={{NDLJP|1190228}}}}||[[閑院宮載仁親王]]||大礼使総裁として上洛 |- |}
== 境内 == * 本殿 * 中門 * 拝殿 * 神門 * 歌碑 - [[上田秋成]]、[[湯川秀樹]]。 * 愛の木 - [[カツラ (植物)|桂]]の木の葉が[[ハート (シンボル)|ハート]]形になっている為に名付けられたとされる。 * 天壌無窮の石碑 - 三條實萬が祈念していた軸の言葉を石に刻んだもの。 * 茶室 - 旧春興殿、虚中庵。 * 社務所 Nashinoki-jinja-026.jpg|一の鳥居。[[扁額]]は[[閑院宮載仁親王]]の染筆{{Cite book |和書|author=鉄道省 |title=神まうで |year=1933 |page=92 |id={{NDLJP|1187400/84}}}}。 Nashinoki-jinja-027.jpg|二の鳥居。扁額は[[久邇宮朝彦親王]]の染筆。 === 染井の水 === 境内の[[井戸]]の水は「染井の水」と呼ばれ、'''京都三名水'''の一つで、唯一現存している{{Efn2|他の二つは[[京都御苑]]の「縣井」、堀川通五条西入るにある「左女牛井」。懸井は京都御苑の西北、[[宮内庁]]京都事務所の裏側に水が枯れ、現在は[[1996年]]（平成8年）に近くで掘り直し再生。}}{{Efn2|'''京都（御所）三名水'''と混同されることがある。こちらは醒ヶ井・県井・染殿井の三つで、染殿井は、京都御苑の東北、「母と子の森」の南の京都迎賓館の裏側にある。醒ヶ井は現在は石碑のみ。[[1991年]]（平成3年）に、四条堀川付近の四条醒ヶ井角にある京菓子屋「亀屋良長」が、社屋改築の際に湧いた豊富な地下水を「{{読み仮名|醒ヶ井|さめがい}}」と名付け復活した。染井と染殿井は同一水源と思われるが異なる井戸である。}}。飲料可。[[藤原良房]]の館にあり染物に使っていた{{Cite book |和書|author=小野芳朗 |title=水の環境史「京の名水」はなぜ失われたか |publisher=[[PHP研究所]] |series=[[PHP新書]] |date=2001年5月 |page=208 |ISBN=9784569616186}}。 == 祭礼 == * 毎月1日・15日：月次祭 * 1月最終日曜（または2月第一日曜）：[[元服]]式 * 4月18日：春季例祭 * 9月中旬 - 下旬：萩祭り * 10月10日：秋季例祭 * 10月最終日曜：[[七五三]] == アクセス == * [[京都市営バス|京都市バス]]、[[京都バス]] 「府立医大病院前」停留所下車徒歩1分 == 関連図書 == * {{Cite book |和書|author=二六興信所 編纂 |author2=山田米吉 編 |title=勤王事蹟別格官幣社精史 |publisher=二六興信所 |year=1935 |pages=74-77 |chapter=梨木神社 |id={{NDLJP|1112175/48}}}} * {{Cite book |和書|author=安津素彦 |author2=梅田義彦 編集兼監修者 |title=神道辞典 |publisher=[[神社新報|神社新報社]] |year=1968 |page=43 |id={{NDLJP|2997629}}}} * {{Cite book |和書|author=白井永二 |author-link=白井永二 |author2=土岐昌訓 編 |title=神社辞典 |publisher=[[東京堂出版]] |year=1979 |page=256 |id={{NDLJP|12266197}}}} == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{Notelist2}} === 出典 === {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite book |和書 |editor=山階会 |title=山階宮三代 |volume=上 |publisher=山階会 |year=1982 |id={{NDLJP|12260162}} |ref=harv}} == 関連項目 == * [[梨木神社爆破事件]] == 外部リンク == {{commonscat|Nashinoki-jinja}} {{osm box|w|638453452}} * {{Official website}} * {{Wayback|date=20100402162411|url=http://www.kyoto-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/02/014/index.html|title=梨木神社}}（京都府神社庁・神社本庁からの脱退によりリンク切れ） * {{京都観光Navi|7|435}} * {{TripAdvisor|access-date=2021-3-24}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:なしのきしんしや}} [[Category:京都市の神社]] [[Category:別格官幣社]] [[Category:上京区の歴史]] [[Category:三条家|社なしのき]] [[Category:上京区の建築物]] [[Category:京都府の景観重要建造物]] [[Category:三条実美]] [[Category:単立神社]]

[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}}{| class="infobox vcard" style="border-spacing:2px;" ! colspan="2" class="infobox-above" style="background-color:" |Nashinoki Shrine |- | colspan="2" class="infobox-image" |[[File:Nashinoki Shrine Haiden 3.jpg|frameless]] |} Nashinoki Shrine (梨木神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] in {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|en}}. Although it was never a [[Beppyo shrine]], it used to have the rank of Bekkaku kanpeisha under the modern system of ranked Shinto shrines.
Nashinoki Shrine is a relatively new Shinto shrine in Kyoto. It was built in 1885.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Fushimi Inari Taisha {{!}} Location {{!}} Shajiraku – Quality traditional food and cultural experiences at Kyoto shrines and temples.|url=https://www.shajiraku.jp/en/plan/location_detail.php?id=2|access-date=2023-04-07|website=社寺楽 Shajiraku|language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|date=2020-10-04|title=Nashinoki Shrine Kyoto, a Bush Clover Shrine {{!}} All About Kyoto|url=https://allaboutkyoto.com/en/nashinoki-shrine-kyoto/|access-date=2023-04-07|language=en-US|archive-date=2023-04-07|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230407205909/https://allaboutkyoto.com/en/nashinoki-shrine-kyoto/|url-status=dead}}</ref>
The Nashinoki Shrine hosts an annual {{ill|Bush clover|en}} festival.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Hagi Matsuri|url=https://www.discoverkyoto.com/event-calendar/september/hagi-matsuri-nashinoki-shrine/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Discover Kyoto|language=en}}</ref>
== Bombing == The '''Nashinoki Shrine Bombing Incident''' (なしのきじんじゃばくはじけん) was a [[1977]] ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 52) guerrilla bombing that took place on [[January 1]] in {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}'s {{ill|Kamigyo Ward|ja|上京区}}, at {{ill|Nashinoki Shrine|ja|梨木神社}}. It was perpetrated by {{ill|Saburō Katō (New Left)|ja|加藤三郎 (新左翼)}} of the {{ill|Japanese New Left|ja|日本の新左翼}}.
=== History of the Damaged Structure === Nashinoki Shrine was established in [[1885]] based on a decree from {{ill|Prince Kuni Asahiko|ja|久邇宮朝彦親王}}, on the site of the former Sanjo residence, enshrining {{ill|Sanetomi Sanjo|ja|三条実万}}, who contributed to the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}. Later, his son, {{ill|Sanjō Sanetomi|ja|三条実美}}, was also enshrined there. Katō criticized Sanjō Sanetomi as "the highest authority ({{ill|Daijō Daijin|ja|太政大臣}}) of the Meiji government, which became the bearer of {{ill|Japanese imperialism|ja|日本帝国主義}}," and proceeded to bomb Nashinoki Shrine, which enshrines him.
=== Overview of the Incident === On January 1, [[1977]] ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 52) at around 8:45 PM, an [[:en:Improvised explosive device|Improvised explosive device]] installed by Katō exploded in the main hall of Nashinoki Shrine, causing a fire. Thanks to the immediate firefighting efforts by the {{ill|guji|ja|宮司}} and {{ill|negi|ja|禰宜}}, the damage was limited to part of the main hall being scorched. Although it was New Year's Day, there were no worshippers present as it was nighttime, and no injuries were reported. Subsequent investigation found fragments of a fire extinguisher, confirming the incident as a bombing with a fire extinguisher bomb.
On January 5, Katō sent a {{ill|claim of responsibility|ja|犯行声明}} to the media under the names "Yami no Tsuchigumo" and "Uke Hime no Kodomotachi." The statement mentioned the "three crimes of Sanjō Sanetomi (establishing the exploitative and oppressive Meiji government, invading other peoples, and suppressing anti-establishment movements)," sentencing him to death by bombing. It is said that the inclusion of a non-terrorist sounding name like "Uke Hime no Kodomotachi" was intended as an antithesis to the "paternalistic {{ill|Emperor system|ja|天皇制}}."
== See Also == * {{ill|Theory of Japanese Indigenous People|ja|日本原住民論}} * {{ill|Anti-Japaneseism|ja|反日亡国論}} * {{ill|Anti-monarchism in Japan|ja|反天皇制}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nashinoki Shrine Bombing Incident}} [[Category:Explosions_in_Japan]] [[Category:Terrorist incidents in Japan]] [[Category:Religious terrorism]] [[Category:Incidents involving the Japanese New Left]] [[Category:Shinto history incidents]] [[Category:History of Kamigyo-ku]] [[Category:1977 crimes in Japan]] [[Category:January 1977 events in Asia]] [[Category:Shōwa period postwar Kyoto]]
== References == * {{ill|Kyoto Shimbun|ja|京都新聞}}, January 3, 1977 morning edition, January 13, 1977 morning edition * Saburō Katō, "Opinion Paper 'From the Pigs of the Earth to You'" {{ill|The Science of Thought Society|ja|思想の科学社}}, 1992

=== Overview of the Incident === On January 1, [[1977]] ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 52) at around 8:45 PM, an [[:en:Improvised explosive device|Improvised explosive device]] installed by Katō exploded in the main hall of Nashinoki Shrine, causing a fire. On January 5, Katō sent a {{ill|claim of responsibility|ja|犯行声明}} to the media under the names "Yami no Tsuchigumo" and "Uke Hime no Kodomotachi." The statement mentioned the "three crimes of Sanjō Sanetomi (establishing the exploitative and oppressive Meiji government, invading other peoples, and suppressing anti-establishment movements)," sentencing him to death by bombing. It is said that the inclusion of a non-terrorist sounding name like "Uke Hime no Kodomotachi" was intended as an antithesis to the "paternalistic {{ill|Emperor system|ja|天皇制}}."

The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here [[every:Nashinoki Shrine|Nashinoki Shrine]] and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Nashinoki Shrine]][[en:draft:Nashinoki Shrine]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}}{| class="infobox vcard" style="border-spacing:2px;" ! colspan="2" class="infobox-above" style="background-color:" |Nashinoki Shrine |- | colspan="2" class="infobox-image" |[[File:Nashinoki Shrine Haiden 3.jpg|frameless]] |} Nashinoki Shrine (梨木神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] in {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|en}}. Although it was never a [[Beppyo shrine]], it used to have the rank of Bekkaku kanpeisha under the modern system of ranked Shinto shrines.
Nashinoki Shrine is a relatively new Shinto shrine in Kyoto. It was built in 1885.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Fushimi Inari Taisha {{!}} Location {{!}} Shajiraku – Quality traditional food and cultural experiences at Kyoto shrines and temples.|url=https://www.shajiraku.jp/en/plan/location_detail.php?id=2|access-date=2023-04-07|website=社寺楽 Shajiraku|language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|date=2020-10-04|title=Nashinoki Shrine Kyoto, a Bush Clover Shrine {{!}} All About Kyoto|url=https://allaboutkyoto.com/en/nashinoki-shrine-kyoto/|access-date=2023-04-07|language=en-US|archive-date=2023-04-07|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230407205909/https://allaboutkyoto.com/en/nashinoki-shrine-kyoto/|url-status=dead}}</ref>
The Nashinoki Shrine hosts an annual {{ill|Bush clover|en}} festival.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Hagi Matsuri|url=https://www.discoverkyoto.com/event-calendar/september/hagi-matsuri-nashinoki-shrine/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Discover Kyoto|language=en}}</ref>
== Bombing == The '''Nashinoki Shrine Bombing Incident''' (なしのきじんじゃばくはじけん) was a [[1977]] ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 52) guerrilla bombing that took place on [[January 1]] in {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}'s {{ill|Kamigyo Ward|ja|上京区}}, at {{ill|Nashinoki Shrine|ja|梨木神社}}. It was perpetrated by {{ill|Saburō Katō (New Left)|ja|加藤三郎 (新左翼)}} of the {{ill|Japanese New Left|ja|日本の新左翼}}.
=== History of the Damaged Structure === Nashinoki Shrine was established in [[1885]] based on a decree from {{ill|Prince Kuni Asahiko|ja|久邇宮朝彦親王}}, on the site of the former Sanjo residence, enshrining {{ill|Sanetomi Sanjo|ja|三条実万}}, who contributed to the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}. Later, his son, {{ill|Sanjō Sanetomi|ja|三条実美}}, was also enshrined there. Katō criticized Sanjō Sanetomi as "the highest authority ({{ill|Daijō Daijin|ja|太政大臣}}) of the Meiji government, which became the bearer of {{ill|Japanese imperialism|ja|日本帝国主義}}," and proceeded to bomb Nashinoki Shrine, which enshrines him.
=== Overview of the Incident === On January 1, [[1977]] ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 52) at around 8:45 PM, an [[:en:Improvised explosive device|Improvised explosive device]] installed by Katō exploded in the main hall of Nashinoki Shrine, causing a fire. Thanks to the immediate firefighting efforts by the {{ill|guji|ja|宮司}} and {{ill|negi|ja|禰宜}}, the damage was limited to part of the main hall being scorched. Although it was New Year's Day, there were no worshippers present as it was nighttime, and no injuries were reported. Subsequent investigation found fragments of a fire extinguisher, confirming the incident as a bombing with a fire extinguisher bomb.
On January 5, Katō sent a {{ill|claim of responsibility|ja|犯行声明}} to the media under the names "Yami no Tsuchigumo" and "Uke Hime no Kodomotachi." The statement mentioned the "three crimes of Sanjō Sanetomi (establishing the exploitative and oppressive Meiji government, invading other peoples, and suppressing anti-establishment movements)," sentencing him to death by bombing. It is said that the inclusion of a non-terrorist sounding name like "Uke Hime no Kodomotachi" was intended as an antithesis to the "paternalistic {{ill|Emperor system|ja|天皇制}}."
== See Also == * {{ill|Theory of Japanese Indigenous People|ja|日本原住民論}} * {{ill|Anti-Japaneseism|ja|反日亡国論}} * {{ill|Anti-monarchism in Japan|ja|反天皇制}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nashinoki Shrine Bombing Incident}} [[Category:Explosions_in_Japan]] [[Category:Terrorist incidents in Japan]] [[Category:Religious terrorism]] [[Category:Incidents involving the Japanese New Left]] [[Category:Shinto history incidents]] [[Category:History of Kamigyo-ku]] [[Category:1977 crimes in Japan]] [[Category:January 1977 events in Asia]] [[Category:Shōwa period postwar Kyoto]]

Although it was New Year's Day, there were no worshippers present as it was nighttime, and no injuries were reported. Subsequent investigation found fragments of a fire extinguisher, confirming the incident as a bombing with a fire extinguisher bomb.
On January 5, Katō sent a {{ill|claim of responsibility|ja|犯行声明}} to the media under the names "Yami no Tsuchigumo" and "Uke Hime no Kodomotachi." The statement mentioned the "three crimes of Sanjō Sanetomi (establishing the exploitative and oppressive Meiji government, invading other peoples, and suppressing anti-establishment movements)," sentencing him to death by bombing. It is said that the inclusion of a non-terrorist sounding name like "Uke Hime no Kodomotachi" was intended as an antithesis to the "paternalistic {{ill|Emperor system|ja|天皇制}}."
== See Also == * {{ill|Theory of Japanese Indigenous People|ja|日本原住民論}} * {{ill|Anti-Japaneseism|ja|反日亡国論}} * {{ill|Anti-monarchism in Japan|ja|反天皇制}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nashinoki Shrine Bombing Incident}} [[Category:Explosions_in_Japan]] [[Category:Terrorist incidents in Japan]] [[Category:Religious terrorism]] [[Category:Incidents involving the Japanese New Left]] [[Category:Shinto history incidents]] [[Category:History of Kamigyo-ku]] [[Category:1977 crimes in Japan]] [[Category:January 1977 events in Asia]] [[Category:Shōwa period postwar Kyoto]]
== References == * {{ill|Kyoto Shimbun|ja|京都新聞}}, January 3, 1977 morning edition, January 13, 1977 morning edition * Saburō Katō, "Opinion Paper 'From the Pigs of the Earth to You'" {{ill|The Science of Thought Society|ja|思想の科学社}}, 1992
== References == {{Reflist}} [[Category:Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] [[Category:Nashinoki Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted]][[Category:Completion tier 1]][[Category:tier 2]][[Category:tier 1]][[Category: Completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Nashinoki Shrine-qq]] [[simple:Nashinoki Shrine]][[Category:Nashinoki Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted]][[Category:Completion tier 1]][[Category:tier 2]][[Category:tier 1]][[Category: Completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Nashinoki Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Nashinoki Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted]][[Category:Completion tier 1]][[Category:tier 2]][[Category:tier 1]][[Category: Completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Nashinoki Shrine-qq]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Nashinoki Shrine]][[Category:Nashinoki Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted]][[Category:Completion tier 1]][[Category:tier 2]][[Category:tier 1]][[Category: Completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Nashinoki Shrine-qq]] [[en:Nashinoki Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[en:Nashinoki Shrine]][[en:Nashinoki Shrine]][[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Nashinoki Shrine]][[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Nashinoki Shrine]][[simple:Nashinoki Shrine]]

{{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here [[every:Nashinoki Shrine|Nashinoki Shrine]] and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Nashinoki Shrine]][[en:draft:Nashinoki Shrine]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:36, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here [[every:Nashinoki Shrine|Nashinoki Shrine]] and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Nashinoki Shrine]][[en:draft:Nashinoki Shrine]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}}{| class="infobox vcard" style="border-spacing:2px;" ! colspan="2" class="infobox-above" style="background-color:" |Nashinoki Shrine |- | colspan="2" class="infobox-image" |[[File:Nashinoki Shrine Haiden 3.jpg|frameless]] |} Nashinoki Shrine (梨木神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] in {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|en}}. Although it was never a [[Beppyo shrine]], it used to have the rank of Bekkaku kanpeisha under the modern system of ranked Shinto shrines.
Nashinoki Shrine is a relatively new Shinto shrine in Kyoto. It was built in 1885.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Fushimi Inari Taisha {{!}} Location {{!}} Shajiraku – Quality traditional food and cultural experiences at Kyoto shrines and temples.|url=https://www.shajiraku.jp/en/plan/location_detail.php?id=2|access-date=2023-04-07|website=社寺楽 Shajiraku|language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|date=2020-10-04|title=Nashinoki Shrine Kyoto, a Bush Clover Shrine {{!}} All About Kyoto|url=https://allaboutkyoto.com/en/nashinoki-shrine-kyoto/|access-date=2023-04-07|language=en-US|archive-date=2023-04-07|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230407205909/https://allaboutkyoto.com/en/nashinoki-shrine-kyoto/|url-status=dead}}</ref>
The Nashinoki Shrine hosts an annual {{ill|Bush clover|en}} festival.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Hagi Matsuri|url=https://www.discoverkyoto.com/event-calendar/september/hagi-matsuri-nashinoki-shrine/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Discover Kyoto|language=en}}</ref>
== Bombing == The '''Nashinoki Shrine Bombing Incident''' (なしのきじんじゃばくはじけん) was a [[1977]] ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 52) guerrilla bombing that took place on [[January 1]] in {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}'s {{ill|Kamigyo Ward|ja|上京区}}, at {{ill|Nashinoki Shrine|ja|梨木神社}}. It was perpetrated by {{ill|Saburō Katō (New Left)|ja|加藤三郎 (新左翼)}} of the {{ill|Japanese New Left|ja|日本の新左翼}}.
=== History of the Damaged Structure === Nashinoki Shrine was established in [[1885]] based on a decree from {{ill|Prince Kuni Asahiko|ja|久邇宮朝彦親王}}, on the site of the former Sanjo residence, enshrining {{ill|Sanetomi Sanjo|ja|三条実万}}, who contributed to the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}. Later, his son, {{ill|Sanjō Sanetomi|ja|三条実美}}, was also enshrined there. Katō criticized Sanjō Sanetomi as "the highest authority ({{ill|Daijō Daijin|ja|太政大臣}}) of the Meiji government, which became the bearer of {{ill|Japanese imperialism|ja|日本帝国主義}}," and proceeded to bomb Nashinoki Shrine, which enshrines him.
=== Overview of the Incident === On January 1, [[1977]] ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 52) at around 8:45 PM, an [[:en:Improvised explosive device|Improvised explosive device]] installed by Katō exploded in the main hall of Nashinoki Shrine, causing a fire. Thanks to the immediate firefighting efforts by the {{ill|guji|ja|宮司}} and {{ill|negi|ja|禰宜}}, the damage was limited to part of the main hall being scorched. Although it was New Year's Day, there were no worshippers present as it was nighttime, and no injuries were reported. Subsequent investigation found fragments of a fire extinguisher, confirming the incident as a bombing with a fire extinguisher bomb.
On January 5, Katō sent a {{ill|claim of responsibility|ja|犯行声明}} to the media under the names "Yami no Tsuchigumo" and "Uke Hime no Kodomotachi." The statement mentioned the "three crimes of Sanjō Sanetomi (establishing the exploitative and oppressive Meiji government, invading other peoples, and suppressing anti-establishment movements)," sentencing him to death by bombing. It is said that the inclusion of a non-terrorist sounding name like "Uke Hime no Kodomotachi" was intended as an antithesis to the "paternalistic {{ill|Emperor system|ja|天皇制}}."
== See Also == * {{ill|Theory of Japanese Indigenous People|ja|日本原住民論}} * {{ill|Anti-Japaneseism|ja|反日亡国論}} * {{ill|Anti-monarchism in Japan|ja|反天皇制}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nashinoki Shrine Bombing Incident}} [[Category:Explosions_in_Japan]] [[Category:Terrorist incidents in Japan]] [[Category:Religious terrorism]] [[Category:Incidents involving the Japanese New Left]] [[Category:Shinto history incidents]] [[Category:History of Kamigyo-ku]] [[Category:1977 crimes in Japan]] [[Category:January 1977 events in Asia]] [[Category:Shōwa period postwar Kyoto]]
== References == * {{ill|Kyoto Shimbun|ja|京都新聞}}, January 3, 1977 morning edition, January 13, 1977 morning edition * Saburō Katō, "Opinion Paper 'From the Pigs of the Earth to You'" {{ill|The Science of Thought Society|ja|思想の科学社}}, 1992

It was built in 1885.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Fushimi Inari Taisha {{!}} Location {{!}} Shajiraku – Quality traditional food and cultural experiences at Kyoto shrines and temples.|url=https://www.shajiraku.jp/en/plan/location_detail.php?id=2|access-date=2023-04-07|website=社寺楽 Shajiraku|language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|date=2020-10-04|title=Nashinoki Shrine Kyoto, a Bush Clover Shrine {{!}} All About Kyoto|url=https://allaboutkyoto.com/en/nashinoki-shrine-kyoto/|access-date=2023-04-07|language=en-US|archive-date=2023-04-07|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230407205909/https://allaboutkyoto.com/en/nashinoki-shrine-kyoto/|url-status=dead}}</ref>

== Bombing == The '''Nashinoki Shrine Bombing Incident''' (なしのきじんじゃばくはじけん) was a [[1977]] ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 52) guerrilla bombing that took place on [[January 1]] in {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}'s {{ill|Kamigyo Ward|ja|上京区}}, at {{ill|Nashinoki Shrine|ja|梨木神社}}. It was perpetrated by {{ill|Saburō Katō (New Left)|ja|加藤三郎 (新左翼)}} of the {{ill|Japanese New Left|ja|日本の新左翼}}.
=== History of the Damaged Structure === Nashinoki Shrine was established in [[1885]] based on a decree from {{ill|Prince Kuni Asahiko|ja|久邇宮朝彦親王}}, on the site of the former Sanjo residence, enshrining {{ill|Sanetomi Sanjo|ja|三条実万}}, who contributed to the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}. Later, his son, {{ill|Sanjō Sanetomi|ja|三条実美}}, was also enshrined there. Katō criticized Sanjō Sanetomi as "the highest authority ({{ill|Daijō Daijin|ja|太政大臣}}) of the Meiji government, which became the bearer of {{ill|Japanese imperialism|ja|日本帝国主義}}," and proceeded to bomb Nashinoki Shrine, which enshrines him.
=== Overview of the Incident === On January 1, [[1977]] ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 52) at around 8:45 PM, an [[:en:Improvised explosive device|Improvised explosive device]] installed by Katō exploded in the main hall of Nashinoki Shrine, causing a fire. Thanks to the immediate firefighting efforts by the {{ill|guji|ja|宮司}} and {{ill|negi|ja|禰宜}}, the damage was limited to part of the main hall being scorched. Although it was New Year's Day, there were no worshippers present as it was nighttime, and no injuries were reported. Subsequent investigation found fragments of a fire extinguisher, confirming the incident as a bombing with a fire extinguisher bomb.
On January 5, Katō sent a {{ill|claim of responsibility|ja|犯行声明}} to the media under the names "Yami no Tsuchigumo" and "Uke Hime no Kodomotachi." The statement mentioned the "three crimes of Sanjō Sanetomi (establishing the exploitative and oppressive Meiji government, invading other peoples, and suppressing anti-establishment movements)," sentencing him to death by bombing. It is said that the inclusion of a non-terrorist sounding name like "Uke Hime no Kodomotachi" was intended as an antithesis to the "paternalistic {{ill|Emperor system|ja|天皇制}}."
== See Also == * {{ill|Theory of Japanese Indigenous People|ja|日本原住民論}} * {{ill|Anti-Japaneseism|ja|反日亡国論}} * {{ill|Anti-monarchism in Japan|ja|反天皇制}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nashinoki Shrine Bombing Incident}} [[Category:Explosions_in_Japan]] [[Category:Terrorist incidents in Japan]] [[Category:Religious terrorism]] [[Category:Incidents involving the Japanese New Left]] [[Category:Shinto history incidents]] [[Category:History of Kamigyo-ku]] [[Category:1977 crimes in Japan]] [[Category:January 1977 events in Asia]] [[Category:Shōwa period postwar Kyoto]]

Although it was never a [[Beppyo shrine]], it used to have the rank of Bekkaku kanpeisha under the modern system of ranked Shinto shrines.
Nashinoki Shrine is a relatively new Shinto shrine in Kyoto. It was built in 1885.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Fushimi Inari Taisha {{!}} Location {{!}} Shajiraku – Quality traditional food and cultural experiences at Kyoto shrines and temples.|url=https://www.shajiraku.jp/en/plan/location_detail.php?id=2|access-date=2023-04-07|website=社寺楽 Shajiraku|language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|date=2020-10-04|title=Nashinoki Shrine Kyoto, a Bush Clover Shrine {{!}} All About Kyoto|url=https://allaboutkyoto.com/en/nashinoki-shrine-kyoto/|access-date=2023-04-07|language=en-US|archive-date=2023-04-07|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230407205909/https://allaboutkyoto.com/en/nashinoki-shrine-kyoto/|url-status=dead}}</ref>
The Nashinoki Shrine hosts an annual {{ill|Bush clover|en}} festival.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Hagi Matsuri|url=https://www.discoverkyoto.com/event-calendar/september/hagi-matsuri-nashinoki-shrine/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Discover Kyoto|language=en}}</ref>
