[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:36, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here [[every:Nashinoki Shrine|Nashinoki Shrine]] and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Nashinoki Shrine]][[en:draft:Nashinoki Shrine]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}}{| class="infobox vcard" style="border-spacing:2px;" ! colspan="2" class="infobox-above" style="background-color:" |Nashinoki Shrine |- | colspan="2" class="infobox-image" |[[File:Nashinoki Shrine Haiden 3.jpg|frameless]] |} Nashinoki Shrine (梨木神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] in {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|en}}. Although it was never a [[Beppyo shrine]], it used to have the rank of Bekkaku kanpeisha under the modern system of ranked Shinto shrines.
Nashinoki Shrine is a relatively new Shinto shrine in Kyoto. It was built in 1885.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Fushimi Inari Taisha {{!}} Location {{!}} Shajiraku – Quality traditional food and cultural experiences at Kyoto shrines and temples.|url=https://www.shajiraku.jp/en/plan/location_detail.php?id=2|access-date=2023-04-07|website=社寺楽 Shajiraku|language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|date=2020-10-04|title=Nashinoki Shrine Kyoto, a Bush Clover Shrine {{!}} All About Kyoto|url=https://allaboutkyoto.com/en/nashinoki-shrine-kyoto/|access-date=2023-04-07|language=en-US|archive-date=2023-04-07|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230407205909/https://allaboutkyoto.com/en/nashinoki-shrine-kyoto/|url-status=dead}}</ref>
The Nashinoki Shrine hosts an annual {{ill|Bush clover|en}} festival.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Hagi Matsuri|url=https://www.discoverkyoto.com/event-calendar/september/hagi-matsuri-nashinoki-shrine/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Discover Kyoto|language=en}}</ref>
== Bombing == The '''Nashinoki Shrine Bombing Incident''' (なしのきじんじゃばくはじけん) was a [[1977]] ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 52) guerrilla bombing that took place on [[January 1]] in {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}'s {{ill|Kamigyo Ward|ja|上京区}}, at {{ill|Nashinoki Shrine|ja|梨木神社}}. It was perpetrated by {{ill|Saburō Katō (New Left)|ja|加藤三郎 (新左翼)}} of the {{ill|Japanese New Left|ja|日本の新左翼}}.
=== History of the Damaged Structure === Nashinoki Shrine was established in [[1885]] based on a decree from {{ill|Prince Kuni Asahiko|ja|久邇宮朝彦親王}}, on the site of the former Sanjo residence, enshrining {{ill|Sanetomi Sanjo|ja|三条実万}}, who contributed to the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}. Later, his son, {{ill|Sanjō Sanetomi|ja|三条実美}}, was also enshrined there. Katō criticized Sanjō Sanetomi as "the highest authority ({{ill|Daijō Daijin|ja|太政大臣}}) of the Meiji government, which became the bearer of {{ill|Japanese imperialism|ja|日本帝国主義}}," and proceeded to bomb Nashinoki Shrine, which enshrines him.
=== Overview of the Incident === On January 1, [[1977]] ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 52) at around 8:45 PM, an [[:en:Improvised explosive device|Improvised explosive device]] installed by Katō exploded in the main hall of Nashinoki Shrine, causing a fire. Thanks to the immediate firefighting efforts by the {{ill|guji|ja|宮司}} and {{ill|negi|ja|禰宜}}, the damage was limited to part of the main hall being scorched. Although it was New Year's Day, there were no worshippers present as it was nighttime, and no injuries were reported. Subsequent investigation found fragments of a fire extinguisher, confirming the incident as a bombing with a fire extinguisher bomb.
On January 5, Katō sent a {{ill|claim of responsibility|ja|犯行声明}} to the media under the names "Yami no Tsuchigumo" and "Uke Hime no Kodomotachi." The statement mentioned the "three crimes of Sanjō Sanetomi (establishing the exploitative and oppressive Meiji government, invading other peoples, and suppressing anti-establishment movements)," sentencing him to death by bombing. It is said that the inclusion of a non-terrorist sounding name like "Uke Hime no Kodomotachi" was intended as an antithesis to the "paternalistic {{ill|Emperor system|ja|天皇制}}."
== See Also == * {{ill|Theory of Japanese Indigenous People|ja|日本原住民論}} * {{ill|Anti-Japaneseism|ja|反日亡国論}} * {{ill|Anti-monarchism in Japan|ja|反天皇制}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nashinoki Shrine Bombing Incident}} [[Category:Explosions_in_Japan]] [[Category:Terrorist incidents in Japan]] [[Category:Religious terrorism]] [[Category:Incidents involving the Japanese New Left]] [[Category:Shinto history incidents]] [[Category:History of Kamigyo-ku]] [[Category:1977 crimes in Japan]] [[Category:January 1977 events in Asia]] [[Category:Shōwa period postwar Kyoto]]
== References == * {{ill|Kyoto Shimbun|ja|京都新聞}}, January 3, 1977 morning edition, January 13, 1977 morning edition * Saburō Katō, "Opinion Paper 'From the Pigs of the Earth to You'" {{ill|The Science of Thought Society|ja|思想の科学社}}, 1992

It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here [[every:Nashinoki Shrine|Nashinoki Shrine]] and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Nashinoki Shrine]][[en:draft:Nashinoki Shrine]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}}{| class="infobox vcard" style="border-spacing:2px;" ! colspan="2" class="infobox-above" style="background-color:" |Nashinoki Shrine |- | colspan="2" class="infobox-image" |[[File:Nashinoki Shrine Haiden 3.jpg|frameless]] |} Nashinoki Shrine (梨木神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] in {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|en}}. Although it was never a [[Beppyo shrine]], it used to have the rank of Bekkaku kanpeisha under the modern system of ranked Shinto shrines.
Nashinoki Shrine is a relatively new Shinto shrine in Kyoto. It was built in 1885.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Fushimi Inari Taisha {{!}} Location {{!}} Shajiraku – Quality traditional food and cultural experiences at Kyoto shrines and temples.|url=https://www.shajiraku.jp/en/plan/location_detail.php?id=2|access-date=2023-04-07|website=社寺楽 Shajiraku|language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|date=2020-10-04|title=Nashinoki Shrine Kyoto, a Bush Clover Shrine {{!}} All About Kyoto|url=https://allaboutkyoto.com/en/nashinoki-shrine-kyoto/|access-date=2023-04-07|language=en-US|archive-date=2023-04-07|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230407205909/https://allaboutkyoto.com/en/nashinoki-shrine-kyoto/|url-status=dead}}</ref>
The Nashinoki Shrine hosts an annual {{ill|Bush clover|en}} festival.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Hagi Matsuri|url=https://www.discoverkyoto.com/event-calendar/september/hagi-matsuri-nashinoki-shrine/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Discover Kyoto|language=en}}</ref>
== Bombing == The '''Nashinoki Shrine Bombing Incident''' (なしのきじんじゃばくはじけん) was a [[1977]] ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 52) guerrilla bombing that took place on [[January 1]] in {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}'s {{ill|Kamigyo Ward|ja|上京区}}, at {{ill|Nashinoki Shrine|ja|梨木神社}}. It was perpetrated by {{ill|Saburō Katō (New Left)|ja|加藤三郎 (新左翼)}} of the {{ill|Japanese New Left|ja|日本の新左翼}}.
=== History of the Damaged Structure === Nashinoki Shrine was established in [[1885]] based on a decree from {{ill|Prince Kuni Asahiko|ja|久邇宮朝彦親王}}, on the site of the former Sanjo residence, enshrining {{ill|Sanetomi Sanjo|ja|三条実万}}, who contributed to the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}. Later, his son, {{ill|Sanjō Sanetomi|ja|三条実美}}, was also enshrined there. Katō criticized Sanjō Sanetomi as "the highest authority ({{ill|Daijō Daijin|ja|太政大臣}}) of the Meiji government, which became the bearer of {{ill|Japanese imperialism|ja|日本帝国主義}}," and proceeded to bomb Nashinoki Shrine, which enshrines him.
=== Overview of the Incident === On January 1, [[1977]] ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 52) at around 8:45 PM, an [[:en:Improvised explosive device|Improvised explosive device]] installed by Katō exploded in the main hall of Nashinoki Shrine, causing a fire. Thanks to the immediate firefighting efforts by the {{ill|guji|ja|宮司}} and {{ill|negi|ja|禰宜}}, the damage was limited to part of the main hall being scorched. Although it was New Year's Day, there were no worshippers present as it was nighttime, and no injuries were reported. Subsequent investigation found fragments of a fire extinguisher, confirming the incident as a bombing with a fire extinguisher bomb.
On January 5, Katō sent a {{ill|claim of responsibility|ja|犯行声明}} to the media under the names "Yami no Tsuchigumo" and "Uke Hime no Kodomotachi." The statement mentioned the "three crimes of Sanjō Sanetomi (establishing the exploitative and oppressive Meiji government, invading other peoples, and suppressing anti-establishment movements)," sentencing him to death by bombing. It is said that the inclusion of a non-terrorist sounding name like "Uke Hime no Kodomotachi" was intended as an antithesis to the "paternalistic {{ill|Emperor system|ja|天皇制}}."
== See Also == * {{ill|Theory of Japanese Indigenous People|ja|日本原住民論}} * {{ill|Anti-Japaneseism|ja|反日亡国論}} * {{ill|Anti-monarchism in Japan|ja|反天皇制}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nashinoki Shrine Bombing Incident}} [[Category:Explosions_in_Japan]] [[Category:Terrorist incidents in Japan]] [[Category:Religious terrorism]] [[Category:Incidents involving the Japanese New Left]] [[Category:Shinto history incidents]] [[Category:History of Kamigyo-ku]] [[Category:1977 crimes in Japan]] [[Category:January 1977 events in Asia]] [[Category:Shōwa period postwar Kyoto]]

It was perpetrated by {{ill|Saburō Katō (New Left)|ja|加藤三郎 (新左翼)}} of the {{ill|Japanese New Left|ja|日本の新左翼}}.
=== History of the Damaged Structure === Nashinoki Shrine was established in [[1885]] based on a decree from {{ill|Prince Kuni Asahiko|ja|久邇宮朝彦親王}}, on the site of the former Sanjo residence, enshrining {{ill|Sanetomi Sanjo|ja|三条実万}}, who contributed to the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}. Later, his son, {{ill|Sanjō Sanetomi|ja|三条実美}}, was also enshrined there. Katō criticized Sanjō Sanetomi as "the highest authority ({{ill|Daijō Daijin|ja|太政大臣}}) of the Meiji government, which became the bearer of {{ill|Japanese imperialism|ja|日本帝国主義}}," and proceeded to bomb Nashinoki Shrine, which enshrines him.
=== Overview of the Incident === On January 1, [[1977]] ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 52) at around 8:45 PM, an [[:en:Improvised explosive device|Improvised explosive device]] installed by Katō exploded in the main hall of Nashinoki Shrine, causing a fire. Thanks to the immediate firefighting efforts by the {{ill|guji|ja|宮司}} and {{ill|negi|ja|禰宜}}, the damage was limited to part of the main hall being scorched. Although it was New Year's Day, there were no worshippers present as it was nighttime, and no injuries were reported. Subsequent investigation found fragments of a fire extinguisher, confirming the incident as a bombing with a fire extinguisher bomb.
On January 5, Katō sent a {{ill|claim of responsibility|ja|犯行声明}} to the media under the names "Yami no Tsuchigumo" and "Uke Hime no Kodomotachi." The statement mentioned the "three crimes of Sanjō Sanetomi (establishing the exploitative and oppressive Meiji government, invading other peoples, and suppressing anti-establishment movements)," sentencing him to death by bombing. It is said that the inclusion of a non-terrorist sounding name like "Uke Hime no Kodomotachi" was intended as an antithesis to the "paternalistic {{ill|Emperor system|ja|天皇制}}."
== See Also == * {{ill|Theory of Japanese Indigenous People|ja|日本原住民論}} * {{ill|Anti-Japaneseism|ja|反日亡国論}} * {{ill|Anti-monarchism in Japan|ja|反天皇制}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nashinoki Shrine Bombing Incident}} [[Category:Explosions_in_Japan]] [[Category:Terrorist incidents in Japan]] [[Category:Religious terrorism]] [[Category:Incidents involving the Japanese New Left]] [[Category:Shinto history incidents]] [[Category:History of Kamigyo-ku]] [[Category:1977 crimes in Japan]] [[Category:January 1977 events in Asia]] [[Category:Shōwa period postwar Kyoto]]

[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}}{| class="infobox vcard" style="border-spacing:2px;" ! colspan="2" class="infobox-above" style="background-color:" |Nashinoki Shrine |- | colspan="2" class="infobox-image" |[[File:Nashinoki Shrine Haiden 3.jpg|frameless]] |} Nashinoki Shrine (梨木神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] in {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|en}}. Although it was never a [[Beppyo shrine]], it used to have the rank of Bekkaku kanpeisha under the modern system of ranked Shinto shrines.
Nashinoki Shrine is a relatively new Shinto shrine in Kyoto. It was built in 1885.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Fushimi Inari Taisha {{!}} Location {{!}} Shajiraku – Quality traditional food and cultural experiences at Kyoto shrines and temples.|url=https://www.shajiraku.jp/en/plan/location_detail.php?id=2|access-date=2023-04-07|website=社寺楽 Shajiraku|language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|date=2020-10-04|title=Nashinoki Shrine Kyoto, a Bush Clover Shrine {{!}} All About Kyoto|url=https://allaboutkyoto.com/en/nashinoki-shrine-kyoto/|access-date=2023-04-07|language=en-US|archive-date=2023-04-07|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230407205909/https://allaboutkyoto.com/en/nashinoki-shrine-kyoto/|url-status=dead}}</ref>
The Nashinoki Shrine hosts an annual {{ill|Bush clover|en}} festival.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Hagi Matsuri|url=https://www.discoverkyoto.com/event-calendar/september/hagi-matsuri-nashinoki-shrine/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Discover Kyoto|language=en}}</ref>

[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}}{| class="infobox vcard" style="border-spacing:2px;" ! colspan="2" class="infobox-above" style="background-color:" |Nashinoki Shrine |- | colspan="2" class="infobox-image" |[[File:Nashinoki Shrine Haiden 3.jpg|frameless]] |} Nashinoki Shrine (梨木神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] in {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|en}}. Although it was never a [[Beppyo shrine]], it used to have the rank of Bekkaku kanpeisha under the modern system of ranked Shinto shrines.
Nashinoki Shrine is a relatively new Shinto shrine in Kyoto. It was built in 1885.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Fushimi Inari Taisha {{!}} Location {{!}} Shajiraku – Quality traditional food and cultural experiences at Kyoto shrines and temples.|url=https://www.shajiraku.jp/en/plan/location_detail.php?id=2|access-date=2023-04-07|website=社寺楽 Shajiraku|language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|date=2020-10-04|title=Nashinoki Shrine Kyoto, a Bush Clover Shrine {{!}} All About Kyoto|url=https://allaboutkyoto.com/en/nashinoki-shrine-kyoto/|access-date=2023-04-07|language=en-US|archive-date=2023-04-07|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230407205909/https://allaboutkyoto.com/en/nashinoki-shrine-kyoto/|url-status=dead}}</ref>
The Nashinoki Shrine hosts an annual {{ill|Bush clover|en}} festival.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Hagi Matsuri|url=https://www.discoverkyoto.com/event-calendar/september/hagi-matsuri-nashinoki-shrine/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Discover Kyoto|language=en}}</ref>
== Bombing == The '''Nashinoki Shrine Bombing Incident''' (なしのきじんじゃばくはじけん) was a [[1977]] ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 52) guerrilla bombing that took place on [[January 1]] in {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}'s {{ill|Kamigyo Ward|ja|上京区}}, at {{ill|Nashinoki Shrine|ja|梨木神社}}. It was perpetrated by {{ill|Saburō Katō (New Left)|ja|加藤三郎 (新左翼)}} of the {{ill|Japanese New Left|ja|日本の新左翼}}.
=== History of the Damaged Structure === Nashinoki Shrine was established in [[1885]] based on a decree from {{ill|Prince Kuni Asahiko|ja|久邇宮朝彦親王}}, on the site of the former Sanjo residence, enshrining {{ill|Sanetomi Sanjo|ja|三条実万}}, who contributed to the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}. Later, his son, {{ill|Sanjō Sanetomi|ja|三条実美}}, was also enshrined there. Katō criticized Sanjō Sanetomi as "the highest authority ({{ill|Daijō Daijin|ja|太政大臣}}) of the Meiji government, which became the bearer of {{ill|Japanese imperialism|ja|日本帝国主義}}," and proceeded to bomb Nashinoki Shrine, which enshrines him.
=== Overview of the Incident === On January 1, [[1977]] ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 52) at around 8:45 PM, an [[:en:Improvised explosive device|Improvised explosive device]] installed by Katō exploded in the main hall of Nashinoki Shrine, causing a fire. Thanks to the immediate firefighting efforts by the {{ill|guji|ja|宮司}} and {{ill|negi|ja|禰宜}}, the damage was limited to part of the main hall being scorched. Although it was New Year's Day, there were no worshippers present as it was nighttime, and no injuries were reported. Subsequent investigation found fragments of a fire extinguisher, confirming the incident as a bombing with a fire extinguisher bomb.
On January 5, Katō sent a {{ill|claim of responsibility|ja|犯行声明}} to the media under the names "Yami no Tsuchigumo" and "Uke Hime no Kodomotachi." The statement mentioned the "three crimes of Sanjō Sanetomi (establishing the exploitative and oppressive Meiji government, invading other peoples, and suppressing anti-establishment movements)," sentencing him to death by bombing. It is said that the inclusion of a non-terrorist sounding name like "Uke Hime no Kodomotachi" was intended as an antithesis to the "paternalistic {{ill|Emperor system|ja|天皇制}}."
== See Also == * {{ill|Theory of Japanese Indigenous People|ja|日本原住民論}} * {{ill|Anti-Japaneseism|ja|反日亡国論}} * {{ill|Anti-monarchism in Japan|ja|反天皇制}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nashinoki Shrine Bombing Incident}} [[Category:Explosions_in_Japan]] [[Category:Terrorist incidents in Japan]] [[Category:Religious terrorism]] [[Category:Incidents involving the Japanese New Left]] [[Category:Shinto history incidents]] [[Category:History of Kamigyo-ku]] [[Category:1977 crimes in Japan]] [[Category:January 1977 events in Asia]] [[Category:Shōwa period postwar Kyoto]]

[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:36, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here [[every:Nashinoki Shrine|Nashinoki Shrine]] and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Nashinoki Shrine]][[en:draft:Nashinoki Shrine]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}}{| class="infobox vcard" style="border-spacing:2px;" ! colspan="2" class="infobox-above" style="background-color:" |Nashinoki Shrine |- | colspan="2" class="infobox-image" |[[File:Nashinoki Shrine Haiden 3.jpg|frameless]] |} Nashinoki Shrine (梨木神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] in {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|en}}. Although it was never a [[Beppyo shrine]], it used to have the rank of Bekkaku kanpeisha under the modern system of ranked Shinto shrines.
Nashinoki Shrine is a relatively new Shinto shrine in Kyoto. It was built in 1885.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Fushimi Inari Taisha {{!}} Location {{!}} Shajiraku – Quality traditional food and cultural experiences at Kyoto shrines and temples.|url=https://www.shajiraku.jp/en/plan/location_detail.php?id=2|access-date=2023-04-07|website=社寺楽 Shajiraku|language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|date=2020-10-04|title=Nashinoki Shrine Kyoto, a Bush Clover Shrine {{!}} All About Kyoto|url=https://allaboutkyoto.com/en/nashinoki-shrine-kyoto/|access-date=2023-04-07|language=en-US|archive-date=2023-04-07|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230407205909/https://allaboutkyoto.com/en/nashinoki-shrine-kyoto/|url-status=dead}}</ref>
The Nashinoki Shrine hosts an annual {{ill|Bush clover|en}} festival.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Hagi Matsuri|url=https://www.discoverkyoto.com/event-calendar/september/hagi-matsuri-nashinoki-shrine/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Discover Kyoto|language=en}}</ref>
== Bombing == The '''Nashinoki Shrine Bombing Incident''' (なしのきじんじゃばくはじけん) was a [[1977]] ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 52) guerrilla bombing that took place on [[January 1]] in {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}'s {{ill|Kamigyo Ward|ja|上京区}}, at {{ill|Nashinoki Shrine|ja|梨木神社}}. It was perpetrated by {{ill|Saburō Katō (New Left)|ja|加藤三郎 (新左翼)}} of the {{ill|Japanese New Left|ja|日本の新左翼}}.
=== History of the Damaged Structure === Nashinoki Shrine was established in [[1885]] based on a decree from {{ill|Prince Kuni Asahiko|ja|久邇宮朝彦親王}}, on the site of the former Sanjo residence, enshrining {{ill|Sanetomi Sanjo|ja|三条実万}}, who contributed to the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}. Later, his son, {{ill|Sanjō Sanetomi|ja|三条実美}}, was also enshrined there. Katō criticized Sanjō Sanetomi as "the highest authority ({{ill|Daijō Daijin|ja|太政大臣}}) of the Meiji government, which became the bearer of {{ill|Japanese imperialism|ja|日本帝国主義}}," and proceeded to bomb Nashinoki Shrine, which enshrines him.
=== Overview of the Incident === On January 1, [[1977]] ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 52) at around 8:45 PM, an [[:en:Improvised explosive device|Improvised explosive device]] installed by Katō exploded in the main hall of Nashinoki Shrine, causing a fire. Thanks to the immediate firefighting efforts by the {{ill|guji|ja|宮司}} and {{ill|negi|ja|禰宜}}, the damage was limited to part of the main hall being scorched. Although it was New Year's Day, there were no worshippers present as it was nighttime, and no injuries were reported. Subsequent investigation found fragments of a fire extinguisher, confirming the incident as a bombing with a fire extinguisher bomb.
On January 5, Katō sent a {{ill|claim of responsibility|ja|犯行声明}} to the media under the names "Yami no Tsuchigumo" and "Uke Hime no Kodomotachi." The statement mentioned the "three crimes of Sanjō Sanetomi (establishing the exploitative and oppressive Meiji government, invading other peoples, and suppressing anti-establishment movements)," sentencing him to death by bombing. It is said that the inclusion of a non-terrorist sounding name like "Uke Hime no Kodomotachi" was intended as an antithesis to the "paternalistic {{ill|Emperor system|ja|天皇制}}."
== See Also == * {{ill|Theory of Japanese Indigenous People|ja|日本原住民論}} * {{ill|Anti-Japaneseism|ja|反日亡国論}} * {{ill|Anti-monarchism in Japan|ja|反天皇制}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nashinoki Shrine Bombing Incident}} [[Category:Explosions_in_Japan]] [[Category:Terrorist incidents in Japan]] [[Category:Religious terrorism]] [[Category:Incidents involving the Japanese New Left]] [[Category:Shinto history incidents]] [[Category:History of Kamigyo-ku]] [[Category:1977 crimes in Japan]] [[Category:January 1977 events in Asia]] [[Category:Shōwa period postwar Kyoto]]
== References == * {{ill|Kyoto Shimbun|ja|京都新聞}}, January 3, 1977 morning edition, January 13, 1977 morning edition * Saburō Katō, "Opinion Paper 'From the Pigs of the Earth to You'" {{ill|The Science of Thought Society|ja|思想の科学社}}, 1992

Katō criticized Sanjō Sanetomi as "the highest authority ({{ill|Daijō Daijin|ja|太政大臣}}) of the Meiji government, which became the bearer of {{ill|Japanese imperialism|ja|日本帝国主義}}," and proceeded to bomb Nashinoki Shrine, which enshrines him.
=== Overview of the Incident === On January 1, [[1977]] ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 52) at around 8:45 PM, an [[:en:Improvised explosive device|Improvised explosive device]] installed by Katō exploded in the main hall of Nashinoki Shrine, causing a fire. Thanks to the immediate firefighting efforts by the {{ill|guji|ja|宮司}} and {{ill|negi|ja|禰宜}}, the damage was limited to part of the main hall being scorched. Although it was New Year's Day, there were no worshippers present as it was nighttime, and no injuries were reported. Subsequent investigation found fragments of a fire extinguisher, confirming the incident as a bombing with a fire extinguisher bomb.
On January 5, Katō sent a {{ill|claim of responsibility|ja|犯行声明}} to the media under the names "Yami no Tsuchigumo" and "Uke Hime no Kodomotachi." The statement mentioned the "three crimes of Sanjō Sanetomi (establishing the exploitative and oppressive Meiji government, invading other peoples, and suppressing anti-establishment movements)," sentencing him to death by bombing. It is said that the inclusion of a non-terrorist sounding name like "Uke Hime no Kodomotachi" was intended as an antithesis to the "paternalistic {{ill|Emperor system|ja|天皇制}}."
== See Also == * {{ill|Theory of Japanese Indigenous People|ja|日本原住民論}} * {{ill|Anti-Japaneseism|ja|反日亡国論}} * {{ill|Anti-monarchism in Japan|ja|反天皇制}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nashinoki Shrine Bombing Incident}} [[Category:Explosions_in_Japan]] [[Category:Terrorist incidents in Japan]] [[Category:Religious terrorism]] [[Category:Incidents involving the Japanese New Left]] [[Category:Shinto history incidents]] [[Category:History of Kamigyo-ku]] [[Category:1977 crimes in Japan]] [[Category:January 1977 events in Asia]] [[Category:Shōwa period postwar Kyoto]]

The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here [[every:Nashinoki Shrine|Nashinoki Shrine]] and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Nashinoki Shrine]][[en:draft:Nashinoki Shrine]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}}{| class="infobox vcard" style="border-spacing:2px;" ! colspan="2" class="infobox-above" style="background-color:" |Nashinoki Shrine |- | colspan="2" class="infobox-image" |[[File:Nashinoki Shrine Haiden 3.jpg|frameless]] |} Nashinoki Shrine (梨木神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] in {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|en}}. Although it was never a [[Beppyo shrine]], it used to have the rank of Bekkaku kanpeisha under the modern system of ranked Shinto shrines.
Nashinoki Shrine is a relatively new Shinto shrine in Kyoto. It was built in 1885.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Fushimi Inari Taisha {{!}} Location {{!}} Shajiraku – Quality traditional food and cultural experiences at Kyoto shrines and temples.|url=https://www.shajiraku.jp/en/plan/location_detail.php?id=2|access-date=2023-04-07|website=社寺楽 Shajiraku|language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|date=2020-10-04|title=Nashinoki Shrine Kyoto, a Bush Clover Shrine {{!}} All About Kyoto|url=https://allaboutkyoto.com/en/nashinoki-shrine-kyoto/|access-date=2023-04-07|language=en-US|archive-date=2023-04-07|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230407205909/https://allaboutkyoto.com/en/nashinoki-shrine-kyoto/|url-status=dead}}</ref>
The Nashinoki Shrine hosts an annual {{ill|Bush clover|en}} festival.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Hagi Matsuri|url=https://www.discoverkyoto.com/event-calendar/september/hagi-matsuri-nashinoki-shrine/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Discover Kyoto|language=en}}</ref>
== Bombing == The '''Nashinoki Shrine Bombing Incident''' (なしのきじんじゃばくはじけん) was a [[1977]] ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 52) guerrilla bombing that took place on [[January 1]] in {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}'s {{ill|Kamigyo Ward|ja|上京区}}, at {{ill|Nashinoki Shrine|ja|梨木神社}}. It was perpetrated by {{ill|Saburō Katō (New Left)|ja|加藤三郎 (新左翼)}} of the {{ill|Japanese New Left|ja|日本の新左翼}}.
=== History of the Damaged Structure === Nashinoki Shrine was established in [[1885]] based on a decree from {{ill|Prince Kuni Asahiko|ja|久邇宮朝彦親王}}, on the site of the former Sanjo residence, enshrining {{ill|Sanetomi Sanjo|ja|三条実万}}, who contributed to the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}. Later, his son, {{ill|Sanjō Sanetomi|ja|三条実美}}, was also enshrined there. Katō criticized Sanjō Sanetomi as "the highest authority ({{ill|Daijō Daijin|ja|太政大臣}}) of the Meiji government, which became the bearer of {{ill|Japanese imperialism|ja|日本帝国主義}}," and proceeded to bomb Nashinoki Shrine, which enshrines him.
=== Overview of the Incident === On January 1, [[1977]] ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 52) at around 8:45 PM, an [[:en:Improvised explosive device|Improvised explosive device]] installed by Katō exploded in the main hall of Nashinoki Shrine, causing a fire. Thanks to the immediate firefighting efforts by the {{ill|guji|ja|宮司}} and {{ill|negi|ja|禰宜}}, the damage was limited to part of the main hall being scorched. Although it was New Year's Day, there were no worshippers present as it was nighttime, and no injuries were reported. Subsequent investigation found fragments of a fire extinguisher, confirming the incident as a bombing with a fire extinguisher bomb.
On January 5, Katō sent a {{ill|claim of responsibility|ja|犯行声明}} to the media under the names "Yami no Tsuchigumo" and "Uke Hime no Kodomotachi." The statement mentioned the "three crimes of Sanjō Sanetomi (establishing the exploitative and oppressive Meiji government, invading other peoples, and suppressing anti-establishment movements)," sentencing him to death by bombing. It is said that the inclusion of a non-terrorist sounding name like "Uke Hime no Kodomotachi" was intended as an antithesis to the "paternalistic {{ill|Emperor system|ja|天皇制}}."
== See Also == * {{ill|Theory of Japanese Indigenous People|ja|日本原住民論}} * {{ill|Anti-Japaneseism|ja|反日亡国論}} * {{ill|Anti-monarchism in Japan|ja|反天皇制}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nashinoki Shrine Bombing Incident}} [[Category:Explosions_in_Japan]] [[Category:Terrorist incidents in Japan]] [[Category:Religious terrorism]] [[Category:Incidents involving the Japanese New Left]] [[Category:Shinto history incidents]] [[Category:History of Kamigyo-ku]] [[Category:1977 crimes in Japan]] [[Category:January 1977 events in Asia]] [[Category:Shōwa period postwar Kyoto]]

The Nashinoki Shrine hosts an annual {{ill|Bush clover|en}} festival.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Hagi Matsuri|url=https://www.discoverkyoto.com/event-calendar/september/hagi-matsuri-nashinoki-shrine/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Discover Kyoto|language=en}}</ref>
== Bombing == The '''Nashinoki Shrine Bombing Incident''' (なしのきじんじゃばくはじけん) was a [[1977]] ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 52) guerrilla bombing that took place on [[January 1]] in {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}'s {{ill|Kamigyo Ward|ja|上京区}}, at {{ill|Nashinoki Shrine|ja|梨木神社}}. It was perpetrated by {{ill|Saburō Katō (New Left)|ja|加藤三郎 (新左翼)}} of the {{ill|Japanese New Left|ja|日本の新左翼}}.
=== History of the Damaged Structure === Nashinoki Shrine was established in [[1885]] based on a decree from {{ill|Prince Kuni Asahiko|ja|久邇宮朝彦親王}}, on the site of the former Sanjo residence, enshrining {{ill|Sanetomi Sanjo|ja|三条実万}}, who contributed to the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}. Later, his son, {{ill|Sanjō Sanetomi|ja|三条実美}}, was also enshrined there. Katō criticized Sanjō Sanetomi as "the highest authority ({{ill|Daijō Daijin|ja|太政大臣}}) of the Meiji government, which became the bearer of {{ill|Japanese imperialism|ja|日本帝国主義}}," and proceeded to bomb Nashinoki Shrine, which enshrines him.
=== Overview of the Incident === On January 1, [[1977]] ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 52) at around 8:45 PM, an [[:en:Improvised explosive device|Improvised explosive device]] installed by Katō exploded in the main hall of Nashinoki Shrine, causing a fire. Thanks to the immediate firefighting efforts by the {{ill|guji|ja|宮司}} and {{ill|negi|ja|禰宜}}, the damage was limited to part of the main hall being scorched. Although it was New Year's Day, there were no worshippers present as it was nighttime, and no injuries were reported. Subsequent investigation found fragments of a fire extinguisher, confirming the incident as a bombing with a fire extinguisher bomb.
On January 5, Katō sent a {{ill|claim of responsibility|ja|犯行声明}} to the media under the names "Yami no Tsuchigumo" and "Uke Hime no Kodomotachi." The statement mentioned the "three crimes of Sanjō Sanetomi (establishing the exploitative and oppressive Meiji government, invading other peoples, and suppressing anti-establishment movements)," sentencing him to death by bombing. It is said that the inclusion of a non-terrorist sounding name like "Uke Hime no Kodomotachi" was intended as an antithesis to the "paternalistic {{ill|Emperor system|ja|天皇制}}."
== See Also == * {{ill|Theory of Japanese Indigenous People|ja|日本原住民論}} * {{ill|Anti-Japaneseism|ja|反日亡国論}} * {{ill|Anti-monarchism in Japan|ja|反天皇制}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nashinoki Shrine Bombing Incident}} [[Category:Explosions_in_Japan]] [[Category:Terrorist incidents in Japan]] [[Category:Religious terrorism]] [[Category:Incidents involving the Japanese New Left]] [[Category:Shinto history incidents]] [[Category:History of Kamigyo-ku]] [[Category:1977 crimes in Japan]] [[Category:January 1977 events in Asia]] [[Category:Shōwa period postwar Kyoto]]
== References == * {{ill|Kyoto Shimbun|ja|京都新聞}}, January 3, 1977 morning edition, January 13, 1977 morning edition * Saburō Katō, "Opinion Paper 'From the Pigs of the Earth to You'" {{ill|The Science of Thought Society|ja|思想の科学社}}, 1992

It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here [[every:Nashinoki Shrine|Nashinoki Shrine]] and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Nashinoki Shrine]][[en:draft:Nashinoki Shrine]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}}{| class="infobox vcard" style="border-spacing:2px;" ! colspan="2" class="infobox-above" style="background-color:" |Nashinoki Shrine |- | colspan="2" class="infobox-image" |[[File:Nashinoki Shrine Haiden 3.jpg|frameless]] |} Nashinoki Shrine (梨木神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] in {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|en}}. Although it was never a [[Beppyo shrine]], it used to have the rank of Bekkaku kanpeisha under the modern system of ranked Shinto shrines.
Nashinoki Shrine is a relatively new Shinto shrine in Kyoto. It was built in 1885.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Fushimi Inari Taisha {{!}} Location {{!}} Shajiraku – Quality traditional food and cultural experiences at Kyoto shrines and temples.|url=https://www.shajiraku.jp/en/plan/location_detail.php?id=2|access-date=2023-04-07|website=社寺楽 Shajiraku|language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|date=2020-10-04|title=Nashinoki Shrine Kyoto, a Bush Clover Shrine {{!}} All About Kyoto|url=https://allaboutkyoto.com/en/nashinoki-shrine-kyoto/|access-date=2023-04-07|language=en-US|archive-date=2023-04-07|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230407205909/https://allaboutkyoto.com/en/nashinoki-shrine-kyoto/|url-status=dead}}</ref>
The Nashinoki Shrine hosts an annual {{ill|Bush clover|en}} festival.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Hagi Matsuri|url=https://www.discoverkyoto.com/event-calendar/september/hagi-matsuri-nashinoki-shrine/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Discover Kyoto|language=en}}</ref>
== Bombing == The '''Nashinoki Shrine Bombing Incident''' (なしのきじんじゃばくはじけん) was a [[1977]] ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 52) guerrilla bombing that took place on [[January 1]] in {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}'s {{ill|Kamigyo Ward|ja|上京区}}, at {{ill|Nashinoki Shrine|ja|梨木神社}}. It was perpetrated by {{ill|Saburō Katō (New Left)|ja|加藤三郎 (新左翼)}} of the {{ill|Japanese New Left|ja|日本の新左翼}}.
=== History of the Damaged Structure === Nashinoki Shrine was established in [[1885]] based on a decree from {{ill|Prince Kuni Asahiko|ja|久邇宮朝彦親王}}, on the site of the former Sanjo residence, enshrining {{ill|Sanetomi Sanjo|ja|三条実万}}, who contributed to the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}. Later, his son, {{ill|Sanjō Sanetomi|ja|三条実美}}, was also enshrined there. Katō criticized Sanjō Sanetomi as "the highest authority ({{ill|Daijō Daijin|ja|太政大臣}}) of the Meiji government, which became the bearer of {{ill|Japanese imperialism|ja|日本帝国主義}}," and proceeded to bomb Nashinoki Shrine, which enshrines him.
=== Overview of the Incident === On January 1, [[1977]] ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 52) at around 8:45 PM, an [[:en:Improvised explosive device|Improvised explosive device]] installed by Katō exploded in the main hall of Nashinoki Shrine, causing a fire. Thanks to the immediate firefighting efforts by the {{ill|guji|ja|宮司}} and {{ill|negi|ja|禰宜}}, the damage was limited to part of the main hall being scorched. Although it was New Year's Day, there were no worshippers present as it was nighttime, and no injuries were reported. Subsequent investigation found fragments of a fire extinguisher, confirming the incident as a bombing with a fire extinguisher bomb.
On January 5, Katō sent a {{ill|claim of responsibility|ja|犯行声明}} to the media under the names "Yami no Tsuchigumo" and "Uke Hime no Kodomotachi." The statement mentioned the "three crimes of Sanjō Sanetomi (establishing the exploitative and oppressive Meiji government, invading other peoples, and suppressing anti-establishment movements)," sentencing him to death by bombing. It is said that the inclusion of a non-terrorist sounding name like "Uke Hime no Kodomotachi" was intended as an antithesis to the "paternalistic {{ill|Emperor system|ja|天皇制}}."
== See Also == * {{ill|Japanese Indigenous Theory|ja|日本原住民論}} * {{ill|Anti-Japaneseism|ja|反日亡国論}} * {{ill|Anti-monarchism in Japan|ja|反天皇制}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nashinoki Shrine Bombing Incident}} [[Category:Explosions_in_Japan]] [[Category:Terrorist incidents in Japan]] [[Category:Religious terrorism]] [[Category:Incidents involving the Japanese New Left]] [[Category:Shinto history incidents]] [[Category:History of Kamigyo-ku]] [[Category:1977 crimes in Japan]] [[Category:January 1977 events in Asia]] [[Category:Shōwa period postwar Kyoto]]
== References == * {{ill|Kyoto Shimbun|ja|京都新聞}}, January 3, 1977 morning edition, January 13, 1977 morning edition * Saburō Katō, "Opinion Paper 'From the Pigs of the Earth to You'" {{ill|The Science of Thought Society|ja|思想の科学社}}, 1992

The statement mentioned the "three crimes of Sanjō Sanetomi (establishing the exploitative and oppressive Meiji government, invading other peoples, and suppressing anti-establishment movements)," sentencing him to death by bombing. It is said that the inclusion of a non-terrorist sounding name like "Uke Hime no Kodomotachi" was intended as an antithesis to the "paternalistic {{ill|Emperor system|ja|天皇制}}."

The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here [[every:Nashinoki Shrine|Nashinoki Shrine]] and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Nashinoki Shrine]][[en:draft:Nashinoki Shrine]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:36, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here [[every:Nashinoki Shrine|Nashinoki Shrine]] and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Nashinoki Shrine]][[en:draft:Nashinoki Shrine]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}}{| class="infobox vcard" style="border-spacing:2px;" ! colspan="2" class="infobox-above" style="background-color:" |Nashinoki Shrine |- | colspan="2" class="infobox-image" |[[File:Nashinoki Shrine Haiden 3.jpg|frameless]] |} Nashinoki Shrine (梨木神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] in {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|en}}. Although it was never a [[Beppyo shrine]], it used to have the rank of Bekkaku kanpeisha under the modern system of ranked Shinto shrines.
Nashinoki Shrine is a relatively new Shinto shrine in Kyoto. It was built in 1885.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Fushimi Inari Taisha {{!}} Location {{!}} Shajiraku – Quality traditional food and cultural experiences at Kyoto shrines and temples.|url=https://www.shajiraku.jp/en/plan/location_detail.php?id=2|access-date=2023-04-07|website=社寺楽 Shajiraku|language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|date=2020-10-04|title=Nashinoki Shrine Kyoto, a Bush Clover Shrine {{!}} All About Kyoto|url=https://allaboutkyoto.com/en/nashinoki-shrine-kyoto/|access-date=2023-04-07|language=en-US|archive-date=2023-04-07|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230407205909/https://allaboutkyoto.com/en/nashinoki-shrine-kyoto/|url-status=dead}}</ref>
The Nashinoki Shrine hosts an annual {{ill|Bush clover|en}} festival.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Hagi Matsuri|url=https://www.discoverkyoto.com/event-calendar/september/hagi-matsuri-nashinoki-shrine/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Discover Kyoto|language=en}}</ref>
== Bombing == The '''Nashinoki Shrine Bombing Incident''' (なしのきじんじゃばくはじけん) was a [[1977]] ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 52) guerrilla bombing that took place on [[January 1]] in {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}'s {{ill|Kamigyo Ward|ja|上京区}}, at {{ill|Nashinoki Shrine|ja|梨木神社}}. It was perpetrated by {{ill|Saburō Katō (New Left)|ja|加藤三郎 (新左翼)}} of the {{ill|Japanese New Left|ja|日本の新左翼}}.
=== History of the Damaged Structure === Nashinoki Shrine was established in [[1885]] based on a decree from {{ill|Prince Kuni Asahiko|ja|久邇宮朝彦親王}}, on the site of the former Sanjo residence, enshrining {{ill|Sanetomi Sanjo|ja|三条実万}}, who contributed to the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}. Later, his son, {{ill|Sanjō Sanetomi|ja|三条実美}}, was also enshrined there. Katō criticized Sanjō Sanetomi as "the highest authority ({{ill|Daijō Daijin|ja|太政大臣}}) of the Meiji government, which became the bearer of {{ill|Japanese imperialism|ja|日本帝国主義}}," and proceeded to bomb Nashinoki Shrine, which enshrines him.
=== Overview of the Incident === On January 1, [[1977]] ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 52) at around 8:45 PM, an [[:en:Improvised explosive device|Improvised explosive device]] installed by Katō exploded in the main hall of Nashinoki Shrine, causing a fire. Thanks to the immediate firefighting efforts by the {{ill|guji|ja|宮司}} and {{ill|negi|ja|禰宜}}, the damage was limited to part of the main hall being scorched. Although it was New Year's Day, there were no worshippers present as it was nighttime, and no injuries were reported. Subsequent investigation found fragments of a fire extinguisher, confirming the incident as a bombing with a fire extinguisher bomb.
On January 5, Katō sent a {{ill|claim of responsibility|ja|犯行声明}} to the media under the names "Yami no Tsuchigumo" and "Uke Hime no Kodomotachi." The statement mentioned the "three crimes of Sanjō Sanetomi (establishing the exploitative and oppressive Meiji government, invading other peoples, and suppressing anti-establishment movements)," sentencing him to death by bombing. It is said that the inclusion of a non-terrorist sounding name like "Uke Hime no Kodomotachi" was intended as an antithesis to the "paternalistic {{ill|Emperor system|ja|天皇制}}."
== See Also == * {{ill|Japanese Indigenous Theory|ja|日本原住民論}} * {{ill|Anti-Japaneseism|ja|反日亡国論}} * {{ill|Anti-monarchism in Japan|ja|反天皇制}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nashinoki Shrine Bombing Incident}} [[Category:Explosions_in_Japan]] [[Category:Terrorist incidents in Japan]] [[Category:Religious terrorism]] [[Category:Incidents involving the Japanese New Left]] [[Category:Shinto history incidents]] [[Category:History of Kamigyo-ku]] [[Category:1977 crimes in Japan]] [[Category:January 1977 events in Asia]] [[Category:Shōwa period postwar Kyoto]]
== References == * {{ill|Kyoto Shimbun|ja|京都新聞}}, January 3, 1977 morning edition, January 13, 1977 morning edition * Saburō Katō, "Opinion Paper 'From the Pigs of the Earth to You'" {{ill|The Science of Thought Society|ja|思想の科学社}}, 1992

Nashinoki Shrine is a relatively new Shinto shrine in Kyoto. It was built in 1885.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Fushimi Inari Taisha {{!}} Location {{!}} Shajiraku – Quality traditional food and cultural experiences at Kyoto shrines and temples.|url=https://www.shajiraku.jp/en/plan/location_detail.php?id=2|access-date=2023-04-07|website=社寺楽 Shajiraku|language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|date=2020-10-04|title=Nashinoki Shrine Kyoto, a Bush Clover Shrine {{!}} All About Kyoto|url=https://allaboutkyoto.com/en/nashinoki-shrine-kyoto/|access-date=2023-04-07|language=en-US|archive-date=2023-04-07|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230407205909/https://allaboutkyoto.com/en/nashinoki-shrine-kyoto/|url-status=dead}}</ref>
The Nashinoki Shrine hosts an annual {{ill|Bush clover|en}} festival.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Hagi Matsuri|url=https://www.discoverkyoto.com/event-calendar/september/hagi-matsuri-nashinoki-shrine/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Discover Kyoto|language=en}}</ref>
== Bombing == The '''Nashinoki Shrine Bombing Incident''' (なしのきじんじゃばくはじけん) was a [[1977]] ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 52) guerrilla bombing that took place on [[January 1]] in {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}'s {{ill|Kamigyo Ward|ja|上京区}}, at {{ill|Nashinoki Shrine|ja|梨木神社}}. It was perpetrated by {{ill|Saburō Katō (New Left)|ja|加藤三郎 (新左翼)}} of the {{ill|Japanese New Left|ja|日本の新左翼}}.
=== History of the Damaged Structure === Nashinoki Shrine was established in [[1885]] based on a decree from {{ill|Prince Kuni Asahiko|ja|久邇宮朝彦親王}}, on the site of the former Sanjo residence, enshrining {{ill|Sanetomi Sanjo|ja|三条実万}}, who contributed to the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}. Later, his son, {{ill|Sanjō Sanetomi|ja|三条実美}}, was also enshrined there. Katō criticized Sanjō Sanetomi as "the highest authority ({{ill|Daijō Daijin|ja|太政大臣}}) of the Meiji government, which became the bearer of {{ill|Japanese imperialism|ja|日本帝国主義}}," and proceeded to bomb Nashinoki Shrine, which enshrines him.
=== Overview of the Incident === On January 1, [[1977]] ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 52) at around 8:45 PM, an [[:en:Improvised explosive device|Improvised explosive device]] installed by Katō exploded in the main hall of Nashinoki Shrine, causing a fire. Thanks to the immediate firefighting efforts by the {{ill|guji|ja|宮司}} and {{ill|negi|ja|禰宜}}, the damage was limited to part of the main hall being scorched. Although it was New Year's Day, there were no worshippers present as it was nighttime, and no injuries were reported. Subsequent investigation found fragments of a fire extinguisher, confirming the incident as a bombing with a fire extinguisher bomb.
On January 5, Katō sent a {{ill|claim of responsibility|ja|犯行声明}} to the media under the names "Yami no Tsuchigumo" and "Uke Hime no Kodomotachi." The statement mentioned the "three crimes of Sanjō Sanetomi (establishing the exploitative and oppressive Meiji government, invading other peoples, and suppressing anti-establishment movements)," sentencing him to death by bombing. It is said that the inclusion of a non-terrorist sounding name like "Uke Hime no Kodomotachi" was intended as an antithesis to the "paternalistic {{ill|Emperor system|ja|天皇制}}."

[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}}{| class="infobox vcard" style="border-spacing:2px;" ! colspan="2" class="infobox-above" style="background-color:" |Nashinoki Shrine |- | colspan="2" class="infobox-image" |[[File:Nashinoki Shrine Haiden 3.jpg|frameless]] |} Nashinoki Shrine (梨木神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] in {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|en}}. Although it was never a [[Beppyo shrine]], it used to have the rank of Bekkaku kanpeisha under the modern system of ranked Shinto shrines.
Nashinoki Shrine is a relatively new Shinto shrine in Kyoto. It was built in 1885.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Fushimi Inari Taisha {{!}} Location {{!}} Shajiraku – Quality traditional food and cultural experiences at Kyoto shrines and temples.|url=https://www.shajiraku.jp/en/plan/location_detail.php?id=2|access-date=2023-04-07|website=社寺楽 Shajiraku|language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|date=2020-10-04|title=Nashinoki Shrine Kyoto, a Bush Clover Shrine {{!}} All About Kyoto|url=https://allaboutkyoto.com/en/nashinoki-shrine-kyoto/|access-date=2023-04-07|language=en-US|archive-date=2023-04-07|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230407205909/https://allaboutkyoto.com/en/nashinoki-shrine-kyoto/|url-status=dead}}</ref>
The Nashinoki Shrine hosts an annual {{ill|Bush clover|en}} festival.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Hagi Matsuri|url=https://www.discoverkyoto.com/event-calendar/september/hagi-matsuri-nashinoki-shrine/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Discover Kyoto|language=en}}</ref>
== Bombing == The '''Nashinoki Shrine Bombing Incident''' (なしのきじんじゃばくはじけん) was a [[1977]] ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 52) guerrilla bombing that took place on [[January 1]] in {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}'s {{ill|Kamigyo Ward|ja|上京区}}, at {{ill|Nashinoki Shrine|ja|梨木神社}}. It was perpetrated by {{ill|Saburō Katō (New Left)|ja|加藤三郎 (新左翼)}} of the {{ill|Japanese New Left|ja|日本の新左翼}}.
=== History of the Damaged Structure === Nashinoki Shrine was established in [[1885]] based on a decree from {{ill|Prince Kuni Asahiko|ja|久邇宮朝彦親王}}, on the site of the former Sanjo residence, enshrining {{ill|Sanetomi Sanjo|ja|三条実万}}, who contributed to the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}. Later, his son, {{ill|Sanjō Sanetomi|ja|三条実美}}, was also enshrined there. Katō criticized Sanjō Sanetomi as "the highest authority ({{ill|Daijō Daijin|ja|太政大臣}}) of the Meiji government, which became the bearer of {{ill|Japanese imperialism|ja|日本帝国主義}}," and proceeded to bomb Nashinoki Shrine, which enshrines him.
=== Overview of the Incident === On January 1, [[1977]] ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 52) at around 8:45 PM, an [[:en:Improvised explosive device|Improvised explosive device]] installed by Katō exploded in the main hall of Nashinoki Shrine, causing a fire. Thanks to the immediate firefighting efforts by the {{ill|guji|ja|宮司}} and {{ill|negi|ja|禰宜}}, the damage was limited to part of the main hall being scorched. Although it was New Year's Day, there were no worshippers present as it was nighttime, and no injuries were reported. Subsequent investigation found fragments of a fire extinguisher, confirming the incident as a bombing with a fire extinguisher bomb.
On January 5, Katō sent a {{ill|claim of responsibility|ja|犯行声明}} to the media under the names "Yami no Tsuchigumo" and "Uke Hime no Kodomotachi." The statement mentioned the "three crimes of Sanjō Sanetomi (establishing the exploitative and oppressive Meiji government, invading other peoples, and suppressing anti-establishment movements)," sentencing him to death by bombing. It is said that the inclusion of a non-terrorist sounding name like "Uke Hime no Kodomotachi" was intended as an antithesis to the "paternalistic {{ill|Emperor system|ja|天皇制}}."

Although it was never a [[Beppyo shrine]], it used to have the rank of Bekkaku kanpeisha under the modern system of ranked Shinto shrines. Nashinoki Shrine is a relatively new Shinto shrine in Kyoto. It was built in 1885.{{Cite web|title=Fushimi Inari Taisha {{!}} Location {{!}} Shajiraku – Quality traditional food and cultural experiences at Kyoto shrines and temples.|url=https://www.shajiraku.jp/en/plan/location_detail.php?id=2|access-date=2023-04-07|website=社寺楽 Shajiraku|language=en}}{{Cite web|date=2020-10-04|title=Nashinoki Shrine Kyoto, a Bush Clover Shrine {{!}} All About Kyoto|url=https://allaboutkyoto.com/en/nashinoki-shrine-kyoto/|access-date=2023-04-07|language=en-US|archive-date=2023-04-07|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230407205909/https://allaboutkyoto.com/en/nashinoki-shrine-kyoto/|url-status=dead}}[https://kyoto-kanko.net/en/kyoto-spot/18818-2/ Nashinoki Shrine | Kyoto tourist spot - Kyoto Tourism Net] It is located adjacent to the Kyoto Imperial Palace. The Nashinoki Shrine hosts an annual {{ill|Bush clover|en}} festival.{{Cite web|title=Hagi Matsuri|url=https://www.discoverkyoto.com/event-calendar/september/hagi-matsuri-nashinoki-shrine/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Discover Kyoto|language=en}} == Bombing == The '''Nashinoki Shrine Bombing Incident''' (なしのきじんじゃばくはじけん) was a [[1977]] ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 52) guerrilla bombing that took place on [[January 1]] in {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}'s {{ill|Kamigyo Ward|ja|上京区}}, at {{ill|Nashinoki Shrine|ja|梨木神社}}. It was perpetrated by {{ill|Saburō Katō (New Left)|ja|加藤三郎 (新左翼)}} of the {{ill|Japanese New Left|ja|日本の新左翼}}. === History of the Damaged Structure === Nashinoki Shrine was established in [[1885]] based on a decree from {{ill|Prince Kuni Asahiko|ja|久邇宮朝彦親王}}, on the site of the former Sanjo residence, enshrining {{ill|Sanetomi Sanjo|ja|三条実万}}, who contributed to the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}. Later, his son, {{ill|Sanjō Sanetomi|ja|三条実美}}, was also enshrined there. Katō criticized Sanjō Sanetomi as "the highest authority ({{ill|Daijō Daijin|ja|太政大臣}}) of the Meiji government, which became the bearer of {{ill|Japanese imperialism|ja|日本帝国主義}}," and proceeded to bomb Nashinoki Shrine, which enshrines him. === Overview of the Incident === On January 1, [[1977]] ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 52) at around 8:45 PM, an [[:en:Improvised explosive device|Improvised explosive device]] installed by Katō exploded in the main hall of Nashinoki Shrine, causing a fire. Thanks to the immediate firefighting efforts by the {{ill|guji|ja|宮司}} and {{ill|negi|ja|禰宜}}, the damage was limited to part of the main hall being scorched. Although it was New Year's Day, there were no worshippers present as it was nighttime, and no injuries were reported. Subsequent investigation found fragments of a fire extinguisher, confirming the incident as a bombing with a fire extinguisher bomb. On January 5, Katō sent a {{ill|claim of responsibility|ja|犯行声明}} to the media under the names "Yami no Tsuchigumo" and "Uke Hime no Kodomotachi." The statement mentioned the "three crimes of Sanjō Sanetomi (establishing the exploitative and oppressive Meiji government, invading other peoples, and suppressing anti-establishment movements)," sentencing him to death by bombing. It is said that the inclusion of a non-terrorist sounding name like "Uke Hime no Kodomotachi" was intended as an antithesis to the "paternalistic {{ill|Emperor system|ja|天皇制}}." == See Also == * {{ill|Japanese Indigenous Theory|ja|日本原住民論}} * {{ill|Anti-Japaneseism|ja|反日亡国論}} * {{ill|Anti-monarchism in Japan|ja|反天皇制}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nashinoki Shrine Bombing Incident}} {{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here [[every:Nashinoki Shrine|Nashinoki Shrine]] and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Nashinoki Shrine]][[en:draft:Nashinoki Shrine]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:36, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here [[every:Nashinoki Shrine|Nashinoki Shrine]] and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Nashinoki Shrine]][[en:draft:Nashinoki Shrine]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}}{| class="infobox vcard" style="border-spacing:2px;" [[File:Nashinoki_Shrine_Haiden_3.jpg|thumb]] {{神社|名称=梨木神社|画像=[[画像:Nashinoki Shrine Haiden 3.jpg|250px]]
拝殿|所在地=[[京都府]][[京都市]][[上京区]][[寺町通]][[広小路通 (京都市)|広小路]]上ル染殿町680|位置={{Coord|35|1|30|N|135|46|2.5|E|region:JP-26:type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|祭神={{Ublist| [[三条実万|三條實萬]] | [[三条実美|三條實美]] }}|社格=旧[[別格官幣社]]|創建=[[1885年]]（[[明治]]18年）|別名=萩の宮|例祭=[[4月18日]]・[[10月10日]]|地図={{Location map|Japan Kyoto city|label=梨木神社|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|width=200|caption=}}}} Nashinoki Shrine (梨木神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] in {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|en}}. Although it was never a [[Beppyo shrine]], it used to have the rank of Bekkaku kanpeisha under the modern system of ranked Shinto shrines. Nashinoki Shrine is a relatively new Shinto shrine in Kyoto. It was built in 1885.{{Cite web|title=Fushimi Inari Taisha {{!}} Location {{!}} Shajiraku – Quality traditional food and cultural experiences at Kyoto shrines and temples.|url=https://www.shajiraku.jp/en/plan/location_detail.php?id=2|access-date=2023-04-07|website=社寺楽 Shajiraku|language=en}}{{Cite web|date=2020-10-04|title=Nashinoki Shrine Kyoto, a Bush Clover Shrine {{!}} All About Kyoto|url=https://allaboutkyoto.com/en/nashinoki-shrine-kyoto/|access-date=2023-04-07|language=en-US|archive-date=2023-04-07|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230407205909/https://allaboutkyoto.com/en/nashinoki-shrine-kyoto/|url-status=dead}}[https://kyoto-kanko.net/en/kyoto-spot/18818-2/ Nashinoki Shrine | Kyoto tourist spot - Kyoto Tourism Net] It is located adjacent to the Kyoto Imperial Palace. The Nashinoki Shrine hosts an annual {{ill|Bush clover|en}} festival.{{Cite web|title=Hagi Matsuri|url=https://www.discoverkyoto.com/event-calendar/september/hagi-matsuri-nashinoki-shrine/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Discover Kyoto|language=en}} == Bombing == The '''Nashinoki Shrine Bombing Incident''' (なしのきじんじゃばくはじけん) was a [[1977]] ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 52) guerrilla bombing that took place on [[January 1]] in {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}'s {{ill|Kamigyo Ward|ja|上京区}}, at {{ill|Nashinoki Shrine|ja|梨木神社}}. It was perpetrated by {{ill|Saburō Katō (New Left)|ja|加藤三郎 (新左翼)}} of the {{ill|Japanese New Left|ja|日本の新左翼}}. === History of the Damaged Structure === Nashinoki Shrine was established in [[1885]] based on a decree from {{ill|Prince Kuni Asahiko|ja|久邇宮朝彦親王}}, on the site of the former Sanjo residence, enshrining {{ill|Sanetomi Sanjo|ja|三条実万}}, who contributed to the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}. Later, his son, {{ill|Sanjō Sanetomi|ja|三条実美}}, was also enshrined there. Katō criticized Sanjō Sanetomi as "the highest authority ({{ill|Daijō Daijin|ja|太政大臣}}) of the Meiji government, which became the bearer of {{ill|Japanese imperialism|ja|日本帝国主義}}," and proceeded to bomb Nashinoki Shrine, which enshrines him. === Overview of the Incident === On January 1, [[1977]] ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 52) at around 8:45 PM, an [[:en:Improvised explosive device|Improvised explosive device]] installed by Katō exploded in the main hall of Nashinoki Shrine, causing a fire. Thanks to the immediate firefighting efforts by the {{ill|guji|ja|宮司}} and {{ill|negi|ja|禰宜}}, the damage was limited to part of the main hall being scorched. Although it was New Year's Day, there were no worshippers present as it was nighttime, and no injuries were reported. Subsequent investigation found fragments of a fire extinguisher, confirming the incident as a bombing with a fire extinguisher bomb. On January 5, Katō sent a {{ill|claim of responsibility|ja|犯行声明}} to the media under the names "Yami no Tsuchigumo" and "Uke Hime no Kodomotachi." The statement mentioned the "three crimes of Sanjō Sanetomi (establishing the exploitative and oppressive Meiji government, invading other peoples, and suppressing anti-establishment movements)," sentencing him to death by bombing. It is said that the inclusion of a non-terrorist sounding name like "Uke Hime no Kodomotachi" was intended as an antithesis to the "paternalistic {{ill|Emperor system|ja|天皇制}}."== See Also == * {{ill|Japanese Indigenous Theory|ja|日本原住民論}} * {{ill|Anti-Japaneseism|ja|反日亡国論}} * {{ill|Anti-monarchism in Japan|ja|反天皇制}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nashinoki Shrine Bombing Incident}} [[Category:Explosions_in_Japan]] [[Category:Terrorist incidents in Japan]] [[Category:Religious terrorism]] [[Category:Incidents involving the Japanese New Left]] [[Category:Shinto history incidents]] [[Category:History of Kamigyo-ku]] [[Category:1977 crimes in Japan]] [[Category:January 1977 events in Asia]] [[Category:Shōwa period postwar Kyoto]] == References == * {{ill|Kyoto Shimbun|ja|京都新聞}}, January 3, 1977 morning edition, January 13, 1977 morning edition * Saburō Katō, "Opinion Paper 'From the Pigs of the Earth to You'" {{ill|The Science of Thought Society|ja|思想の科学社}}, 1992 == It is said that the inclusion of a non-terrorist sounding name like "Uke Hime no Kodomotachi" was intended as an antithesis to the "paternalistic {{ill|Emperor system|ja|天皇制}}."== See Also == * {{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here [[every:Nashinoki Shrine|Nashinoki Shrine]] and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Nashinoki Shrine]][[en:draft:Nashinoki Shrine]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:36, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here [[every:Nashinoki Shrine|Nashinoki Shrine]] and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Nashinoki Shrine]][[en:draft:Nashinoki Shrine]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}}{| class="infobox vcard" style="border-spacing:2px;" [[File:Nashinoki_Shrine_Haiden_3.jpg|thumb]] {{神社|名称=梨木神社|画像=[[画像:Nashinoki Shrine Haiden 3.jpg|250px]]
拝殿|所在地=[[京都府]][[京都市]][[上京区]][[寺町通]][[広小路通 (京都市)|広小路]]上ル染殿町680|位置={{Coord|35|1|30|N|135|46|2.5|E|region:JP-26:type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|祭神={{Ublist| [[三条実万|三條實萬]] | [[三条実美|三條實美]] }}|社格=旧[[別格官幣社]]|創建=[[1885年]]（[[明治]]18年）|別名=萩の宮|例祭=[[4月18日]]・[[10月10日]]|地図={{Location map|Japan Kyoto city|label=梨木神社|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|width=200|caption=}}}} Nashinoki Shrine (梨木神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] in {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|en}}. Although it was never a [[Beppyo shrine]], it used to have the rank of Bekkaku kanpeisha under the modern system of ranked Shinto shrines. Nashinoki Shrine is a relatively new Shinto shrine in Kyoto. It was built in 1885.{{Cite web|title=Fushimi Inari Taisha {{!}} Location {{!}} Shajiraku – Quality traditional food and cultural experiences at Kyoto shrines and temples.|url=https://www.shajiraku.jp/en/plan/location_detail.php?id=2|access-date=2023-04-07|website=社寺楽 Shajiraku|language=en}}{{Cite web|date=2020-10-04|title=Nashinoki Shrine Kyoto, a Bush Clover Shrine {{!}} All About Kyoto|url=https://allaboutkyoto.com/en/nashinoki-shrine-kyoto/|access-date=2023-04-07|language=en-US|archive-date=2023-04-07|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230407205909/https://allaboutkyoto.com/en/nashinoki-shrine-kyoto/|url-status=dead}}[https://kyoto-kanko.net/en/kyoto-spot/18818-2/ Nashinoki Shrine | Kyoto tourist spot - Kyoto Tourism Net] It is located adjacent to the Kyoto Imperial Palace. The Nashinoki Shrine hosts an annual {{ill|Bush clover|en}} festival.{{Cite web|title=Hagi Matsuri|url=https://www.discoverkyoto.com/event-calendar/september/hagi-matsuri-nashinoki-shrine/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Discover Kyoto|language=en}} == Bombing == The '''Nashinoki Shrine Bombing Incident''' (なしのきじんじゃばくはじけん) was a [[1977]] ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 52) guerrilla bombing that took place on [[January 1]] in {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}'s {{ill|Kamigyo Ward|ja|上京区}}, at {{ill|Nashinoki Shrine|ja|梨木神社}}. It was perpetrated by {{ill|Saburō Katō (New Left)|ja|加藤三郎 (新左翼)}} of the {{ill|Japanese New Left|ja|日本の新左翼}}. === History of the Damaged Structure === Nashinoki Shrine was established in [[1885]] based on a decree from {{ill|Prince Kuni Asahiko|ja|久邇宮朝彦親王}}, on the site of the former Sanjo residence, enshrining {{ill|Sanetomi Sanjo|ja|三条実万}}, who contributed to the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}. Later, his son, {{ill|Sanjō Sanetomi|ja|三条実美}}, was also enshrined there. Katō criticized Sanjō Sanetomi as "the highest authority ({{ill|Daijō Daijin|ja|太政大臣}}) of the Meiji government, which became the bearer of {{ill|Japanese imperialism|ja|日本帝国主義}}," and proceeded to bomb Nashinoki Shrine, which enshrines him. === Overview of the Incident === On January 1, [[1977]] ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 52) at around 8:45 PM, an [[:en:Improvised explosive device|Improvised explosive device]] installed by Katō exploded in the main hall of Nashinoki Shrine, causing a fire. Thanks to the immediate firefighting efforts by the {{ill|guji|ja|宮司}} and {{ill|negi|ja|禰宜}}, the damage was limited to part of the main hall being scorched. Although it was New Year's Day, there were no worshippers present as it was nighttime, and no injuries were reported. Subsequent investigation found fragments of a fire extinguisher, confirming the incident as a bombing with a fire extinguisher bomb. On January 5, Katō sent a {{ill|claim of responsibility|ja|犯行声明}} to the media under the names "Yami no Tsuchigumo" and "Uke Hime no Kodomotachi." The statement mentioned the "three crimes of Sanjō Sanetomi (establishing the exploitative and oppressive Meiji government, invading other peoples, and suppressing anti-establishment movements)," sentencing him to death by bombing. It is said that the inclusion of a non-terrorist sounding name like "Uke Hime no Kodomotachi" was intended as an antithesis to the "paternalistic {{ill|Emperor system|ja|天皇制}}." == See Also! == * {{ill|Japanese Indigenous Theory|ja|日本原住民論}} * {{ill|Anti-Japaneseism|ja|反日亡国論}} * {{ill|Anti-monarchism in Japan|ja|反天皇制}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nashinoki Shrine Bombing Incident}} [[Category:Explosions_in_Japan]] [[Category:Terrorist incidents in Japan]] [[Category:Religious terrorism]] [[Category:Incidents involving the Japanese New Left]] [[Category:Shinto history incidents]] [[Category:History of Kamigyo-ku]] [[Category:1977 crimes in Japan]] [[Category:January 1977 events in Asia]] [[Category:Shōwa period postwar Kyoto]] == References == * {{ill|Kyoto Shimbun|ja|京都新聞}}, January 3, 1977 morning edition, January 13, 1977 morning edition * Saburō Katō, "Opinion Paper 'From the Pigs of the Earth to You'" {{ill|The Science of Thought Society|ja|思想の科学社}}, 1992 == It is said that the inclusion of a non-terrorist sounding name like "Uke Hime no Kodomotachi" was intended as an antithesis to the "paternalistic {{ill|Emperor system|ja|天皇制}}." == See Also == * 拝殿|所在地=[[京都府]][[京都市]][[上京区]][[寺町通]][[広小路通 (京都市)|広小路]]上ル染殿町680|位置={{Coord|35|1|30|N|135|46|2.5|E|region:JP-26:type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|祭神={{Ublist| [[三条実万|三條實萬]] | [[三条実美|三條實美]] }}|社格=旧[[別格官幣社]]|創建=[[1885th year ]]（[[明治]]18年）|別名=萩の宮|例祭=[[4月18日]]・[[10月10日]]|地図={{Location map|Japan Kyoto city|label=梨木神社|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|width=200|caption=}}}} Nashinoki Shrine (梨木神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] in {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|en}}. Although it was never a [[Beppyo shrine]], it used to have the rank of Bekkaku kanpeisha under the modern system of ranked Shinto shrines. Nashinoki Shrine is a relatively new Shinto shrine in Kyoto. It was built in 1885.{{Cite web|title=Fushimi Inari Taisha {{!}} Location {{!}} Shajiraku – Quality traditional food and cultural experiences at Kyoto shrines and temples.|url=https://www.shajiraku.jp/en/plan/location_detail.php?id=2|access-date=2023-04-07|website=社寺楽 Shajiraku|language=en}}{{Cite web|date=2020-10-04|title=Nashinoki Shrine Kyoto, a Bush Clover Shrine {{!}} All About Kyoto|url=https://allaboutkyoto.com/en/nashinoki-shrine-kyoto/|access-date=2023-04-07|language=en-US|archive-date=2023-04-07|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230407205909/https://allaboutkyoto.com/en/nashinoki-shrine-kyoto/|url-status=dead}}[https://kyoto-kanko.net/en/kyoto-spot/18818-2/ Nashinoki Shrine | Kyoto tourist spot - Kyoto Tourism Net] It is located adjacent to the Kyoto Imperial Palace. The Nashinoki Shrine hosts an annual {{ill|Bush clover|en}} festival.{{Cite web|title=Hagi Matsuri|url=https://www.discoverkyoto.com/event-calendar/september/hagi-matsuri-nashinoki-shrine/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Discover Kyoto|language=en}} == Bombing == The '''Nashinoki Shrine Bombing Incident''' (なしのきじんじゃばくはじけん) was a [[1977]] ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 52) guerrilla bombing that took place on [[January 1]] in {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}'s {{ill|Kamigyo Ward|ja|上京区}}, at {{ill|Nashinoki Shrine|ja|梨木神社}}. It was perpetrated by {{ill|Saburō Katō (New Left)|ja|加藤三郎 (新左翼)}} of the {{ill|Japanese New Left|ja|日本の新左翼}}. === History of the Damaged Structure === Nashinoki Shrine was established in [[1885]] based on a decree from {{ill|Prince Kuni Asahiko|ja|久邇宮朝彦親王}}, on the site of the former Sanjo residence, enshrining {{ill|Sanetomi Sanjo|ja|三条実万}}, who contributed to the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}. Later, his son, {{ill|Sanjō Sanetomi|ja|三条実美}}, was also enshrined there. Katō criticized Sanjō Sanetomi as "the highest authority ({{ill|Daijō Daijin|ja|太政大臣}}) of the Meiji government, which became the bearer of {{ill|Japanese imperialism|ja|日本帝国主義}}," and proceeded to bomb Nashinoki Shrine, which enshrines him. === Overview of the Incident === On January 1, [[1977]] ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 52) at around 8:45 PM, an [[:en:Improvised explosive device|Improvised explosive device]] installed by Katō exploded in the main hall of Nashinoki Shrine, causing a fire. Thanks to the immediate firefighting efforts by the {{ill|guji|ja|宮司}} and {{ill|negi|ja|禰宜}}, the damage was limited to part of the main hall being scorched. Although it was New Year's Day, there were no worshippers present as it was nighttime, and no injuries were reported. Subsequent investigation found fragments of a fire extinguisher, confirming the incident as a bombing with a fire extinguisher bomb. On January 5, Katō sent a {{ill|claim of responsibility|ja|犯行声明}} to the media under the names "Yami no Tsuchigumo" and "Uke Hime no Kodomotachi." The statement mentioned the "three crimes of Sanjō Sanetomi (establishing the exploitative and oppressive Meiji government, invading other peoples, and suppressing anti-establishment movements)," sentencing him to death by bombing. It is said that the inclusion of a non-terrorist sounding name like "Uke Hime no Kodomotachi" was intended as an antithesis to the "paternalistic {{ill|Emperor system|ja|天皇制}}." == See Also == * {{ill|Japanese Indigenous Theory|ja|日本原住民論}} * {{ill|Anti-Japaneseism|ja|反日亡国論}} * {{ill|Anti-monarchism in Japan|ja|反天皇制}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nashinoki Shrine Bombing Incident}} The statement mentioned the "three crimes of Sanjō Sanetomi (establishing the exploitative and oppressive Meiji government, invading other peoples, and suppressing anti-establishment movements)," sentencing him to death by bombing. It is said that the inclusion of a non-terrorist sounding name like "Uke Hime no Kodomotachi" was intended as an antithesis to the "paternalistic {{ill|Emperor system|ja|天皇制}}." == See Also == * {{ill|Japanese Indigenous Theory|ja|日本原住民論}} * {{ill|Anti-Japaneseism|ja|反日亡国論}} * {{ill|Anti-monarchism in Japan|ja|反天皇制}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nashinoki Shrine Bombing Incident}} [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}}{| class="infobox vcard" style="border-spacing:2px;" [[File:Nashinoki_Shrine_Haiden_3.jpg|thumb]] {{神社|名称=梨木神社|画像=[[画像:Nashinoki Shrine Haiden 3.jpg|250px]]
拝殿|所在地=[[京都府]][[京都市]][[上京区]][[寺町通]][[広小路通 (京都市)|広小路]]上ル染殿町680|位置={{Coord|35|1|30|N|135|46|2.5|E|region:JP-26:type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|祭神={{Ublist| [[三条実万|三條實萬]] | [[三条実美|三條實美]] }}|社格=旧[[別格官幣社]]|創建=[[1885th year ]]（[[明治]]18th year ）|別名=萩の宮|例祭=[[4月18日]]・[[10月10日]]|地図={{Location map|Japan Kyoto city|label=梨木神社|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|width=200|caption=}}}} Nashinoki Shrine (梨木神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] in {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|en}}. Although it was never a [[Beppyo shrine]], it used to have the rank of Bekkaku kanpeisha under the modern system of ranked Shinto shrines. Nashinoki Shrine is a relatively new Shinto shrine in Kyoto. It was built in 1885.{{Cite web|title=Fushimi Inari Taisha {{!}} Location {{!}} Shajiraku – Quality traditional food and cultural experiences at Kyoto shrines and temples.|url=https://www.shajiraku.jp/en/plan/location_detail.php?id=2|access-date=2023-04-07|website=社寺楽 Shajiraku|language=en}}{{Cite web|date=2020-10-04|title=Nashinoki Shrine Kyoto, a Bush Clover Shrine {{!}} All About Kyoto|url=https://allaboutkyoto.com/en/nashinoki-shrine-kyoto/|access-date=2023-04-07|language=en-US|archive-date=2023-04-07|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230407205909/https://allaboutkyoto.com/en/nashinoki-shrine-kyoto/|url-status=dead}}[https://kyoto-kanko.net/en/kyoto-spot/18818-2/ Nashinoki Shrine | Kyoto tourist spot - Kyoto Tourism Net] It is located adjacent to the Kyoto Imperial Palace. The Nashinoki Shrine hosts an annual {{ill|Bush clover|en}} festival.{{Cite web|title=Hagi Matsuri|url=https://www.discoverkyoto.com/event-calendar/september/hagi-matsuri-nashinoki-shrine/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Discover Kyoto|language=en}} == Bombing == The '''Nashinoki Shrine Bombing Incident''' (なしのきじんじゃばくはじけん) was a [[1977]] ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 52) guerrilla bombing that took place on [[January 1]] in {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}'s {{ill|Kamigyo Ward|ja|上京区}}, at {{ill|Nashinoki Shrine|ja|梨木神社}}. It was perpetrated by {{ill|Saburō Katō (New Left)|ja|加藤三郎 (新左翼)}} of the {{ill|Japanese New Left|ja|日本の新左翼}}. === History of the Damaged Structure === Nashinoki Shrine was established in [[1885]] based on a decree from {{ill|Prince Kuni Asahiko|ja|久邇宮朝彦親王}}, on the site of the former Sanjo residence, enshrining {{ill|Sanetomi Sanjo|ja|三条実万}}, who contributed to the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}. Later, his son, {{ill|Sanjō Sanetomi|ja|三条実美}}, was also enshrined there. Katō criticized Sanjō Sanetomi as "the highest authority ({{ill|Daijō Daijin|ja|太政大臣}}) of the Meiji government, which became the bearer of {{ill|Japanese imperialism|ja|日本帝国主義}}," and proceeded to bomb Nashinoki Shrine, which enshrines him. === Overview of the Incident === On January 1, [[1977]] ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 52) at around 8:45 PM, an [[:en:Improvised explosive device|Improvised explosive device]] installed by Katō exploded in the main hall of Nashinoki Shrine, causing a fire. Thanks to the immediate firefighting efforts by the {{ill|guji|ja|宮司}} and {{ill|negi|ja|禰宜}}, the damage was limited to part of the main hall being scorched. Although it was New Year's Day, there were no worshippers present as it was nighttime, and no injuries were reported. Subsequent investigation found fragments of a fire extinguisher, confirming the incident as a bombing with a fire extinguisher bomb. On January 5, Katō sent a {{ill|claim of responsibility|ja|犯行声明}} to the media under the names "Yami no Tsuchigumo" and "Uke Hime no Kodomotachi." The statement mentioned the "three crimes of Sanjō Sanetomi (establishing the exploitative and oppressive Meiji government, invading other peoples, and suppressing anti-establishment movements)," sentencing him to death by bombing. It is said that the inclusion of a non-terrorist sounding name like "Uke Hime no Kodomotachi" was intended as an antithesis to the "paternalistic {{ill|Emperor system|ja|天皇制}}." == See Also == * {{ill|Japanese Indigenous Theory|ja|日本原住民論}} * {{ill|Anti-Japaneseism|ja|反日亡国論}} * {{ill|Anti-monarchism in Japan|ja|反天皇制}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nashinoki Shrine Bombing Incident}} The statement mentioned the "three crimes of Sanjō Sanetomi (establishing the exploitative and oppressive Meiji government, invading other peoples, and suppressing anti-establishment movements)," sentencing him to death by bombing. It is said that the inclusion of a non-terrorist sounding name like "Uke Hime no Kodomotachi" was intended as an antithesis to the "paternalistic {{ill|Emperor system|ja|天皇制}}." == See Also == *
