拝殿|所在地=[[京都府]][[京都市]][[上京区]][[寺町通]][[広小路通 (京都市)|広小路]]上ル染殿町680|位置={{Coord|35|1|30|N|135|46|2.5|E|region:JP-26:type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|祭神={{Ublist| [[三条実万|三條實萬]] | [[三条実美|三條實美]] }}|社格=旧[[別格官幣社]]|創建=[[1885th year ]]（[[明治]]18th year ）|別名=萩の宮|例祭=[[4月18日]]・[[10月10日]]|地図={{Location map|Japan Kyoto city|label=梨木神社|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|width=200|caption=}}}} Nashinoki Shrine (梨木神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] in {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigyō-ku|qq=}}. Although it was never a [[Beppyo shrine]], it used to have the Rank of Bekkaku kanpeisha under the modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines. Nashinoki Shrine is a relatively new Shinto shrine in Kyoto. It was built in 1885.{{Cite web|title=Fushimi Inari Taisha {{!|qq=draft}} Location {{!}} Shajiraku – Quality traditional food and cultural experiences at Kyoto shrines and temples.|url=https://www.shajiraku.jp/en/plan/location_detail.php?id=2|access-date=2023-04-07|website=社寺楽 Shajiraku|language=en}}{{Cite web|date=2020-10-04|title=Nashinoki Shrine Kyoto, a Bush Clover Shrine {{!}} All About Kyoto|url=https://allaboutkyoto.com/en/nashinoki-shrine-kyoto/|access-date=2023-04-07|language=en-US|archive-date=2023-04-07|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230407205909/https://allaboutkyoto.com/en/nashinoki-shrine-kyoto/|url-status=dead}}[https://kyoto-kanko.net/en/kyoto-spot/18818-2/ Nashinoki Shrine | Kyoto tourist spot - Kyoto Tourism Net] It is located adjacent to the Kyoto Imperial Palace. The Nashinoki Shrine hosts an annual {{ill|Bush clover|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bush clover|qq=}} festival.{{Cite web|title=Hagi Matsuri|url=https://www.discoverkyoto.com/event-calendar/september/hagi-matsuri-nashinoki-shrine/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Discover Kyoto|language=en|qq=draft}} == Bombing == The '''Nashinoki Shrine Bombing Incident''' (なしのきじんじゃばくはじけん) was a [[1977]] ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōwa|qq=}} 52) guerrilla bombing that took place on [[January 1]] in {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|qq=}}'s {{ill|Kamigyo Ward|ja|上京区|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigyo Ward|qq=}}, at {{ill|Nashinoki Shrine|ja|梨木神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nashinoki Shrine|qq=}}. It was perpetrated by {{ill|Saburō Katō (New Left)|ja|加藤三郎 (新左翼)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saburō Katō (New Left)|qq=}} of the {{ill|Japanese New Left|ja|日本の新左翼|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese New Left|qq=}}. === History of the Damaged Structure === Nashinoki Shrine was established in [[1885]] based on a decree from {{ill|Prince Kuni Asahiko|ja|久邇宮朝彦親王|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kuni Asahiko|qq=}}, on the site of the former Sanjo residence, enshrining {{ill|Sanetomi Sanjo|ja|三条実万|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanetomi Sanjo|qq=}}, who contributed to the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Restoration|qq=}}. Later, his son, {{ill|Sanjō Sanetomi|ja|三条実美|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanjō Sanetomi|qq=}}, was also enshrined there. Katō criticized Sanjō Sanetomi as "the highest authority ({{ill|Daijō Daijin|ja|太政大臣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijō Daijin|qq=}}) of the Meiji government, which became the bearer of {{ill|Japanese imperialism|ja|日本帝国主義|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese imperialism|qq=}}," and proceeded to bomb Nashinoki Shrine, which enshrines him. === Overview of the Incident === On January 1, [[1977]] ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōwa|qq=}} 52) at around 8:45 PM, an [[:en:Improvised explosive device|Improvised explosive device]] installed by Katō exploded in the main hall of Nashinoki Shrine, causing a fire. Thanks to the immediate firefighting efforts by the {{ill|guji|ja|宮司|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/guji|qq=}} and {{ill|negi|ja|禰宜|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/negi|qq=}}, the damage was limited to part of the main hall being scorched. Although it was New Year's Day, there were no worshippers present as it was nighttime, and no injuries were reported. Subsequent investigation found fragments of a fire extinguisher, confirming the incident as a bombing with a fire extinguisher bomb. On January 5, Katō sent a {{ill|claim of responsibility|ja|犯行声明|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/claim of responsibility|qq=}} to the media under the names "Yami no Tsuchigumo" and "Uke Hime no Kodomotachi." The statement mentioned the "three crimes of Sanjō Sanetomi (establishing the exploitative and oppressive Meiji government, invading other peoples, and suppressing anti-establishment movements)," sentencing him to death by bombing. It is said that the inclusion of a non-terrorist sounding name like "Uke Hime no Kodomotachi" was intended as an antithesis to the "paternalistic {{ill|Emperor system|ja|天皇制|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor system|qq=}}." == See Also == * {{ill|Japanese Indigenous Theory|ja|日本原住民論|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Indigenous Theory|qq=}} * {{ill|Anti-Japaneseism|ja|反日亡国論|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anti-Japaneseism|qq=}} * {{ill|Anti-monarchism in Japan|ja|反天皇制|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anti-monarchism in Japan|qq=}} == References == * {{ill|Kyoto Shimbun|ja|京都新聞|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Shimbun|qq=}}, January 3, 1977 morning edition, January 13, 1977 morning edition * Saburō Katō, "Opinion Paper 'From the Pigs of the Earth to You'" {{ill|The Science of Thought Society|ja|思想の科学社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Science of Thought Society|qq=}}, 1992 == References == {{Reflist}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} On January 5, Katō sent a {{ill|claim of responsibility|ja|犯行声明|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/claim of responsibility|qq=}} to the media under the names "Yami no Tsuchigumo" and "Uke Hime no Kodomotachi." The statement mentioned the "three crimes of Sanjō Sanetomi (establishing the exploitative and oppressive Meiji government, invading other peoples, and suppressing anti-establishment movements)," sentencing him to death by bombing. It is said that the inclusion of a non-terrorist sounding name like "Uke Hime no Kodomotachi" was intended as an antithesis to the "paternalistic {{ill|Emperor system|ja|天皇制|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor system|qq=}}." == See Also == * {{ill|Japanese Indigenous Theory|ja|日本原住民論|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Indigenous Theory|qq=}} * {{ill|Anti-Japaneseism|ja|反日亡国論|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anti-Japaneseism|qq=}} * {{ill|Anti-monarchism in 1977 morning edition, January 13, 1977 morning edition * Saburō Katō, "Opinion Paper 'From the Pigs of the Earth to You'" {{ill|The Science of Thought revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=This page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:26, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Nashinoki Shrine]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:36, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Nashinoki Shrine]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {| class="infobox vcard" style="border-spacing:2px;" [[File:Nashinoki_Shrine_Haiden_3.jpg|thumb]] {{神社|名称=梨木神社|画像=[[画像:Nashinoki Shrine Haiden 3.jpg|250px]]
拝殿|所在地=[[京都府]][[京都市]][[上京区]][[寺町通]][[広小路通 (京都市)|広小路]]上ル染殿町680|位置={{Coord|35|1|30|N|135|46|2.5|E|region:JP-26:type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|祭神={{Ublist| [[三条実万|三條實萬]] | [[三条実美|三條實美]] }}|社格=旧[[別格官幣社]]|創建=[[1885th year ]]（[[明治]]18th year ）|別名=萩の宮|例祭=[[4月18日]]・[[10月10日]]|地図={{Location map|Japan Kyoto city|label=梨木神社|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|width=200|caption=}}}} Nashinoki Shrine (梨木神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] in {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigyō-ku|qq=}}. Although it was never a [[Beppyo Shrine]], it used to have the Rank of Bekkaku kanpeisha under the modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines. Nashinoki Shrine is a relatively new Shinto shrine in Kyoto. It was built in 1885.{{Cite web|title=Fushimi Inari Taisha {{!|qq=draft}} Location {{!}} Shajiraku – Quality traditional food and cultural experiences at Kyoto shrines and temples.|url=https://www.shajiraku.jp/en/plan/location_detail.php?id=2|access-date=2023-04-07|website=社寺楽 Shajiraku|language=en}}{{Cite web|date=2020-10-04|title=Nashinoki Shrine Kyoto, a Bush Clover Shrine {{!}} All About Kyoto|url=https://allaboutkyoto.com/en/nashinoki-shrine-kyoto/|access-date=2023-04-07|language=en-US|archive-date=2023-04-07|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230407205909/https://allaboutkyoto.com/en/nashinoki-shrine-kyoto/|url-status=dead}}[https://kyoto-kanko.net/en/kyoto-spot/18818-2/ Nashinoki Shrine | Kyoto tourist spot - Kyoto Tourism Net] It is located adjacent to the Kyoto Imperial Palace. The Nashinoki Shrine hosts an annual {{ill|Bush clover|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bush clover|qq=}} festival.{{Cite web|title=Hagi Matsuri|url=https://www.discoverkyoto.com/event-calendar/september/hagi-matsuri-nashinoki-shrine/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Discover Kyoto|language=en|qq=draft}} == Bombing == The '''Nashinoki Shrine Bombing Incident''' (なしのきじんじゃばくはじけん) was a [[1977]] ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōwa|qq=}} 52) guerrilla bombing that took place on [[January 1]] in {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|qq=}}'s {{ill|Kamigyo Ward|ja|上京区|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigyo Ward|qq=}}, at {{ill|Nashinoki Shrine|ja|梨木神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nashinoki Shrine|qq=}}. It was perpetrated by {{ill|Saburō Katō (New Left)|ja|加藤三郎 (新左翼)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saburō Katō (New Left)|qq=}} of the {{ill|Japanese New Left|ja|日本の新左翼|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese New Left|qq=}}. === History of the Damaged Structure === Nashinoki Shrine was established in [[1885]] based on a decree from {{ill|Prince Kuni Asahiko|ja|久邇宮朝彦親王|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kuni Asahiko|qq=}}, on the site of the former Sanjo residence, enshrining {{ill|Sanetomi Sanjo|ja|三条実万|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanetomi Sanjo|qq=}}, who contributed to the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Restoration|qq=}}. Later, his son, {{ill|Sanjō Sanetomi|ja|三条実美|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanjō Sanetomi|qq=}}, was also enshrined there. Katō criticized Sanjō Sanetomi as "the highest authority ({{ill|Daijō Daijin|ja|太政大臣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijō Daijin|qq=}}) of the Meiji government, which became the bearer of {{ill|Japanese imperialism|ja|日本帝国主義|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese imperialism|qq=}}," and proceeded to bomb Nashinoki Shrine, which enshrines him. === Overview of the Incident === On January 1, [[1977]] ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōwa|qq=}} 52) at around 8:45 PM, an [[:en:Improvised explosive device|Improvised explosive device]] installed by Katō exploded in the main hall of Nashinoki Shrine, causing a fire. Thanks to the immediate firefighting efforts by the {{ill|guji|ja|宮司|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/guji|qq=}} and {{ill|negi|ja|禰宜|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/negi|qq=}}, the damage was limited to part of the main hall being scorched. Although it was New Year's Day, there were no worshippers present as it was nighttime, and no injuries were reported. Subsequent investigation found fragments of a fire extinguisher, confirming the incident as a bombing with a fire extinguisher bomb. On January 5, Katō sent a {{ill|claim of responsibility|ja|犯行声明|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/claim of responsibility|qq=}} to the media under the names "Yami no Tsuchigumo" and "Uke Hime no Kodomotachi." The statement mentioned the "three crimes of Sanjō Sanetomi (establishing the exploitative and oppressive Meiji government, invading other peoples, and suppressing anti-establishment movements)," sentencing him to death by bombing. It is said that the inclusion of a non-terrorist sounding name like "Uke Hime no Kodomotachi" was intended as an antithesis to the "paternalistic {{ill|Emperor system|ja|天皇制|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor system|qq=}}." == See Also == * {{ill|Japanese Indigenous Theory|ja|日本原住民論|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Indigenous Theory|qq=}} * {{ill|Anti-Japaneseism|ja|反日亡国論|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anti-Japaneseism|qq=}} * {{ill|Anti-monarchism in Japan|ja|反天皇制|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anti-monarchism in Japan|qq=}} == References == * {{ill|Kyoto Shimbun|ja|京都新聞|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Shimbun|qq=}}, January 3, 1977 morning edition, January 13, 1977 morning edition * Saburō Katō, "Opinion Paper 'From the Pigs of the Earth to You'" {{ill|The Science of Thought Society|ja|思想の科学社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Science of Thought Society|qq=}}, 1992 == References == {{Reflist}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{translated page|ja|梨木神社|version=103984425|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 7]] [[Category:Terrorist incidents in Japan]] [[Category:Religious terrorism]] [[Category:Shinto history incidents]] [[Category:History of Kamigyo-ku]] [[Category:1977 crimes in Japan]] [[Category:January 1977 events in Asia]] [[Category:Shōwa period postwar Kyoto]] [[Category:Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] [[Category:Nashinoki Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category: Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Simple english wikipedia and everybodywiki]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[sn:Nashinoki Shrine]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] }} [[every:Nashinoki Shrine]] [[every:Nashinoki Shrine|Nashinoki Shrine]] [[simple:Nashinoki Shrine]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Nashinoki Shrine]] [[en:draft:Nashinoki Shrine]] [[sn:Nashinoki Shrine]] [[en:Nashinoki Shrine]] [[ja:梨木神社]] [[sn:ashinoki Shrine]]

[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {| class="infobox vcard" style="border-spacing:2px;" [[File:Nashinoki_Shrine_Haiden_3.jpg|thumb]] {{神社|名称=梨木神社|画像=[[画像:Nashinoki Shrine Haiden 3.jpg|250px]]
拝殿|所在地=[[京都府]][[京都市]][[上京区]][[寺町通]][[広小路通 (京都市)|広小路]]上ル染殿町680|位置={{Coord|35|1|30|N|135|46|2.5|E|region:JP-26:type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|祭神={{Ublist| [[三条実万|三條實萬]] | [[三条実美|三條實美]] }}|社格=旧[[別格官幣社]]|創建=[[1885th year ]]（[[明治]]18th year ）|別名=萩の宮|例祭=[[4月18日]]・[[10月10日]]|地図={{Location map|Japan Kyoto city|label=梨木神社|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|width=200|caption=}}}} Nashinoki Shrine (梨木神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] in {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigyō-ku|qq=}}. Although it was never a [[Beppyo Shrine]], it used to have the Rank of Bekkaku kanpeisha under the modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines. Nashinoki Shrine is a relatively new Shinto shrine in Kyoto. It was built in 1885.{{Cite web|title=Fushimi Inari Taisha {{!|qq=draft}} Location {{!}} Shajiraku – Quality traditional food and cultural experiences at Kyoto shrines and temples.|url=https://www.shajiraku.jp/en/plan/location_detail.php?id=2|access-date=2023-04-07|website=社寺楽 Shajiraku|language=en}}{{Cite web|date=2020-10-04|title=Nashinoki Shrine Kyoto, a Bush Clover Shrine {{!}} All About Kyoto|url=https://allaboutkyoto.com/en/nashinoki-shrine-kyoto/|access-date=2023-04-07|language=en-US|archive-date=2023-04-07|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230407205909/https://allaboutkyoto.com/en/nashinoki-shrine-kyoto/|url-status=dead}}[https://kyoto-kanko.net/en/kyoto-spot/18818-2/ Nashinoki Shrine | Kyoto tourist spot - Kyoto Tourism Net] It is located adjacent to the Kyoto Imperial Palace. The Nashinoki Shrine hosts an annual {{ill|Bush clover|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bush clover|qq=}} festival.{{Cite web|title=Hagi Matsuri|url=https://www.discoverkyoto.com/event-calendar/september/hagi-matsuri-nashinoki-shrine/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Discover Kyoto|language=en|qq=draft}} == Bombing == The '''Nashinoki Shrine Bombing Incident''' (なしのきじんじゃばくはじけん) was a [[1977]] ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōwa|qq=}} 52) guerrilla bombing that took place on [[January 1]] in {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|qq=}}'s {{ill|Kamigyo Ward|ja|上京区|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigyo Ward|qq=}}, at {{ill|Nashinoki Shrine|ja|梨木神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nashinoki Shrine|qq=}}. It was perpetrated by {{ill|Saburō Katō (New Left)|ja|加藤三郎 (新左翼)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saburō Katō (New Left)|qq=}} of the {{ill|Japanese New Left|ja|日本の新左翼|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese New Left|qq=}}. === History of the Damaged Structure === Nashinoki Shrine was established in [[1885]] based on a decree from {{ill|Prince Kuni Asahiko|ja|久邇宮朝彦親王|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kuni Asahiko|qq=}}, on the site of the former Sanjo residence, enshrining {{ill|Sanetomi Sanjo|ja|三条実万|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanetomi Sanjo|qq=}}, who contributed to the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Restoration|qq=}}. Later, his son, {{ill|Sanjō Sanetomi|ja|三条実美|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanjō Sanetomi|qq=}}, was also enshrined there. Katō criticized Sanjō Sanetomi as "the highest authority ({{ill|Daijō Daijin|ja|太政大臣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijō Daijin|qq=}}) of the Meiji government, which became the bearer of {{ill|Japanese imperialism|ja|日本帝国主義|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese imperialism|qq=}}," and proceeded to bomb Nashinoki Shrine, which enshrines him. === Overview of the Incident === On January 1, [[1977]] ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōwa|qq=}} 52) at around 8:45 PM, an [[:en:Improvised explosive device|Improvised explosive device]] installed by Katō exploded in the main hall of Nashinoki Shrine, causing a fire. Thanks to the immediate firefighting efforts by the {{ill|guji|ja|宮司|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/guji|qq=}} and {{ill|negi|ja|禰宜|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/negi|qq=}}, the damage was limited to part of the main hall being scorched. Although it was New Year's Day, there were no worshippers present as it was nighttime, and no injuries were reported. Subsequent investigation found fragments of a fire extinguisher, confirming the incident as a bombing with a fire extinguisher bomb. On January 5, Katō sent a {{ill|claim of responsibility|ja|犯行声明|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/claim of responsibility|qq=}} to the media under the names "Yami no Tsuchigumo" and "Uke Hime no Kodomotachi." The statement mentioned the "three crimes of Sanjō Sanetomi (establishing the exploitative and oppressive Meiji government, invading other peoples, and suppressing anti-establishment movements)," sentencing him to death by bombing. It is said that the inclusion of a non-terrorist sounding name like "Uke Hime no Kodomotachi" was intended as an antithesis to the "paternalistic {{ill|Emperor system|ja|天皇制|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor system|qq=}}." == See Also == * {{ill|Japanese Indigenous Theory|ja|日本原住民論|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Indigenous Theory|qq=}} * {{ill|Anti-Japaneseism|ja|反日亡国論|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anti-Japaneseism|qq=}} * {{ill|Anti-monarchism in Japan|ja|反天皇制|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anti-monarchism in Japan|qq=}} == References == * {{ill|Kyoto Shimbun|ja|京都新聞|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Shimbun|qq=}}, January 3, 1977 morning edition, January 13, 1977 morning edition * Saburō Katō, "Opinion Paper 'From the Pigs of the Earth to You'" {{ill|The Science of Thought Society|ja|思想の科学社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Science of Thought Society|qq=}}, 1992 == References == {{Reflist}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{translated page|ja|梨木神社|version=103984425|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 7]] [[Category:Terrorist incidents in Japan]] [[Category:Shinto history incidents]] [[Category:History of Kamigyo-ku]] [[Category:1977 crimes in Japan]] [[Category:January 1977 events in Asia]] [[Category:Shōwa period postwar Kyoto]] [[Category:Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category: Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Simple english wikipedia and everybodywiki]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[sn:Nashinoki Shrine]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] }} [[every:Nashinoki Shrine]] [[every:Nashinoki Shrine|Nashinoki Shrine]] [[simple:Nashinoki Shrine]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Nashinoki Shrine]] [[en:draft:Nashinoki Shrine]] [[sn:Nashinoki Shrine]] [[en:Nashinoki Shrine]] [[ja:梨木神社]] [[sn:ashinoki Shrine]]

On January 5, Katō sent a {{ill|claim of responsibility|ja|犯行声明|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/claim of responsibility|qq=}} to the media under the names "Yami no Tsuchigumo" and "Uke Hime no Kodomotachi." The statement mentioned the "three crimes of Sanjō Sanetomi (establishing the exploitative and oppressive Meiji government, invading other peoples, and suppressing anti-establishment movements)," sentencing him to death by bombing. It is said that the inclusion of a non-terrorist sounding name like "Uke Hime no Kodomotachi" was intended as an antithesis to the "paternalistic {{ill|Emperor system|ja|天皇制|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor system|qq=}}." == See Also == * {{ill|Japanese Indigenous Theory|ja|日本原住民論|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Indigenous Theory|qq=}} * {{ill|Anti-Japaneseism|ja|反日亡国論|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anti-Japaneseism|qq=}} * {{ill|Anti-monarchism in Japan|ja|反天皇制|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anti-monarchism in Japan|qq=}} == References == * {{ill|Kyoto Shimbun|ja|京都新聞|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Shimbun|qq=}}, January 3, 1977 morning edition, January 13, 1977 morning edition * Saburō Katō, "Opinion Paper 'From the Pigs of the Earth to You'" {{ill|The Science of Thought Society|ja|思想の科学社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Science of Thought Society|qq=}}, 1992 == References == {{Reflist}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{translated page|ja|梨木神社|version=103984425|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 7]] [[Category:Terrorist incidents in Japan]] [[Category:History of Kamigyo-ku]] [[Category:1977 crimes in Japan]] [[Category:January 1977 events in Asia]] [[Category:Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category: Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Simple english wikipedia and everybodywiki]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[sn:Nashinoki Shrine]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] }} [[every:Nashinoki Shrine]] [[every:Nashinoki Shrine|Nashinoki Shrine]] [[simple:Nashinoki Shrine]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Nashinoki Shrine]] [[en:draft:Nashinoki Shrine]] [[sn:Nashinoki Shrine]] [[en:Nashinoki Shrine]] [[ja:梨木神社]] [[sn:ashinoki Shrine]]

revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=This page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:26, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Nashinoki Shrine]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:36, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Nashinoki Shrine]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {| class="infobox vcard" style="border-spacing:2px;" [[File:Nashinoki_Shrine_Haiden_3.jpg|thumb]] {{神社|名称=梨木神社|画像=[[画像:Nashinoki Shrine Haiden 3.jpg|250px]]
拝殿|所在地=[[京都府]][[京都市]][[上京区]][[寺町通]][[広小路通 (京都市)|広小路]]上ル染殿町680|位置={{Coord|35|1|30|N|135|46|2.5|E|region:JP-26:type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|祭神={{Ublist| [[三条実万|三條實萬]] | [[三条実美|三條實美]] }}|社格=旧[[別格官幣社]]|創建=[[1885th year ]]（[[明治]]18th year ）|別名=萩の宮|例祭=[[4月18日]]・[[10月10日]]|地図={{Location map|Japan Kyoto city|label=梨木神社|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|width=200|caption=}}}} Nashinoki Shrine (梨木神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] in {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigyō-ku|qq=}}. Although it was never a [[Beppyo Shrine]], it used to have the Rank of Bekkaku kanpeisha under the modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines. Nashinoki Shrine is a relatively new Shinto shrine in Kyoto. It was built in 1885.{{Cite web|title=Fushimi Inari Taisha {{!|qq=draft}} Location {{!}} Shajiraku – Quality traditional food and cultural experiences at Kyoto shrines and temples.|url=https://www.shajiraku.jp/en/plan/location_detail.php?id=2|access-date=2023-04-07|website=社寺楽 Shajiraku|language=en}}{{Cite web|date=2020-10-04|title=Nashinoki Shrine Kyoto, a Bush Clover Shrine {{!}} All About Kyoto|url=https://allaboutkyoto.com/en/nashinoki-shrine-kyoto/|access-date=2023-04-07|language=en-US|archive-date=2023-04-07|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230407205909/https://allaboutkyoto.com/en/nashinoki-shrine-kyoto/|url-status=dead}}[https://kyoto-kanko.net/en/kyoto-spot/18818-2/ Nashinoki Shrine | Kyoto tourist spot - Kyoto Tourism Net] It is located adjacent to the Kyoto Imperial Palace. The Nashinoki Shrine hosts an annual {{ill|Bush clover|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bush clover|qq=}} festival.{{Cite web|title=Hagi Matsuri|url=https://www.discoverkyoto.com/event-calendar/september/hagi-matsuri-nashinoki-shrine/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Discover Kyoto|language=en|qq=draft}} == Bombing == The '''Nashinoki Shrine Bombing Incident''' (なしのきじんじゃばくはじけん) was a [[1977]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 52) guerrilla bombing that took place on [[January 1]] in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]]'s [[:en:Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|Kamigyo Ward]], at {{ill|Nashinoki Shrine|ja|梨木神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nashinoki Shrine|qq=}}. It was perpetrated by {{ill|Saburō Katō (New Left)|ja|加藤三郎 (新左翼)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saburō Katō (New Left)|qq=}} of the [[:en:New Left in Japan|Japanese New Left]]. === History of the Damaged Structure === Nashinoki Shrine was established in [[1885]] based on a decree from [[:en:Prince Kuni Asahiko|Prince Kuni Asahiko]], on the site of the former Sanjo residence, enshrining {{ill|Sanetomi Sanjo|ja|三条実万|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanetomi Sanjo|qq=}}, who contributed to the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]]. Later, his son, [[:en:Sanjō Sanetomi|Sanjō Sanetomi]], was also enshrined there. Katō criticized Sanjō Sanetomi as "the highest authority ([[:en:Daijō-daijin|Daijō Daijin]]) of the Meiji government, which became the bearer of [[:en:Japanese colonial empire|Japanese imperialism]]," and proceeded to bomb Nashinoki Shrine, which enshrines him. === Overview of the Incident === On January 1, [[1977]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 52) at around 8:45 PM, an [[:en:Improvised explosive device|Improvised explosive device]] installed by Katō exploded in the main hall of Nashinoki Shrine, causing a fire. Thanks to the immediate firefighting efforts by the {{ill|guji|ja|宮司|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/guji|qq=}} and {{ill|negi|ja|禰宜|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/negi|qq=}}, the damage was limited to part of the main hall being scorched. Although it was New Year's Day, there were no worshippers present as it was nighttime, and no injuries were reported. Subsequent investigation found fragments of a fire extinguisher, confirming the incident as a bombing with a fire extinguisher bomb. On January 5, Katō sent a {{ill|claim of responsibility|ja|犯行声明|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/claim of responsibility|qq=}} to the media under the names "Yami no Tsuchigumo" and "Uke Hime no Kodomotachi." The statement mentioned the "three crimes of Sanjō Sanetomi (establishing the exploitative and oppressive Meiji government, invading other peoples, and suppressing anti-establishment movements)," sentencing him to death by bombing. It is said that the inclusion of a non-terrorist sounding name like "Uke Hime no Kodomotachi" was intended as an antithesis to the "paternalistic [[:en:Emperor system|Emperor system]]." == See Also == * {{ill|Japanese Indigenous Theory|ja|日本原住民論|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Indigenous Theory|qq=}} * [[:en:Anti-Japaneseism|Anti-Japaneseism]] * {{ill|Anti-monarchism in Japan|ja|反天皇制|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anti-monarchism in Japan|qq=}} == References == * [[:en:Kyoto Shimbun|Kyoto Shimbun]], January 3, 1977 morning edition, January 13, 1977 morning edition * Saburō Katō, "Opinion Paper 'From the Pigs of the Earth to You'" {{ill|The Science of Thought Society|ja|思想の科学社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Science of Thought Society|qq=}}, 1992 == References == {{Reflist}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{translated page|ja|梨木神社|version=103984425|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 7]] [[Category:Terrorist incidents in Japan]] [[Category:History of Kamigyo-ku]] [[Category:1977 crimes in Japan]] [[Category:January 1977 events in Asia]] [[Category:Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category: Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Simple english wikipedia and everybodywiki]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[sn:Nashinoki Shrine]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] }} [[every:Nashinoki Shrine]] [[every:Nashinoki Shrine|Nashinoki Shrine]] [[simple:Nashinoki Shrine]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Nashinoki Shrine]] [[en:draft:Nashinoki Shrine]] [[sn:Nashinoki Shrine]] [[en:Nashinoki Shrine]] [[ja:梨木神社]] [[sn:ashinoki Shrine]]

It was perpetrated by {{ill|Saburō Katō (New Left)|ja|加藤三郎 (新左翼)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saburō Katō (New Left)|qq=}} of the [[:en:New Left in Japan|Japanese New Left]]. === History of the Damaged Structure === Nashinoki Shrine was established in [[1885]] based on a decree from [[:en:Prince Kuni Asahiko|Prince Kuni Asahiko]], on the site of the former Sanjo residence, enshrining {{ill|Sanetomi Sanjo|ja|三条実万|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanetomi Sanjo|qq=}}, who contributed to the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]]. Later, his son, [[:en:Sanjō Sanetomi|Sanjō Sanetomi]], was also enshrined there. Katō criticized Sanjō Sanetomi as "the highest authority ([[:en:Daijō-daijin|Daijō Daijin]]) of the Meiji government, which became the bearer of [[:en:Japanese colonial empire|Japanese imperialism]]," and proceeded to bomb Nashinoki Shrine, which enshrines him. === Overview of the Incident === On January 1, [[1977]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 52) at around 8:45 PM, an [[:en:Improvised explosive device|Improvised explosive device]] installed by Katō exploded in the main hall of Nashinoki Shrine, causing a fire. Thanks to the immediate firefighting efforts by the {{ill|guji|ja|宮司|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/guji|qq=}} and {{ill|negi|ja|禰宜|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/negi|qq=}}, the damage was limited to part of the main hall being scorched. Although it was New Year's Day, there were no worshippers present as it was nighttime, and no injuries were reported. Subsequent investigation found fragments of a fire extinguisher, confirming the incident as a bombing with a fire extinguisher bomb. On January 5, Katō sent a {{ill|claim of responsibility|ja|犯行声明|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/claim of responsibility|qq=}} to the media under the names "Yami no Tsuchigumo" and "Uke Hime no Kodomotachi." The statement mentioned the "three crimes of Sanjō Sanetomi (establishing the exploitative and oppressive Meiji government, invading other peoples, and suppressing anti-establishment movements)," sentencing him to death by bombing. It is said that the inclusion of a non-terrorist sounding name like "Uke Hime no Kodomotachi" was intended as an antithesis to the "paternalistic [[:en:Emperor system|Emperor system]]." == See Also == * {{ill|Japanese Indigenous Theory|ja|日本原住民論|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Indigenous Theory|qq=}} * [[:en:Anti-Japaneseism|Anti-Japaneseism]] * {{ill|Anti-monarchism in Japan|ja|反天皇制|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anti-monarchism in Japan|qq=}} == References == * [[:en:Kyoto Shimbun|Kyoto Shimbun]], January 3, 1977 morning edition, January 13, 1977 morning edition * Saburō Katō, "Opinion Paper 'From the Pigs of the Earth to You'" {{ill|The Science of Thought Society|ja|思想の科学社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Science of Thought Society|qq=}}, 1992 == References == {{Reflist}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{translated page|ja|梨木神社|version=103984425|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 7]] [[Category:Terrorist incidents in Japan]] [[Category:History of Kamigyo-ku]] [[Category:1977 crimes in Japan]] [[Category:January 1977 events in Asia]] [[Category:Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category: Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Simple english wikipedia and everybodywiki]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[sn:Nashinoki Shrine]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] }} [[every:Nashinoki Shrine]] [[every:Nashinoki Shrine|Nashinoki Shrine]] [[simple:Nashinoki Shrine]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Nashinoki Shrine]] [[en:draft:Nashinoki Shrine]] [[sn:Nashinoki Shrine]] [[en:Nashinoki Shrine]] [[ja:梨木神社]] [[sn:ashinoki Shrine]] [[Category:三条実美]] [[Category:Sanjō family]]

[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {| class="infobox vcard" style="border-spacing:2px;" [[File:Nashinoki_Shrine_Haiden_3.jpg|thumb]] {{神社|名称=梨木神社|画像=[[画像:Nashinoki Shrine Haiden 3.jpg|250px]]
拝殿|所在地=[[京都府]][[京都市]][[上京区]][[寺町通]][[広小路通 (京都市)|広小路]]上ル染殿町680|位置={{Coord|35|1|30|N|135|46|2.5|E|region:JP-26:type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|祭神={{Ublist| [[三条実万|三條實萬]] | [[三条実美|三條實美]] }}|社格=旧[[別格官幣社]]|創建=[[1885th year ]]（[[明治]]18th year ）|別名=萩の宮|例祭=[[4月18日]]・[[10月10日]]|地図={{Location map|Japan Kyoto city|label=梨木神社|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|width=200|caption=}}}} Nashinoki Shrine (梨木神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] in {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigyō-ku|qq=}}. Although it was never a [[Beppyo Shrine]], it used to have the Rank of Bekkaku kanpeisha under the modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines. Nashinoki Shrine is a relatively new Shinto shrine in Kyoto. It was built in 1885.{{Cite web|title=Fushimi Inari Taisha {{!|qq=draft}} Location {{!}} Shajiraku – Quality traditional food and cultural experiences at Kyoto shrines and temples.|url=https://www.shajiraku.jp/en/plan/location_detail.php?id=2|access-date=2023-04-07|website=社寺楽 Shajiraku|language=en}}{{Cite web|date=2020-10-04|title=Nashinoki Shrine Kyoto, a Bush Clover Shrine {{!}} All About Kyoto|url=https://allaboutkyoto.com/en/nashinoki-shrine-kyoto/|access-date=2023-04-07|language=en-US|archive-date=2023-04-07|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230407205909/https://allaboutkyoto.com/en/nashinoki-shrine-kyoto/|url-status=dead}}[https://kyoto-kanko.net/en/kyoto-spot/18818-2/ Nashinoki Shrine | Kyoto tourist spot - Kyoto Tourism Net] It is located adjacent to the Kyoto Imperial Palace. The Nashinoki Shrine hosts an annual {{ill|Bush clover|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bush clover|qq=}} festival.{{Cite web|title=Hagi Matsuri|url=https://www.discoverkyoto.com/event-calendar/september/hagi-matsuri-nashinoki-shrine/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Discover Kyoto|language=en|qq=draft}} == Bombing == The '''Nashinoki Shrine Bombing Incident''' (なしのきじんじゃばくはじけん) was a [[1977]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 52) guerrilla bombing that took place on [[January 1]] in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]]'s [[:en:Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|Kamigyo Ward]], at {{ill|Nashinoki Shrine|ja|梨木神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nashinoki Shrine|qq=}}. It was perpetrated by {{ill|Saburō Katō (New Left)|ja|加藤三郎 (新左翼)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saburō Katō (New Left)|qq=}} of the [[:en:New Left in Japan|Japanese New Left]]. === History of the Damaged Structure === Nashinoki Shrine was established in [[1885]] based on a decree from [[:en:Prince Kuni Asahiko|Prince Kuni Asahiko]], on the site of the former Sanjo residence, enshrining {{ill|Sanetomi Sanjo|ja|三条実万|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanetomi Sanjo|qq=}}, who contributed to the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]]. Later, his son, [[:en:Sanjō Sanetomi|Sanjō Sanetomi]], was also enshrined there. Katō criticized Sanjō Sanetomi as "the highest authority ([[:en:Daijō-daijin|Daijō Daijin]]) of the Meiji government, which became the bearer of [[:en:Japanese colonial empire|Japanese imperialism]]," and proceeded to bomb Nashinoki Shrine, which enshrines him. === Overview of the Incident === On January 1, [[1977]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 52) at around 8:45 PM, an [[:en:Improvised explosive device|Improvised explosive device]] installed by Katō exploded in the main hall of Nashinoki Shrine, causing a fire. Thanks to the immediate firefighting efforts by the {{ill|guji|ja|宮司|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/guji|qq=}} and {{ill|negi|ja|禰宜|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/negi|qq=}}, the damage was limited to part of the main hall being scorched. Although it was New Year's Day, there were no worshippers present as it was nighttime, and no injuries were reported. Subsequent investigation found fragments of a fire extinguisher, confirming the incident as a bombing with a fire extinguisher bomb. On January 5, Katō sent a {{ill|claim of responsibility|ja|犯行声明|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/claim of responsibility|qq=}} to the media under the names "Yami no Tsuchigumo" and "Uke Hime no Kodomotachi." The statement mentioned the "three crimes of Sanjō Sanetomi (establishing the exploitative and oppressive Meiji government, invading other peoples, and suppressing anti-establishment movements)," sentencing him to death by bombing. It is said that the inclusion of a non-terrorist sounding name like "Uke Hime no Kodomotachi" was intended as an antithesis to the "paternalistic [[:en:Emperor system|Emperor system]]." == See Also == * {{ill|Japanese Indigenous Theory|ja|日本原住民論|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Indigenous Theory|qq=}} * [[:en:Anti-Japaneseism|Anti-Japaneseism]] * {{ill|Anti-monarchism in Japan|ja|反天皇制|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anti-monarchism in Japan|qq=}} == References == * [[:en:Kyoto Shimbun|Kyoto Shimbun]], January 3, 1977 morning edition, January 13, 1977 morning edition * Saburō Katō, "Opinion Paper 'From the Pigs of the Earth to You'" {{ill|The Science of Thought Society|ja|思想の科学社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Science of Thought Society|qq=}}, 1992 == References == {{Reflist}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{translated page|ja|梨木神社|version=103984425|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 7]] [[Category:Terrorist incidents in Japan]] [[Category:History of Kamigyo-ku]] [[Category:1977 crimes in Japan]] [[Category:January 1977 events in Asia]] [[Category:Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category: Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Simple english wikipedia and everybodywiki]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[sn:Nashinoki Shrine]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] }} [[every:Nashinoki Shrine]] [[every:Nashinoki Shrine|Nashinoki Shrine]] [[simple:Nashinoki Shrine]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Nashinoki Shrine]] [[en:draft:Nashinoki Shrine]] [[sn:Nashinoki Shrine]] [[en:Nashinoki Shrine]] [[ja:梨木神社]] [[sn:ashinoki Shrine]] [[Category:三条実美]] [[Category:Sanjō family]] [[Category:上京区の建築物]]

The Nashinoki Shrine hosts an annual {{ill|Bush clover|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bush clover|qq=}} festival.{{Cite web|title=Hagi Matsuri|url=https://www.discoverkyoto.com/event-calendar/september/hagi-matsuri-nashinoki-shrine/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Discover Kyoto|language=en|qq=draft}} == Bombing == The '''Nashinoki Shrine Bombing Incident''' (なしのきじんじゃばくはじけん) was a [[1977]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 52) guerrilla bombing that took place on [[January 1]] in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]]'s [[:en:Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|Kamigyo Ward]], at {{ill|Nashinoki Shrine|ja|梨木神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nashinoki Shrine|qq=}}. It was perpetrated by {{ill|Saburō Katō (New Left)|ja|加藤三郎 (新左翼)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saburō Katō (New Left)|qq=}} of the [[:en:New Left in Japan|Japanese New Left]]. === History of the Damaged Structure === Nashinoki Shrine was established in [[1885]] based on a decree from [[:en:Prince Kuni Asahiko|Prince Kuni Asahiko]], on the site of the former Sanjo residence, enshrining {{ill|Sanetomi Sanjo|ja|三条実万|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanetomi Sanjo|qq=}}, who contributed to the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]]. Later, his son, [[:en:Sanjō Sanetomi|Sanjō Sanetomi]], was also enshrined there. Katō criticized Sanjō Sanetomi as "the highest authority ([[:en:Daijō-daijin|Daijō Daijin]]) of the Meiji government, which became the bearer of [[:en:Japanese colonial empire|Japanese imperialism]]," and proceeded to bomb Nashinoki Shrine, which enshrines him. === Overview of the Incident === On January 1, [[1977]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 52) at around 8:45 PM, an [[:en:Improvised explosive device|Improvised explosive device]] installed by Katō exploded in the main hall of Nashinoki Shrine, causing a fire. Thanks to the immediate firefighting efforts by the {{ill|guji|ja|宮司|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/guji|qq=}} and {{ill|negi|ja|禰宜|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/negi|qq=}}, the damage was limited to part of the main hall being scorched. Although it was New Year's Day, there were no worshippers present as it was nighttime, and no injuries were reported. Subsequent investigation found fragments of a fire extinguisher, confirming the incident as a bombing with a fire extinguisher bomb. On January 5, Katō sent a {{ill|claim of responsibility|ja|犯行声明|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/claim of responsibility|qq=}} to the media under the names "Yami no Tsuchigumo" and "Uke Hime no Kodomotachi." The statement mentioned the "three crimes of Sanjō Sanetomi (establishing the exploitative and oppressive Meiji government, invading other peoples, and suppressing anti-establishment movements)," sentencing him to death by bombing. It is said that the inclusion of a non-terrorist sounding name like "Uke Hime no Kodomotachi" was intended as an antithesis to the "paternalistic [[:en:Emperor system|Emperor system]]." == See Also == * {{ill|Japanese Indigenous Theory|ja|日本原住民論|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Indigenous Theory|qq=}} * [[:en:Anti-Japaneseism|Anti-Japaneseism]] * {{ill|Anti-monarchism in Japan|ja|反天皇制|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anti-monarchism in Japan|qq=}} == References == * [[:en:Kyoto Shimbun|Kyoto Shimbun]], January 3, 1977 morning edition, January 13, 1977 morning edition * Saburō Katō, "Opinion Paper 'From the Pigs of the Earth to You'" {{ill|The Science of Thought Society|ja|思想の科学社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Science of Thought Society|qq=}}, 1992 == References == {{Reflist}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{translated page|ja|梨木神社|version=103984425|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 7]] [[Category:Terrorist incidents in Japan]] [[Category:History of Kamigyo-ku]] [[Category:1977 crimes in Japan]] [[Category:January 1977 events in Asia]] [[Category:Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category: Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Simple english wikipedia and everybodywiki]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[sn:Nashinoki Shrine]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] }} [[every:Nashinoki Shrine]] [[every:Nashinoki Shrine|Nashinoki Shrine]] [[simple:Nashinoki Shrine]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Nashinoki Shrine]] [[en:draft:Nashinoki Shrine]] [[sn:Nashinoki Shrine]] [[en:Nashinoki Shrine]] [[ja:梨木神社]] [[sn:ashinoki Shrine]] [[Category:三条実美]] [[Category:Sanjō family]] [[Category:上京区の建築物]] [[Category:上京区の歴史]] [[Category:京都府の景観重要建造物]]

拝殿|所在地=[[京都府]][[京都市]][[上京区]][[寺町通]][[広小路通 (京都市)|広小路]]上ル染殿町680|位置={{Coord|35|1|30|N|135|46|2.5|E|region:JP-26:type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|祭神={{Ublist| [[三条実万|三條實萬]] | [[三条実美|三條實美]] }}|社格=旧[[別格官幣社]]|創建=[[1885th year ]]（[[明治]]18th year ）|別名=萩の宮|例祭=[[4月18日]]・[[10月10日]]|地図={{Location map|Japan Kyoto city|label=梨木神社|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|width=200|caption=}}}} Nashinoki Shrine (梨木神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] in {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigyō-ku|qq=}}. Although it was never a [[Beppyo Shrine]], it used to have the Rank of Bekkaku kanpeisha under the modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines. Nashinoki Shrine is a relatively new Shinto shrine in Kyoto. It was built in 1885.{{Cite web|title=Fushimi Inari Taisha {{!|qq=draft}} Location {{!}} Shajiraku – Quality traditional food and cultural experiences at Kyoto shrines and temples.|url=https://www.shajiraku.jp/en/plan/location_detail.php?id=2|access-date=2023-04-07|website=社寺楽 Shajiraku|language=en}}{{Cite web|date=2020-10-04|title=Nashinoki Shrine Kyoto, a Bush Clover Shrine {{!}} All About Kyoto|url=https://allaboutkyoto.com/en/nashinoki-shrine-kyoto/|access-date=2023-04-07|language=en-US|archive-date=2023-04-07|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230407205909/https://allaboutkyoto.com/en/nashinoki-shrine-kyoto/|url-status=dead}}[https://kyoto-kanko.net/en/kyoto-spot/18818-2/ Nashinoki Shrine | Kyoto tourist spot - Kyoto Tourism Net] It is located adjacent to the Kyoto Imperial Palace. The Nashinoki Shrine hosts an annual {{ill|Bush clover|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bush clover|qq=}} festival.{{Cite web|title=Hagi Matsuri|url=https://www.discoverkyoto.com/event-calendar/september/hagi-matsuri-nashinoki-shrine/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Discover Kyoto|language=en|qq=draft}} == Bombing == The '''Nashinoki Shrine Bombing Incident''' (なしのきじんじゃばくはじけん) was a [[1977]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 52) guerrilla bombing that took place on [[January 1]] in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]]'s [[:en:Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|Kamigyo Ward]], at {{ill|Nashinoki Shrine|ja|梨木神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nashinoki Shrine|qq=}}. It was perpetrated by {{ill|Saburō Katō (New Left)|ja|加藤三郎 (新左翼)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saburō Katō (New Left)|qq=}} of the [[:en:New Left in Japan|Japanese New Left]]. === History of the Damaged Structure === Nashinoki Shrine was established in [[1885]] based on a decree from [[:en:Prince Kuni Asahiko|Prince Kuni Asahiko]], on the site of the former Sanjo residence, enshrining {{ill|Sanetomi Sanjo|ja|三条実万|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanetomi Sanjo|qq=}}, who contributed to the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]]. Later, his son, [[:en:Sanjō Sanetomi|Sanjō Sanetomi]], was also enshrined there. Katō criticized Sanjō Sanetomi as "the highest authority ([[:en:Daijō-daijin|Daijō Daijin]]) of the Meiji government, which became the bearer of [[:en:Japanese colonial empire|Japanese imperialism]]," and proceeded to bomb Nashinoki Shrine, which enshrines him. === Overview of the Incident === On January 1, [[1977]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 52) at around 8:45 PM, an [[:en:Improvised explosive device|Improvised explosive device]] installed by Katō exploded in the main hall of Nashinoki Shrine, causing a fire. Thanks to the immediate firefighting efforts by the {{ill|guji|ja|宮司|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/guji|qq=}} and {{ill|negi|ja|禰宜|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/negi|qq=}}, the damage was limited to part of the main hall being scorched. Although it was New Year's Day, there were no worshippers present as it was nighttime, and no injuries were reported. Subsequent investigation found fragments of a fire extinguisher, confirming the incident as a bombing with a fire extinguisher bomb. On January 5, Katō sent a {{ill|claim of responsibility|ja|犯行声明|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/claim of responsibility|qq=}} to the media under the names "Yami no Tsuchigumo" and "Uke Hime no Kodomotachi." The statement mentioned the "three crimes of Sanjō Sanetomi (establishing the exploitative and oppressive Meiji government, invading other peoples, and suppressing anti-establishment movements)," sentencing him to death by bombing. It is said that the inclusion of a non-terrorist sounding name like "Uke Hime no Kodomotachi" was intended as an antithesis to the "paternalistic [[:en:Emperor system|Emperor system]]." == See Also == * {{ill|Japanese Indigenous Theory|ja|日本原住民論|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Indigenous Theory|qq=}} * [[:en:Anti-Japaneseism|Anti-Japaneseism]] * {{ill|Anti-monarchism in Japan|ja|反天皇制|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anti-monarchism in Japan|qq=}} == References == * [[:en:Kyoto Shimbun|Kyoto Shimbun]], January 3, 1977 morning edition, January 13, 1977 morning edition * Saburō Katō, "Opinion Paper 'From the Pigs of the Earth to You'" {{ill|The Science of Thought Society|ja|思想の科学社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Science of Thought Society|qq=}}, 1992 == References == {{Reflist}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{translated page|ja|梨木神社|version=103984425|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 7]] [[Category:Terrorist incidents in Japan]] [[Category:History of Kamigyo-ku]] [[Category:1977 crimes in Japan]] [[Category:January 1977 events in Asia]] [[Category:Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category: Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Simple english wikipedia and everybodywiki]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[sn:Nashinoki Shrine]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] }} [[every:Nashinoki Shrine]] [[every:Nashinoki Shrine|Nashinoki Shrine]] [[simple:Nashinoki Shrine]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Nashinoki Shrine]] [[en:draft:Nashinoki Shrine]] [[sn:Nashinoki Shrine]] [[en:Nashinoki Shrine]] [[ja:梨木神社]] [[sn:ashinoki Shrine]] [[Category:三条実美]] [[Category:Sanjō family]] [[Category:Buildings in Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:京都府の景観重要建造物]] [[Category:Independent shrines]]

{{afc comment|1=This page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:26, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Nashinoki Shrine]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:36, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Nashinoki Shrine]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {| class="infobox vcard" style="border-spacing:2px;" [[File:Nashinoki_Shrine_Haiden_3.jpg|thumb]] {{神社|名称=梨木神社|画像=[[画像:Nashinoki Shrine Haiden 3.jpg|250px]]
拝殿|所在地=[[京都府]][[京都市]][[上京区]][[寺町通]][[広小路通 (京都市)|広小路]]上ル染殿町680|位置={{Coord|35|1|30|N|135|46|2.5|E|region:JP-26:type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|祭神={{Ublist| [[三条実万|三條實萬]] | [[三条実美|三條實美]] }}|社格=旧[[別格官幣社]]|創建=[[1885th year ]]（[[明治]]18th year ）|別名=萩の宮|例祭=[[4月18日]]・[[10月10日]]|地図={{Location map|Japan Kyoto city|label=梨木神社|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|width=200|caption=}}}} Nashinoki Shrine (梨木神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] in {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigyō-ku|qq=}}. Although it was never a [[Beppyo Shrine]], it used to have the Rank of Bekkaku kanpeisha under the modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines. Nashinoki Shrine is a relatively new Shinto shrine in Kyoto. It was built in 1885.{{Cite web|title=Fushimi Inari Taisha {{!|qq=draft}} Location {{!}} Shajiraku – Quality traditional food and cultural experiences at Kyoto shrines and temples.|url=https://www.shajiraku.jp/en/plan/location_detail.php?id=2|access-date=2023-04-07|website=社寺楽 Shajiraku|language=en}}{{Cite web|date=2020-10-04|title=Nashinoki Shrine Kyoto, a Bush Clover Shrine {{!}} All About Kyoto|url=https://allaboutkyoto.com/en/nashinoki-shrine-kyoto/|access-date=2023-04-07|language=en-US|archive-date=2023-04-07|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230407205909/https://allaboutkyoto.com/en/nashinoki-shrine-kyoto/|url-status=dead}}[https://kyoto-kanko.net/en/kyoto-spot/18818-2/ Nashinoki Shrine | Kyoto tourist spot - Kyoto Tourism Net] It is located adjacent to the Kyoto Imperial Palace. The Nashinoki Shrine hosts an annual {{ill|Bush clover|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bush clover|qq=}} festival.{{Cite web|title=Hagi Matsuri|url=https://www.discoverkyoto.com/event-calendar/september/hagi-matsuri-nashinoki-shrine/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Discover Kyoto|language=en|qq=draft}} == Bombing == The '''Nashinoki Shrine Bombing Incident''' (なしのきじんじゃばくはじけん) was a [[1977]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 52) guerrilla bombing that took place on [[January 1]] in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]]'s [[:en:Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|Kamigyo Ward]], at {{ill|Nashinoki Shrine|ja|梨木神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nashinoki Shrine|qq=}}. It was perpetrated by {{ill|Saburō Katō (New Left)|ja|加藤三郎 (新左翼)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saburō Katō (New Left)|qq=}} of the [[:en:New Left in Japan|Japanese New Left]]. === History of the Damaged Structure === Nashinoki Shrine was established in [[1885]] based on a decree from [[:en:Prince Kuni Asahiko|Prince Kuni Asahiko]], on the site of the former Sanjo residence, enshrining {{ill|Sanetomi Sanjo|ja|三条実万|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanetomi Sanjo|qq=}}, who contributed to the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]]. Later, his son, [[:en:Sanjō Sanetomi|Sanjō Sanetomi]], was also enshrined there. Katō criticized Sanjō Sanetomi as "the highest authority ([[:en:Daijō-daijin|Daijō Daijin]]) of the Meiji government, which became the bearer of [[:en:Japanese colonial empire|Japanese imperialism]]," and proceeded to bomb Nashinoki Shrine, which enshrines him. === Overview of the Incident === On January 1, [[1977]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 52) at around 8:45 PM, an [[:en:Improvised explosive device|Improvised explosive device]] installed by Katō exploded in the main hall of Nashinoki Shrine, causing a fire. Thanks to the immediate firefighting efforts by the {{ill|guji|ja|宮司|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/guji|qq=}} and {{ill|negi|ja|禰宜|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/negi|qq=}}, the damage was limited to part of the main hall being scorched. Although it was New Year's Day, there were no worshippers present as it was nighttime, and no injuries were reported. Subsequent investigation found fragments of a fire extinguisher, confirming the incident as a bombing with a fire extinguisher bomb. On January 5, Katō sent a {{ill|claim of responsibility|ja|犯行声明|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/claim of responsibility|qq=}} to the media under the names "Yami no Tsuchigumo" and "Uke Hime no Kodomotachi." The statement mentioned the "three crimes of Sanjō Sanetomi (establishing the exploitative and oppressive Meiji government, invading other peoples, and suppressing anti-establishment movements)," sentencing him to death by bombing. It is said that the inclusion of a non-terrorist sounding name like "Uke Hime no Kodomotachi" was intended as an antithesis to the "paternalistic [[:en:Emperor system|Emperor system]]." == See Also == * {{ill|Japanese Indigenous Theory|ja|日本原住民論|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Indigenous Theory|qq=}} * [[:en:Anti-Japaneseism|Anti-Japaneseism]] * {{ill|Anti-monarchism in Japan|ja|反天皇制|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anti-monarchism in Japan|qq=}} == References == * [[:en:Kyoto Shimbun|Kyoto Shimbun]], January 3, 1977 morning edition, January 13, 1977 morning edition * Saburō Katō, "Opinion Paper 'From the Pigs of the Earth to You'" {{ill|The Science of Thought Society|ja|思想の科学社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Science of Thought Society|qq=}}, 1992 == References == {{Reflist}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{translated page|ja|梨木神社|version=103984425|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 7]] [[Category:Terrorist incidents in Japan]] [[Category:History of Kamigyo-ku]] [[Category:1977 crimes in Japan]] [[Category:January 1977 events in Asia]] [[Category:Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category: Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Simple english wikipedia and everybodywiki]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[sn:Nashinoki Shrine]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] }} [[every:Nashinoki Shrine]] [[every:Nashinoki Shrine|Nashinoki Shrine]] [[simple:Nashinoki Shrine]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Nashinoki Shrine]] [[sn:Nashinoki Shrine]] [[ja:梨木神社]] [[sn:ashinoki Shrine]] [[Category:Sanetomi Sanjō]] [[Category:Sanjō family]] [[Category:Buildings in Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:京都府の景観重要建造物]] [[Category:Independent shrines]] {{Commons category|Nashinoki-jinja}}

revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=This page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:26, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Nashinoki Shrine]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:36, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Nashinoki Shrine]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {| class="infobox vcard" style="border-spacing:2px;" [[File:Nashinoki_Shrine_Haiden_3.jpg|thumb]] {{神社|名称=梨木神社|画像=[[画像:Nashinoki Shrine Haiden 3.jpg|250px]]
拝殿|所在地=[[京都府]][[京都市]][[上京区]][[寺町通]][[広小路通 (京都市)|広小路]]上ル染殿町680|位置={{Coord|35|1|30|N|135|46|2.5|E|region:JP-26:type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|祭神={{Ublist| [[三条実万|三條實萬]] | [[三条実美|三條實美]] }}|社格=旧[[別格官幣社]]|創建=[[1885th year ]]（[[明治]]18th year ）|別名=萩の宮|例祭=[[4月18日]]・[[10月10日]]|地図={{Location map|Japan Kyoto city|label=梨木神社|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|width=200|caption=}}}} Nashinoki Shrine (梨木神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] in {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigyō-ku|qq=}}. Although it was never a [[Beppyo Shrine]], it used to have the Rank of Bekkaku kanpeisha under the modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines. Nashinoki Shrine is a relatively new Shinto shrine in Kyoto. It was built in 1885.{{Cite web|title=Fushimi Inari Taisha {{!|qq=draft}} Location {{!}} Shajiraku – Quality traditional food and cultural experiences at Kyoto shrines and temples.|url=https://www.shajiraku.jp/en/plan/location_detail.php?id=2|access-date=2023-04-07|website=社寺楽 Shajiraku|language=en}}{{Cite web|date=2020-10-04|title=Nashinoki Shrine Kyoto, a Bush Clover Shrine {{!}} All About Kyoto|url=https://allaboutkyoto.com/en/nashinoki-shrine-kyoto/|access-date=2023-04-07|language=en-US|archive-date=2023-04-07|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230407205909/https://allaboutkyoto.com/en/nashinoki-shrine-kyoto/|url-status=dead}}[https://kyoto-kanko.net/en/kyoto-spot/18818-2/ Nashinoki Shrine | Kyoto tourist spot - Kyoto Tourism Net] It is located adjacent to the Kyoto Imperial Palace. The Nashinoki Shrine hosts an annual {{ill|Bush clover|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bush clover|qq=}} festival.{{Cite web|title=Hagi Matsuri|url=https://www.discoverkyoto.com/event-calendar/september/hagi-matsuri-nashinoki-shrine/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Discover Kyoto|language=en|qq=draft}} == Bombing == The '''Nashinoki Shrine Bombing Incident''' (なしのきじんじゃばくはじけん) was a [[1977]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 52) guerrilla bombing that took place on [[January 1]] in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]]'s [[:en:Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|Kamigyo Ward]], at {{ill|Nashinoki Shrine|ja|梨木神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nashinoki Shrine|qq=}}. It was perpetrated by {{ill|Saburō Katō (New Left)|ja|加藤三郎 (新左翼)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saburō Katō (New Left)|qq=}} of the [[:en:New Left in Japan|Japanese New Left]]. === History of the Damaged Structure === Nashinoki Shrine was established in [[:en:1885|1885]] based on a decree from [[:en:Prince Kuni Asahiko|Prince Kuni Asahiko]], on the site of the former Sanjo residence, enshrining {{ill|Sanetomi Sanjo|ja|三条実万|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanetomi Sanjo|qq=}}, who contributed to the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]]. Later, his son, [[:en:Sanjō Sanetomi|Sanjō Sanetomi]], was also enshrined there. Katō criticized Sanjō Sanetomi as "the highest authority ([[:en:Daijō-daijin|Daijō Daijin]]) of the Meiji government, which became the bearer of [[:en:Japanese colonial empire|Japanese imperialism]]," and proceeded to bomb Nashinoki Shrine, which enshrines him. === Overview of the Incident === On January 1, [[1977]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 52) at around 8:45 PM, an [[:en:Improvised explosive device|Improvised explosive device]] installed by Katō exploded in the main hall of Nashinoki Shrine, causing a fire. Thanks to the immediate firefighting efforts by the {{ill|guji|ja|宮司|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/guji|qq=}} and {{ill|negi|ja|禰宜|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/negi|qq=}}, the damage was limited to part of the main hall being scorched. Although it was New Year's Day, there were no worshippers present as it was nighttime, and no injuries were reported. Subsequent investigation found fragments of a fire extinguisher, confirming the incident as a bombing with a fire extinguisher bomb. On January 5, Katō sent a {{ill|claim of responsibility|ja|犯行声明|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/claim of responsibility|qq=}} to the media under the names "Yami no Tsuchigumo" and "Uke Hime no Kodomotachi." The statement mentioned the "three crimes of Sanjō Sanetomi (establishing the exploitative and oppressive Meiji government, invading other peoples, and suppressing anti-establishment movements)," sentencing him to death by bombing. It is said that the inclusion of a non-terrorist sounding name like "Uke Hime no Kodomotachi" was intended as an antithesis to the "paternalistic [[:en:Emperor system|Emperor system]]." == See Also == * {{ill|Japanese Indigenous Theory|ja|日本原住民論|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Indigenous Theory|qq=}} * [[:en:Anti-Japaneseism|Anti-Japaneseism]] * {{ill|Anti-monarchism in Japan|ja|反天皇制|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anti-monarchism in Japan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天皇制廃止論}} == References == * [[:en:Kyoto Shimbun|Kyoto Shimbun]], January 3, 1977 morning edition, January 13, 1977 morning edition * Saburō Katō, "Opinion Paper 'From the Pigs of the Earth to You'" {{ill|The Science of Thought Society|ja|思想の科学社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Science of Thought Society|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 思想の科学}}, 1992 == References == {{Reflist}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{translated page|ja|梨木神社|version=103984425|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 7]] [[Category:Terrorist incidents in Japan]] [[Category:History of Kamigyo-ku]] [[Category:1977 crimes in Japan]] [[Category:January 1977 events in Asia]] [[Category:Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category: Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Simple english wikipedia and everybodywiki]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[sn:Nashinoki Shrine]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] }} [[every:Nashinoki Shrine]] [[every:Nashinoki Shrine|Nashinoki Shrine]] [[simple:Nashinoki Shrine]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Nashinoki Shrine]] [[sn:Nashinoki Shrine]] [[ja:梨木神社]] [[sn:ashinoki Shrine]] [[Category:Sanetomi Sanjō]] [[Category:Sanjō family]] [[Category:Buildings in Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:京都府の景観重要建造物]] [[Category:Independent shrines]] {{Commons category|Nashinoki-jinja}}

It was perpetrated by {{ill|Saburō Katō (New Left)|ja|加藤三郎 (新左翼)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saburō Katō (New Left)|qq=}} of the [[:en:New Left in Japan|Japanese New Left]]. === History of the Damaged Structure === Nashinoki Shrine was established in [[:en:1885|1885]] based on a decree from [[:en:Prince Kuni Asahiko|Prince Kuni Asahiko]], on the site of the former Sanjo residence, enshrining {{ill|Sanetomi Sanjo|ja|三条実万|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanetomi Sanjo|qq=}}, who contributed to the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]]. Later, his son, [[:en:Sanjō Sanetomi|Sanjō Sanetomi]], was also enshrined there. Katō criticized Sanjō Sanetomi as "the highest authority ([[:en:Daijō-daijin|Daijō Daijin]]) of the Meiji government, which became the bearer of [[:en:Japanese colonial empire|Japanese imperialism]]," and proceeded to bomb Nashinoki Shrine, which enshrines him. === Overview of the Incident === On January 1, [[:en:1977|1977]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 52) at around 8:45 PM, an [[:en:Improvised explosive device|Improvised explosive device]] installed by Katō exploded in the main hall of Nashinoki Shrine, causing a fire. Thanks to the immediate firefighting efforts by the {{ill|guji|ja|宮司|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/guji|qq=}} and {{ill|negi|ja|禰宜|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/negi|qq=}}, the damage was limited to part of the main hall being scorched. Although it was New Year's Day, there were no worshippers present as it was nighttime, and no injuries were reported. Subsequent investigation found fragments of a fire extinguisher, confirming the incident as a bombing with a fire extinguisher bomb. On January 5, Katō sent a {{ill|claim of responsibility|ja|犯行声明|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/claim of responsibility|qq=}} to the media under the names "Yami no Tsuchigumo" and "Uke Hime no Kodomotachi." The statement mentioned the "three crimes of Sanjō Sanetomi (establishing the exploitative and oppressive Meiji government, invading other peoples, and suppressing anti-establishment movements)," sentencing him to death by bombing. It is said that the inclusion of a non-terrorist sounding name like "Uke Hime no Kodomotachi" was intended as an antithesis to the "paternalistic [[:en:Emperor system|Emperor system]]." == See Also == * {{ill|Japanese Indigenous Theory|ja|日本原住民論|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Indigenous Theory|qq=}} * [[:en:Anti-Japaneseism|Anti-Japaneseism]] * {{ill|Anti-monarchism in Japan|ja|反天皇制|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anti-monarchism in Japan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天皇制廃止論}} == References == * [[:en:Kyoto Shimbun|Kyoto Shimbun]], January 3, 1977 morning edition, January 13, 1977 morning edition * Saburō Katō, "Opinion Paper 'From the Pigs of the Earth to You'" {{ill|The Science of Thought Society|ja|思想の科学社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Science of Thought Society|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 思想の科学}}, 1992 == References == {{Reflist}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{translated page|ja|梨木神社|version=103984425|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 7]] [[Category:Terrorist incidents in Japan]] [[Category:1977 crimes in Japan]] [[Category:January 1977 events in Asia]] [[Category:Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category: Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Simple english wikipedia and everybodywiki]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[sn:Nashinoki Shrine]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] }} [[every:Nashinoki Shrine]] [[every:Nashinoki Shrine|Nashinoki Shrine]] [[simple:Nashinoki Shrine]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Nashinoki Shrine]] [[sn:Nashinoki Shrine]] [[ja:梨木神社]] [[sn:ashinoki Shrine]] [[Category:Sanetomi Sanjō]] [[Category:Sanjō family]] [[Category:Buildings in Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:京都府の景観重要建造物]] [[Category:Independent shrines]] {{Commons category|Nashinoki-jinja}}

拝殿|所在地=[[京都府]][[京都市]][[上京区]][[寺町通]][[広小路通 (京都市)|広小路]]上ル染殿町680|位置={{Coord|35|1|30|N|135|46|2.5|E|region:JP-26:type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|祭神={{Ublist| [[三条実万|三條實萬]] | [[三条実美|三條實美]] }}|社格=旧[[別格官幣社]]|創建=[[1885th year ]]（[[明治]]18th year ）|別名=萩の宮|例祭=[[4月18日]]・[[10月10日]]|地図={{Location map|Japan Kyoto city|label=梨木神社|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|width=200|caption=}}}} Nashinoki Shrine (梨木神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] in {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigyō-ku|qq=}}. Although it was never a [[Beppyo Shrine]], it used to have the Rank of Bekkaku kanpeisha under the modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines. Nashinoki Shrine is a relatively new Shinto shrine in Kyoto. It was built in 1885.{{Cite web|title=Fushimi Inari Taisha {{!|qq=draft}} Location {{!}} Shajiraku – Quality traditional food and cultural experiences at Kyoto shrines and temples.|url=https://www.shajiraku.jp/en/plan/location_detail.php?id=2|access-date=2023-04-07|website=社寺楽 Shajiraku|language=en}}{{Cite web|date=2020-10-04|title=Nashinoki Shrine Kyoto, a Bush Clover Shrine {{!}} All About Kyoto|url=https://allaboutkyoto.com/en/nashinoki-shrine-kyoto/|access-date=2023-04-07|language=en-US|archive-date=2023-04-07|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230407205909/https://allaboutkyoto.com/en/nashinoki-shrine-kyoto/|url-status=dead}}[https://kyoto-kanko.net/en/kyoto-spot/18818-2/ Nashinoki Shrine | Kyoto tourist spot - Kyoto Tourism Net] It is located adjacent to the Kyoto Imperial Palace. The Nashinoki Shrine hosts an annual {{ill|Bush clover|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bush clover|qq=}} festival.{{Cite web|title=Hagi Matsuri|url=https://www.discoverkyoto.com/event-calendar/september/hagi-matsuri-nashinoki-shrine/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Discover Kyoto|language=en|qq=draft}} == Bombing == The '''Nashinoki Shrine Bombing Incident''' (なしのきじんじゃばくはじけん) was a [[:en:1977|1977]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 52) guerrilla bombing that took place on [[January 1]] in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]]'s [[:en:Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|Kamigyo Ward]], at {{ill|Nashinoki Shrine|ja|梨木神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nashinoki Shrine|qq=}}. It was perpetrated by {{ill|Saburō Katō (New Left)|ja|加藤三郎 (新左翼)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saburō Katō (New Left)|qq=}} of the [[:en:New Left in Japan|Japanese New Left]]. === History of the Damaged Structure === Nashinoki Shrine was established in [[:en:1885|1885]] based on a decree from [[:en:Prince Kuni Asahiko|Prince Kuni Asahiko]], on the site of the former Sanjo residence, enshrining {{ill|Sanetomi Sanjo|ja|三条実万|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanetomi Sanjo|qq=}}, who contributed to the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]]. Later, his son, [[:en:Sanjō Sanetomi|Sanjō Sanetomi]], was also enshrined there. Katō criticized Sanjō Sanetomi as "the highest authority ([[:en:Daijō-daijin|Daijō Daijin]]) of the Meiji government, which became the bearer of [[:en:Japanese colonial empire|Japanese imperialism]]," and proceeded to bomb Nashinoki Shrine, which enshrines him. === Overview of the Incident === On January 1, [[:en:1977|1977]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 52) at around 8:45 PM, an [[:en:Improvised explosive device|Improvised explosive device]] installed by Katō exploded in the main hall of Nashinoki Shrine, causing a fire.
